title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Examples behavior contributes positive environment community include: Demonstrating empathy kindness toward people respectful differing opinions, viewpoints, experiences Giving gracefully accepting constructive feedback Accepting responsibility apologizing affected mistakes, learning experience Focusing best just us individuals, overall community Examples unacceptable behavior include: use sexualized language imagery, sexual attention advances kind Trolling, insulting derogatory comments, personal political attacks Public private harassment Publishing others’ private information, physical email address, without explicit permission conduct reasonably considered inappropriate professional setting

title: Contributing to sntutils
text: want make bigger change, ’s good idea first file issue make sure someone team agrees ’s needed. ’ve found bug, please file issue illustrates bug minimal reprex (also help write unit test, needed). See guide create great issue advice.

title: Contributing to sntutils
text: Fork package clone onto computer. haven’t done , recommend using usethis::create_from_github("ahadi-analytics/sntutils", fork = TRUE). Install development dependencies devtools::install_dev_deps(), make sure package passes R CMD check running devtools::check(). R CMD check doesn’t pass cleanly, ’s good idea ask help continuing. Create Git branch pull request (PR). recommend using usethis::pr_init("brief-description--change"). Make changes, commit git, create PR running usethis::pr_push(), following prompts browser. title PR briefly describe change. body PR contain Fixes #issue-number. user-facing changes, add bullet top NEWS.md (.e. just first header). Follow style described https://style.tidyverse.org/news.html.

title: Contributing to sntutils
text: New code follow tidyverse style guide. can use styler package apply styles, please don’t restyle code nothing PR. use roxygen2, Markdown syntax, documentation. use testthat unit tests. Contributions test cases included easier accept.

title: Contributing to sntutils
text: Please note sntutils project released Contributor Code Conduct. contributing project agree abide terms.

title: Climate
text: CHIRPS workhorse rainfall product African SNT analyses. package supports four standard CHIRPS monthly subsets. download_chirps() resumable - never re-downloads file already present right size. result directory .tifs named e.g. chirps-v2.0.2022.01.tif, ready raster batch processors .
code: library(sntutils)  # what's available chirps_options() #> # A tibble: 4 × 4 #>   dataset             frequency label                                 subdir #>   <chr>               <chr>     <chr>                                 <chr> #> 1 global_monthly      monthly   Global (Monthly)                      global_monthly/tifs #> 2 africa_monthly      monthly   Africa (Monthly)                      africa_monthly/tifs #> 3 camer-carib_monthly monthly   Caribbean & Central America (Monthly) camer-carib_monthly/tifs #> 4 EAC_monthly         monthly   East African Community (Monthly)      EAC_monthly/tifs  # what years/months exist for a chosen subset check_chirps_available(dataset_code = "africa_monthly") #> ✔ africa_monthly: Data available from Jan 1981 to Mar 2025.  # download Africa monthly rainfall for Jan–Mar 2022 download_chirps(   dataset = "africa_monthly",   start   = "2022-01",   end     = "2022-03",   out_dir = "01_data/1.5_environment/1.5a_climate/raw/chirps" )

title: Climate
text: ERA5 (ECMWF reanalysis) gives temperature, dewpoint, wind, surface pressure, many variables hourly daily resolution. download path goes Copernicus Climate Data Store needs (free) API key. get_era5_metadata() print_era5_metadata() extract embedded metadata ERA5 NetCDF without loading data memory - useful auditing ’s cache. read_era5() opens NetCDF files sensible defaults (CRS, dimension renaming, unit conversion). migrate_era5_filenames() renames older ERA5 caches current naming convention.
code: # set CDS API key once - see https://cds.climate.copernicus.eu Sys.setenv(CDS_USER = "<uid>", CDS_KEY = "<key>")  # list known datasets and their variable catalogues era5_options()  # what years / variables exist for a chosen dataset check_era5_available(   dataset_code = "reanalysis-era5-land-monthly-means",   years        = 2018:2024,   months       = 1:12 )  # download daily 2m temperature and dewpoint over Sierra Leone download_era5(   dataset   = "reanalysis-era5-land",   variables = c("2m_temperature", "2m_dewpoint_temperature"),   years     = 2020:2023,   months    = 1:12,   bbox      = c(-13.5, 6.9, -10.2, 10.0),       # xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax   daily_stat   = "mean",   daily_hfreq  = "1_hourly",   daily_tz     = "Africa/Freetown",   out_dir      = "01_data/1.5_environment/1.5a_climate/raw/era5",   aggregated_dir    = "01_data/1.5_environment/1.5a_climate/processed/era5",   aggregated_format = "tif" )

title: Climate
text: MODIS provides EVI, NDVI, surface temperature, fire land-cover layers. download_modis() function wraps NASA Earthdata search + download clips tile bounding box shapefile. output tidy set GeoTIFFs date-suffixed filenames, ready raster processors.
code: # what products and bands are supported modis_options(search = "MOD11")  # download monthly land surface temperature for Sierra Leone download_modis(   shapefile = sle_adm2_clean,   start     = "2022-01-01",   end       = "2022-12-31",   product   = "MOD11A2",   band      = "LST_Day_1km",   out_dir   = "01_data/1.5_environment/1.5a_climate/raw/modis",   username  = Sys.getenv("EARTHDATA_USER"),   password  = Sys.getenv("EARTHDATA_PASS") )

title: Climate
text: agro-climate variables (rainfall, irradiance, humidity, wind speed, several temperature flavours) point locations, download_process_nasapower() fastest path: issues one query per polygon centroid returns tidy long tibble already joined admin codes.
code: power_df <- download_process_nasapower(   adm_sf       = sle_adm2_clean,   admin_cols   = c("adm1_name", "adm2_name"),   start_date   = "2020-01-01",   end_date     = "2023-12-31",   power_vars   = c("PRECTOTCORR", "T2M", "T2M_MIN", "T2M_MAX",                    "RH2M", "WS2M", "ALLSKY_SFC_SW_DWN"),   power_community = "ag",   max_retries  = 3,   dict_language = "en" )  dplyr::glimpse(power_df) #> Rows: ~ 1.7M #> Columns: 11 #> $ adm1_name <chr> "Eastern", "Eastern", "Eastern", ... #> $ adm2_name <chr> "Kailahun", "Kailahun", ... #> $ date      <date> 2020-01-01, 2020-01-02, ... #> $ PRECTOTCORR <dbl> 0.0, 0.0, 0.32, ... #> $ T2M       <dbl> 25.4, 25.9, 26.1, ...

title: Climate
text: download functions deposit .tifs disk. turn tidy tibble keyed admin date, use batch raster processors - process_raster_collection() plain zonal stats, process_weighted_raster_collection() population-weighted extraction, process_rasters_by_year() boundaries change vintages, process_ihme_u5m_raster() IHME -5 mortality rasters. Rasters article walks detail.

title: Climate
text: climate extracted admin, facetted_map_gradient() facetted_map_bins() small-area visualisation pair. take sf object (boundaries + indicator rows) colour vector, join extraction output boundaries first pick colours get_palette().
code: # 1. extract CHIRPS to adm2 (see the Rasters article) rain_adm2 <- process_raster_collection(   directory    = "01_data/1.5_environment/1.5a_climate/raw/chirps",   shapefile    = sle_adm2_clean,   id_cols      = c("adm0_name", "adm1_name", "adm2_name"),   aggregations = "mean" )  # 2. summarise to annual and join to the sf so each polygon carries its value rain_annual_sf <- rain_adm2 |>   dplyr::group_by(adm0_name, adm1_name, adm2_name, year) |>   dplyr::summarise(rain_mm = mean(mean, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>   dplyr::left_join(sle_adm2_clean, y = _, by = c("adm0_name", "adm1_name", "adm2_name")) |>   sf::st_as_sf()

title: Climate
text: continuous indicator like rainfall, facetted_map_gradient() draws small-multiples choropleth straight joined sf. Pick colours via get_palette() - list_palettes() lists built-palettes ("ylgnbu", "blues", "viridis", "spectral", …).
code: list_palettes() #> [1] "default"  "byor"     "gyor"     "ylord"    "ylgnbu"   "blues" #> [7] "greens"   "reds"     "oranges"  "purples"  "bupu"     "orrd" #> ... etc  facetted_map_gradient(   data      = rain_annual_sf,   fill_col  = "rain_mm",   facet_col = "year",   colors    = get_palette("ylgnbu"),   limits    = c(0, 300),   ncol      = 3,   title     = "Mean monthly rainfall (mm) - Sierra Leone",   fill_label = "Rainfall (mm)",   caption   = "Source: CHIRPS v2.0, monthly Africa subset." )

title: Climate
text: indicator naturally read categories - e.g. low / moderate / high rainfall - bin values auto_bin() (manually) pair facetted_map_bins() named-colour vector get_palette() length bin levels. helpers can write compressed PNG straight disk passing output_file = "...". also accept optional adm1_shp overlay higher-admin outlines top adm2 fills - useful SNT reports district fills sit inside region borders.
code: rain_banded_sf <- rain_annual_sf |>   dplyr::mutate(     rain_band = auto_bin(rain_mm, n_breaks = 5, style = "quantile")   )  # one colour per band, in the bin order bin_colors <- stats::setNames(   get_palette("ylgnbu", n = nlevels(rain_banded_sf$rain_band)),   levels(rain_banded_sf$rain_band) )  facetted_map_bins(   data        = rain_banded_sf,   fill_col    = "rain_band",   facet_col   = "year",   fill_colors = bin_colors,   title       = "Mean monthly rainfall, quantile bands",   fill_label  = "mm / month" )

what: A climate pipeline, end to end
title: Climate
text: ’s two major archives reduced one tibble keyed admin date - canonical climate input stratification modelling steps downstream.
code: # 1. download CHIRPS monthly rainfall download_chirps(   dataset = "africa_monthly",   start   = "2020-01",   end     = "2024-12",   out_dir = "01_data/1.5_environment/1.5a_climate/raw/chirps" )  # 2. download ERA5 daily temperature for the same period download_era5(   dataset      = "reanalysis-era5-land",   variables    = c("2m_temperature"),   years        = 2020:2024,   months       = 1:12,   bbox         = c(-13.5, 6.9, -10.2, 10.0),   daily_stat   = "mean",   out_dir      = "01_data/1.5_environment/1.5a_climate/raw/era5" )  # 3. extract both to adm2, population-weighted by the 2020 WorldPop surface rain_adm2 <- process_weighted_raster_collection(   directory     = "01_data/1.5_environment/1.5a_climate/raw/chirps",   shapefile     = sle_adm2_clean,   weight_raster = "01_data/1.1_foundational/1.1c_population/1.1cii_worldpop_rasters/sle_ppp_2020_1km.tif",   id_cols       = c("adm1_name", "adm2_name"),   aggregations  = "mean" )  temp_adm2 <- process_weighted_raster_collection(   directory     = "01_data/1.5_environment/1.5a_climate/raw/era5",   shapefile     = sle_adm2_clean,   weight_raster = "01_data/1.1_foundational/1.1c_population/1.1cii_worldpop_rasters/sle_ppp_2020_1km.tif",   id_cols       = c("adm1_name", "adm2_name"),   aggregations  = "mean" )  # 4. combine - ready for stratification and modelling climate_adm2 <- rain_adm2 |>   dplyr::left_join(     temp_adm2,     by = c("adm1_name", "adm2_name", "year", "month"),     suffix = c("_rain", "_temp")   )

title: Read & clean
text: read() write() thin wrappers rio plus spatial ecosystem. pick importer exporter based file extension, can stop thinking whether something readr::read_csv() haven::read_dta() sf::st_read(). read() defers underlying reader arguments, can pass sheet, sep, skip, col_types etc. exactly directly.
code: library(sntutils)  # tabular formats df_csv    <- read("path/to/file.csv", sep = ",") df_excel  <- read("path/to/file.xlsx", sheet = 1) df_stata  <- read("path/to/file.dta") df_spss   <- read("path/to/file.sav") df_rds    <- read("path/to/file.rds")  # spatial formats - returns an sf object sf_geojson  <- read("path/to/file.geojson") sf_shape    <- read("path/to/file.shp")  # write in the format implied by the extension write(df, "path/to/export.csv") write(df, "path/to/export.xlsx") write(df, "path/to/export.dta") write(sf_geojson, "path/to/export.geojson")  # write multiple tibbles as sheets in one Excel file write(   list(facilities = hf_df, cases = case_df, pop = pop_df),   "path/to/multi_sheet.xlsx" )

title: Read & clean
text: data want track across runs - DHIS2 exports, processed intermediate files, anything land 01_data/ - use write_snt_data() read_snt_data() instead. write atomically, hash payload, drop sidecar JSON next file schema, row count, write timestamp hash. read_snt_data() looks file logical name inside directory, rest pipeline doesn’t care extension. sidecar makes easy detect upstream changes: input hash hasn’t moved, downstream steps can cached.
code: # write - atomic, hashed, sidecar metadata next to the file write_snt_data(   obj  = cleaned_dhis2,   path = "01_data/1.2_epidemiology/1.2a_routine_surveillance/processed",   data_name = "sl_dhis2_clean",   formats = c("rds", "csv") )  # read - finds the most recent file with that logical name sl_dhis2 <- read_snt_data(   path = "01_data/1.2_epidemiology/1.2a_routine_surveillance/processed",   data_name = "sl_dhis2_clean" )

title: Read & clean
text: DHIS2 exports often arrive names like "OPD Total -  Suspected Malaria (U5)". standardize_names() collapses something joinable: function lowercases uppercases, strips accents punctuation, converts Roman numerals digits, sorts internal tokens "CHP Kpalime III" "III CHP Kpalime" collapse string. Set to_upper = FALSE lowercase_with_underscores (handy column names). clean_filenames() equivalent job file names - useful unzipping country submissions filenames carry spaces accents.
code: standardize_names(c("Chp Kpalime-III", "Kpalimé CHP 3")) #> [1] "CHP KPALIME 3" "CHP KPALIME 3"  # applied across a tibble sl_dhis2 <- sl_dhis2 |>   dplyr::mutate(     dplyr::across(c(adm1, adm2, adm3, hf), standardize_names)   )

title: Read & clean
text: autoparse_dates() walks list candidate formats detects one applies per cell. handles ISO-8601 timezones, ambiguous dd/mm vs mm/dd cases, Excel serial dates. see formats knows , inspect available_date_formats: Pass additional formats = vector extend list country whose data carries unusual encoding (MIS exports use %d %m %Y literal space, example).

title: Read & clean
text: DHIS2 / Excel pipelines almost always leave numerics character factor-like columns freeform strings. two helpers recover right types without losing IDs look numeric aren’t.

title: Read & clean
text: Walks column converts narrowest sensible type. recognises ID-like columns name suffix (*_id, *_uid, *_code, etc.) keeps character leading zeros survive. Control factor-vs-character cut-via max_levels (default 50): columns unique values stay character, facility_name column 1700 distinct values doesn’t get coerced 1700-level factor.
code: df <- tibble::tibble(   hf_uid    = c("001", "002", "003"),       # preserved as character   sex       = c("M", "F", "F"),             # detected as factor   age       = c("1", "2", "3"),             # parsed to integer   weight_kg = c("12.4", "15.0", "9.8"),     # parsed to double   dob       = c("2001-05-10", "2003-02-14", "2005-09-30"),  # to Date   notes     = c("ok", "review", NA_character_)  # stays character )  auto_parse_types(df) #> # A tibble: 3 × 6 #>   hf_uid sex     age weight_kg dob        notes #>   <chr>  <fct> <int>     <dbl> <date>     <chr> #> 1 001    M         1      12.4 2001-05-10 ok #> 2 002    F         2      15   2003-02-14 review #> 3 003    F         3       9.8 2005-09-30 <NA>

title: Read & clean
text: detect_factors() lower-level helper powers factor decision auto_parse_types(). Call directly want see factorised committing conversion: reason column makes easy audit type decisions country submission looks suspicious.
code: tibble::tibble(   adm  = c("A", "B", "A", "C", "B"),       # 3 levels - good factor candidate   code = c("01", "02", "03", "04", "05"),  # 5 unique - looks like an ID   note = letters[1:5]                       # 5 unique - just text ) |>   detect_factors(max_levels = 4) #> # A tibble: 3 × 4 #>   variable n_unique would_factor reason #>   <chr>       <int> <lgl>        <chr> #> 1 adm             3 TRUE         under max_levels #> 2 code            5 FALSE        looks like an ID column #> 3 note            5 FALSE        over max_levels

title: Read & clean
text: big one. prep_geonames() cleans hierarchical admin names combining string-distance matching interactive picker durable cache decisions. supports six admin levels, accepts lookup table, can also run non-interactively batch jobs. lookup_df given, prep_geonames() falls back geonames shipped package. Decisions persisted disk, second run data instant. demo interactive flow README GitHub.
code: # input data with messy spellings dhis2_dummy <- data.frame(   country  = c("ANGOLA", "UGA", "ZAMBIA", "KEN"),   province = c("CABONDA", "TESO", "LUSAKA", "NAIROBY"),   district = c("BALIZE", "BOKEDEA", "RAFUNSA", "KIBRA") )  # the reference table to match against my_lookup <- data.frame(   country  = c("Angola", "Uganda", "Zambia", "Kenya"),   province = c("Cabinda", "Teso",   "Lusaka", "Nairobi"),   district = c("Belize",  "Bukedea", "Rufunsa", "Kibera") )  cleaned <- prep_geonames(   target_df  = dhis2_dummy,   lookup_df  = my_lookup,   level0     = "country",   level1     = "province",   level2     = "district",   interactive = TRUE )

what: Dictionaries: producing one, and using the curated SNT schema
title: Read & clean
text: Two flavours dictionary live sntutils. come every country project, ’re worth knowing well.

previous_headings: Dictionaries: producing one, and using the curated SNT schema
title: Read & clean
text: package ships canonical schema SNT variable names rest sntutils expects (conf, pres, test, allout, maltreat_u5, …). snt_data_dict() returns curated dictionary tibble, bilingual labels category tag variable. source truth variable naming across country teams. check_snt_var() validates one variable names schema, fuzzy matching names close---exact: Use sanity check start every analysis, joining DHIS2 anything else.
code: snt_data_dict() |>   dplyr::select(variable, category, label_en, label_fr) |>   dplyr::filter(category == "case_management") |>   utils::head() #> # A tibble: 6 × 4 #>   variable    category         label_en                       label_fr #>   <chr>       <chr>            <chr>                          <chr> #> 1 allout      case_management  All-cause outpatient visits    Consultations externes ... #> 2 susp        case_management  Suspected malaria cases        Cas suspects de paludisme #> 3 test        case_management  Malaria tests performed        Tests de paludisme ... #> 4 conf        case_management  Confirmed malaria cases        Cas confirmés #> 5 pres        case_management  Presumed malaria cases         Cas présumés #> 6 maltreat    case_management  Malaria cases treated          Cas traités check_snt_var("maltreat_u5") #> ✔ maltreat_u5 - Malaria cases treated, under 5  check_snt_var("conf_uner5")     # typo #> ✖ Unknown SNT variable: conf_uner5 #> ℹ Did you mean: conf_u5?

previous_headings: Dictionaries: producing one, and using the curated SNT schema
what: Generate a dictionary for your own dataset
title: Read & clean
text: dataset clean, build_dictionary() produces per-variable summary tibble - name, type, number unique values, value range top levels, (’re known) English label SNT schema. artefact hand country teams documentation processed dataset. output drops cleanly multi-sheet Excel via write(), can ship data_dictionary.xlsx alongside every processed dataset without writing hand. clear_snt_cache() resets -memory cache editing underlying labels CSV - call ’ve updated schema mid-session.
code: dd <- build_dictionary(sl_dhis2)  dd |>   dplyr::select(variable, type, n_unique, label_en) |>   utils::head() #> # A tibble: 6 × 4 #>   variable type        n_unique label_en #>   <chr>    <chr>          <int> <chr> #> 1 adm0     factor             1 Country (adm0) #> 2 adm1     factor             5 Region (adm1) #> 3 adm2     factor            17 District (adm2) #> 4 adm3     factor           191 Chiefdom (adm3) #> 5 hf       character       1702 Health facility name #> 6 conf     numeric            - Confirmed malaria cases

what: A clean pipeline, end to end
title: Read & clean
text: Putting together - cleaning step Get started example, explicit intermediate writes can audit stage: , downstream articles take clean object turn reporting rates, consistency checks, maps, climate-adjusted indicators.
code: raw <- read("01_data/1.2_epidemiology/1.2a_routine_surveillance/raw/sl_dhis2.csv")  clean <- raw |>   standardize_names() |>             # tidy column names   autoparse_dates(date_cols = "date") |>   # normalise dates   auto_parse_types() |>              # infer types   dplyr::mutate(     hf_uid = vdigest(paste0(adm1, adm2, hf), algo = "xxhash32")   )  write_snt_data(   obj       = clean,   path      = "01_data/1.2_epidemiology/1.2a_routine_surveillance/processed",   data_name = "sl_dhis2_clean",   formats   = c("rds", "csv") )

title: Data quality
text: malaria care cascade fixed direction: outpatients ≥ suspected ≥ tested ≥ confirmed ≥ treated, admissions ≥ malaria admissions ≥ malaria deaths. consistency_check() flags facility-months violate renders scatter input vs output violating points highlighted. Common cascade checks include: outpatients ≥ suspected malaria Malaria tests ≥ confirmed cases Confirmed cases ≥ cases treated admissions ≥ malaria admissions Malaria admissions ≥ malaria deaths
code: # tests (inputs) vs confirmed cases (outputs) consistency_check(   sl_dhis2,   inputs  = c("test"),   outputs = c("conf") )  # save the plot consistency_check(   sl_dhis2,   inputs    = c("test"),   outputs   = c("conf"),   save_plot = TRUE,   plot_path = "plots/consistency_check_plots" )  # translated labels (French) consistency_check(   sl_dhis2,   inputs          = c("test"),   outputs         = c("conf"),   target_language = "fr" )  # multiple cascades at once consistency_check(   sl_dhis2,   inputs          = c("test", "conf", "alladm"),   outputs         = c("conf", "maltreat", "maladm"),   target_language = "fr" )

title: Data quality
text: consistency_map() renders choropleth cascade-violation rates admin unit - useful spotting whether cascade breaking particular districts vs. system-wide:
code: consistency_map(   data       = sl_dhis2,   shapefile  = sle_adm2_clean,   input_var  = "test",   output_var = "conf",   adm_var    = "adm2",   x_var      = "year",   language   = "en" )

title: Data quality
text: detect_outliers() flags unusual values numeric column using three complementary methods, detection done within groups admin unit × facility × year seasonal contextual variation isn’t mistaken noise: Mean ± 3 SD - classic, parametric, sensitive extremes. Median ± 15 × MAD - robust, workhorse surveillance data. Tukey’s fences - quartile-based, tunable via iqr_multiplier. output one row per record. Join back record_id filter correct needed.
code: outlier_results <- detect_outliers(   data       = sl_dhis2,   column     = "conf",   yearmon    = "year_mon",   record_id  = "record_id",   adm1       = "adm1",   adm2       = "adm2",   iqr_multiplier = 2 )  outlier_results |>   dplyr::select(record_id, value, outliers_iqr, outliers_median, outliers_mean) |>   utils::tail() #> # A tibble: 6 × 5 #>   record_id value outliers_iqr outliers_median outliers_mean #>   <chr>     <dbl> <chr>        <chr>           <chr> #> 1 e8947016    321 normal value normal value    normal value #> 2 28b6ea90    353 normal value normal value    normal value #> 3 8aa281d9    246 normal value normal value    normal value #> 4 8b337b53    305 normal value normal value    normal value #> 5 7358f600    284 normal value normal value    normal value #> 6 499f0390    309 normal value normal value    normal value

title: Data quality
text: outlier_plot() returns list ggplot objects - one per method - faceted district coloured status. Facet labels show share outliers district.
code: plots <- outlier_plot(   data      = sl_dhis2,   column    = "conf",   record_id = "record_id",   adm1      = "adm1",   adm2      = "adm2",   yearmon   = "year_mon",   methods   = c("iqr", "median", "mean") )  plots$iqr plots$median plots$mean

title: Data quality
text: outliers identified, correct_outliers() replaces flagged values using one several strategies: supporting functions: impute_outlier_ma() - moving-average imputation; workhorse inside correct_outliers() method = "moving_average". impute_higher_admin() - borrow strength parent admin unit facility’s history sparse support within-unit estimate. fallback_diff(), fallback_row_sum(), safe_sum() - defensive numerical helpers used inside imputation paths. tolerate -NA rows, return zeros appropriate, refuse silently sum characters.
code: sl_corrected <- correct_outliers(   data        = sl_dhis2,   outliers    = outlier_results,   column      = "conf",   method      = "moving_average",   flag_method = "iqr" )

what: A quality pipeline, end to end
title: Data quality
text: output sl_clean - shape input flagged--replaced values - plus diagnostic objects (cascade, outliers) can hand reviewers evidence record edited.
code: # 1. cascade consistency at the facility-month level cascade <- consistency_check(   data      = sl_dhis2,   inputs    = c("test", "conf"),   outputs   = c("conf", "maltreat"),   show_plot = FALSE )  # 2. detect outliers on confirmed cases outliers <- detect_outliers(   data      = sl_dhis2,   column    = "conf",   yearmon   = "year_mon",   record_id = "record_id",   adm1      = "adm1",   adm2      = "adm2" )  # 3. correct using a moving average, flagged by the robust median rule sl_clean <- correct_outliers(   data        = sl_dhis2,   outliers    = outliers,   column      = "conf",   method      = "moving_average",   flag_method = "median" )

title: DHS
text: downloading anything, find indicator IDs want. check_dhs_indicators() thin wrapper around DHS API’s metadata endpoint, returning indicator catalogue tibble. Filter survey type, year range characteristic narrow : Returned fields include IndicatorId, Label, Definition default - pass returnFields add .
code: library(sntutils)  # what indicators exist for Sierra Leone surveys sl_indicators <- check_dhs_indicators(countryIds = "SL")  sl_indicators |>   dplyr::filter(stringr::str_detect(Label, "ITN|net")) |>   dplyr::select(IndicatorId, Label) |>   utils::head() #> # A tibble: 6 × 2 #>   IndicatorId        Label #>   <chr>              <chr> #> 1 ML_NETP_H_IT0      Households with at least one ITN #> 2 ML_NETP_H_IT2      Households with at least one ITN per two persons #> 3 ML_NETC_C_ITN      Children under 5 who slept under an ITN last night #> 4 ML_NETW_W_ITN      Women 15-49 who slept under an ITN last night #> 5 ML_NETP_H_ANY      Households with any mosquito net #> 6 ML_NETC_C_NET      Children under 5 who slept under any net last night # only MIS surveys since 2015 check_dhs_indicators(   countryIds       = "SL",   surveyType       = "MIS",   surveyYearStart  = 2015 )

title: DHS
text: indicator IDs, download_dhs_indicators() pulls estimates . hits DHS data endpoint directly (rdhs dependency, caching SQLite store).
code: # national-level malaria indicators for Sierra Leone & Togo, 2010+ dhs_df <- download_dhs_indicators(   countryIds   = "SL,TG",   indicatorIds = "ML_NETP_H_IT2,ML_PMAL_C_RDT,CN_NUTS_C_WH2",   surveyYearStart = 2010,   breakdown    = "national" )  dplyr::glimpse(dhs_df) #> Rows: 18 #> Columns: 11 #> $ DataId         <int> ... #> $ Indicator      <chr> "Households with at least one ITN per two... #> $ IndicatorId    <chr> "ML_NETP_H_IT2", ... #> $ Value          <dbl> 52.3, 60.1, ... #> $ Precision      <int> 1, 1, ... #> $ SurveyId       <chr> "SL2016MIS", "SL2019DHS", ... #> $ SurveyYear     <int> 2016, 2019, ... #> $ CountryName    <chr> "Sierra Leone", "Sierra Leone", ... #> $ ...

title: DHS
text: SNT usually want subnational values: output adds region-level rows whose CharacteristicLabel columns identify admin unit reported (typically adm1).
code: download_dhs_indicators(   countryIds   = "SL",   indicatorIds = "ML_NETC_C_ITN",   surveyYear   = 2019,   breakdown    = "subnational" )

title: DHS
text: know exactly survey want:
code: download_dhs_indicators(   countryIds   = "SL",   surveyIds    = "SL2016MIS",   indicatorIds = "ML_NETW_W_ITN,ML_PMAL_C_RDT" )

title: DHS
text: published indicators don’t cover need (custom denominators, multi-variable cross-tabs, restricted populations), answer DHS microdata. AHADI projects typically store parquet datasets per file type (HR, IR, KR, PR, BR, MR, …). get_dhs_data() registers directory parquet files DuckDB views, returning list ready dplyr / dbplyr queries. function: skips parquet files fail open (corrupted month doesn’t block whole load), exposes con element direct SQL queries, stores file metadata dhs$metadata (per-file row counts, schemas) audit. get tibble back memory: Close connection done DBI::dbDisconnect(dhs$con).
code: dhs <- get_dhs_data(   path  = "01_data/1.6_health_systems/1.6a_dhs/parquet",   types = c("HR", "IR", "KR")     # household, individual women, children )  dhs$HR |> dplyr::tbl() #> # Source:   table<HR> [?? x ??] #> # Database: DuckDB #>    hv001 hv002 hv005    hv024 hv025 ... #> 1     1     2    ...      Bo Urban #> ... itn_by_region <- dhs$HR |>   dplyr::tbl() |>   dplyr::group_by(hv024) |>   dplyr::summarise(     has_itn = mean(as.integer(hml1 >= 1), na.rm = TRUE)   ) |>   dplyr::collect()

what: A DHS pipeline, end to end
title: DHS
text: published indicators get us 80% way; microdata path 20% country team needs something DHS chapter never published.
code: # 1. find the indicator IDs for the malaria coverage chapter itn_meta <- check_dhs_indicators(   countryIds = "SL,TG",   surveyType = "MIS" ) |>   dplyr::filter(stringr::str_detect(Label, "ITN|net"))  # 2. pull the subnational values for those indicators itn_subnat <- download_dhs_indicators(   countryIds      = "SL,TG",   indicatorIds    = paste(itn_meta$IndicatorId, collapse = ","),   surveyYearStart = 2015,   breakdown       = "subnational" )  # 3. if we need a custom denominator, drop to microdata via DuckDB dhs <- get_dhs_data(   path  = "01_data/1.6_health_systems/1.6a_dhs/parquet",   types = c("HR", "PR") )  custom <- dhs$HR |>   dplyr::tbl() |>   dplyr::filter(hv025 == 1) |>            # urban only   dplyr::group_by(hv024) |>   dplyr::summarise(     has_itn = mean(as.integer(hml1 >= 1), na.rm = TRUE)   ) |>   dplyr::collect()  DBI::dbDisconnect(dhs$con)

title: Get started
text: sntutils R package developed AHADI support Subnational Tailoring (SNT) malaria interventions. bundles small, repeated operations find every country support analysis: reading messy DHIS2 exports, harmonising admin names across vintages shapefiles, validating facility coordinates, calculating reporting rates, extracting climate population rasters admin units, producing plots maps language country team works . package built around four ideas: One way , one way . read() write() handle every common format. read_snt_data() write_snt_data() add hashing metadata sidecars need reproducible audit trail. Tidy outputs. Every function returns tibble sf data frame stable, documented column names - never list lists. Country-aware defaults. Functions accept target_language plots, labels month names match team’s working language. Small surface area, big leverage. R/ folder ~50 source files; site groups , . New Subnational Tailoring? AHADI SNT Code Library methodology-level companion package - country examples, analytical reasoning, workflow behind every step sntutils automates. Good starting points: , Analysts, Producing high-quality outputs.

title: Get started
text: System dependencies: sntutils uses sf terra, require GDAL, GEOS PROJ. macOS install brew install gdal proj geos; Ubuntu use GDAL PPA. RStudio recent Posit Workbench builds already ship .
code: # 1) install pak if needed install.packages("pak")  # 2) install sntutils from GitHub pak::pkg_install("ahadi-analytics/sntutils")

what: The shape of an SNT pipeline
title: Get started
text: SNT projects move stages. sntutils provides function (small family functions) step: articles Workflows menu walk stage detail. page just shows whole pipeline , end--end.

title: Get started
text: read Sierra Leone DHIS2 sample, parse dates, standardise column names, calculate reporting rates district-month, draw plot. dataset ships package. ’s complete SNT mini-pipeline: read → clean → metric → plot, external state manual format wrangling. rest articles drill step.
code: library(sntutils)  # 1. read - sntutils::read() picks the importer from the file extension sl_dhis2 <- read(   system.file("extdata", "sl_exmaple_dhis2.rds", package = "sntutils") )  # 2. clean - make column names lowercase_with_underscores and parse dates sl_dhis2 <- sl_dhis2 |>   standardize_names() |>   autoparse_dates(date_cols = "date") |>   dplyr::rename(year_mon = date) |>   dplyr::mutate(     hf_uid    = vdigest(paste0(adm1, adm2, hf), algo = "xxhash32"),     record_id = vdigest(paste(hf_uid, year_mon),  algo = "xxhash32")   )  # 3. analyse - reporting rate by district-month rates <- calculate_reporting_metrics(   data             = sl_dhis2,   vars_of_interest = c("conf", "pres"),   x_var            = "year_mon",   y_var            = "adm2",   hf_col           = "hf_uid",   key_indicators   = c("allout", "test", "treat", "conf", "pres") )  tail(rates) #> # A tibble: 6 × 6 #>   year_mon adm2                                  rep   exp reprate missrate #>   <chr>    <chr>                               <int> <int>   <dbl>    <dbl> #> 1 2023-12  Moyamba District Council              106   108   0.981   0.0185 #> 2 2023-12  Port Loko City Council                  2     2   1       0 #> 3 2023-12  Port Loko District Council             99   103   0.961   0.0388 #> 4 2023-12  Pujehun District Council               96   104   0.923   0.0769 #> 5 2023-12  Tonkolili District Council            109   115   0.948   0.0522 #> 6 2023-12  Western Area Rural District Council    62    64   0.969   0.0312  # 4. visualise - facility-level reporting plot, in English reporting_rate_plot(   data             = sl_dhis2,   vars_of_interest = "conf",   x_var            = "year_mon",   y_var            = "adm2",   hf_col           = "hf_uid",   key_indicators   = c("allout", "test", "treat", "conf", "pres") )

title: Get started
text: Data /O cleaning - every read()/write() shortcut plus name date harmonisation. Spatial validation mapping - admin geometries, coordinates, shapefile crosswalks, fuzzy facility matching. Reporting rates data quality - three reporting-rate scenarios, consistency checks outlier methods. Climate population downloads - CHIRPS, ERA5, MODIS, NASA POWER, WorldPop DHS, extract admin units. Project setup utilities - folder scaffolding, paths, caching, translation small numeric helpers. something missing surprising, please open issue GitHub - package evolves SNT support work .

title: Project utilities
text: AHADI projects follow fixed top-level layout scripts written one country drop straight another. Two functions build .

title: Project utilities
text: Creates data tree 01_data/. domain folder raw/ processed/ subfolders. Every leaf folder (except 1.10_final/ 1.1f_cache_files/) gets raw/ processed/ subfolders automatically.
code: library(sntutils)  create_data_structure(base_path = ".") 01_data/ ├── 1.1_foundational/ │   ├── 1.1a_admin_boundaries/ │   ├── 1.1b_physical_features/ │   ├── 1.1c_health_facilities/ │   ├── 1.1d_community_health_workers/ │   ├── 1.1e_population/ │   │   ├── 1.1ei_national/ │   │   ├── 1.1eii_worldpop_rasters/ │   │   └── 1.1eiii_displaced_pop/ │   └── 1.1f_cache_files/ ├── 1.2_epidemiology/ │   ├── 1.2a_routine_surveillance/ │   ├── 1.2b_pfpr_estimates/ │   └── 1.2c_mortality_estimates/ ├── 1.3_interventions/ │   ├── 1.3a_itns/ │   ├── 1.3b_iptp/ │   ├── 1.3c_smc/ │   ├── 1.3d_vap/ │   ├── 1.3e_anc/ │   └── 1.3f_irs/ ├── 1.4_drug_efficacy_resistance/ ├── 1.5_environment/ │   ├── 1.5a_climate/ │   ├── 1.5b_accessibility/ │   └── 1.5c_land_use/ ├── 1.6_health_systems/ │   └── 1.6a_dhs/ ├── 1.7_entomology/ ├── 1.8_commodities/ ├── 1.9_finance/ └── 1.10_final/

title: Project utilities
text: data tree plus surrounding scaffolding scripts, outputs reports - use first day new project.
code: initialize_project_structure(base_path = "my_snt_project") my_snt_project/ ├── 01_data/                       # full hierarchical data tree from above ├── 02_scripts/ ├── 03_outputs/ │   ├── 3.1_validation/            (figures/ + tables/) │   ├── 3.2_intermediate_products/ (figures/ + tables/) │   ├── 3.3_final_snt_outputs/     (figures/ + tables/) │   └── 3.4_model/                 (figures/ + tables/) ├── 04_reports/ └── 05_metadata_docs/

title: Project utilities
text: folders exist, setup_project_paths() returns named list absolute paths every important location. Detects project root via ::() / rprojroot, getwd() fallback. keys short stable scripts don’t know project lives disk. Every download, validation processing function package accepts paths directly, typical script reads: ahadi_path() related helper navigating AHADI shared OneDrive - locates project name across team members’ synced roots, useful cross-machine reproducibility. clear_snt_cache() resets -memory caches used snt_data_dict() related helpers; call editing dictionary CSV without restarting R.
code: paths <- setup_project_paths(quiet = TRUE)  paths$admin_shp #> [1] "/Users/me/projects/sle-snt/01_data/1.1_foundational/1.1a_admin_boundaries"  paths$dhis2 #> [1] "/Users/me/projects/sle-snt/01_data/1.2_epidemiology/1.2a_routine_surveillance"  paths$val_fig #> [1] "/Users/me/projects/sle-snt/03_outputs/validation/figures" paths  <- setup_project_paths() shp    <- read(file.path(paths$admin_shp, "sle_adm2.geojson")) dhis2  <- read_snt_data(paths$dhis2, "sl_dhis2_clean")

title: Project utilities
text: Country teams switch English, French Portuguese near- weekly basis. Every plot label function sntutils accepts target_language argument, routes small translation stack.

title: Project utilities
text: Calls Google Translate (via gtranslate package) per unique string caches response local JSON file. next call (source, target, text) triple returns instantly cache.
code: translate_text("Reporting rate by district",                target_language = "fr",                source_language = "en") #> [1] "Taux de rapportage par district"  translate_text("Malaria cases",                target_language = "pt",                cache_path = "~/translation_cache") #> [1] "Casos de malária"

title: Project utilities
text: Vectorised wrapper translate_text() - translate whole column one call, caching shared across batch.
code: df <- tibble::tibble(   label = c("Confirmed cases", "Presumed cases", "Tests performed") )  df |>   dplyr::mutate(label_es = translate_text_vec(label, target_language = "es")) #> # A tibble: 3 × 2 #>   label           label_es #>   <chr>           <chr> #> 1 Confirmed cases Casos confirmados #> 2 Presumed cases  Casos presuntos #> 3 Tests performed Pruebas realizadas

title: Project utilities
text: Locale-aware month-year formatting time-series axes.
code: dates <- seq(as.Date("2022-01-01"), as.Date("2022-03-01"), by = "month")  translate_yearmon(dates, language = "fr") #> [1] "janv. 2022" "févr. 2022" "mars 2022"  translate_yearmon(dates, language = "es", format = "%B %Y") #> [1] "enero 2022" "febrero 2022" "marzo 2022"

title: Project utilities
text: Google Translate consistently butchers technical malaria acronyms asked translate plot titles, labels report text French - emits “PIT” instead TPI, literal phrase “moustiquaire imprégnée à longue durée d’action” instead MILDA, . french_malaria_acronyms() curated override list every translation-aware function sntutils consults keep French outputs publication-ready. list consumed automatically inside translate_text(), reporting_rate_plot(), consistency_check(), faceted-map helpers function target_language argument - don’t need call directly, ’s worth knowing exists French translation looks want extend override mapping country team.
code: french_malaria_acronyms() |> utils::head() #> # A tibble: 6 × 3 #>   en       fr      definition_fr #>   <chr>    <chr>   <chr> #> 1 ITN      MILDA   Moustiquaire imprégnée à longue durée d'action #> 2 IRS      AID     Aspersion intra-domiciliaire #> 3 IPT      TPI     Traitement préventif intermittent #> 4 IPTp     TPIp    Traitement préventif intermittent pendant la grossesse #> 5 SMC      CPS     Chimioprévention du paludisme saisonnier #> 6 RDT      TDR     Test de diagnostic rapide

title: Project utilities
text: SNT reports get assembled PDFs Word docs, embedded PNGs dominate file size. compress_png() wraps pngquant (installed demand) shrink PNGs ~70% visible quality loss. reporting_rate_plot() consistency_check() call hood saving compress_image = TRUE.
code: # single file compress_png(   "path/to/large_image.png",   output_path = "path/to/consistency_plot.png" ) #> ── Compression Summary ── #> #> ✔ Successfully compressed: consistency_plot.png #> ℹ Total compression: 200.21 KB (71.54% saved) #> ℹ Excellent compression! #> #> ── File Size #> Before compression: 279.87 KB #> After compression:   79.66 KB  # whole directory compress_png(   "path/to/image_folder/",   output_path = "path/to/compressed_folder/",   verbose     = TRUE )

title: Project utilities
text: vdigest() vectorised version digest::digest(). use constantly mint stable IDs facility-month records, deduplicate across DHIS2 exports, detect content changes caching expensive steps. Default algorithm md5, xxhash32 much faster long character vectors typical SNT data produces shorter IDs:
code: sl_dhis2 |>   dplyr::distinct(adm3) |>   dplyr::mutate(adm3_hash = vdigest(adm3)) |>   utils::head() #> # A tibble: 6 × 2 #>   adm3             adm3_hash #>   <chr>            <chr> #> 1 Bo City          c810b59ec12efb2ac8b5cc84f46857ce #> 2 Kakua Chiefdom   27fd84f751fac150c2f8a8f42b71c3da #> 3 Baoma Chiefdom   462ef3c87dc9b40b2ec2e0e0a54dd63e #> 4 Valunia Chiefdom df394518e6987ed686d76e83a409f090 #> 5 Bagbwe Chiefdom  3aa7a61247e34ab397ff813fe520c8b7 #> 6 Wonde Chiefdom   196dc9792e2038b41411ec2afae37e61 sl_dhis2 <- sl_dhis2 |>   dplyr::mutate(     hf_uid    = vdigest(paste0(adm1, adm2, hf), algo = "xxhash32"),     record_id = vdigest(paste(hf_uid, year_mon),  algo = "xxhash32")   )

title: Project utilities
text: small frequently used numerics prevent boilerplate every script.
code: # thousands separator, with configurable mark and decimals big_mark(1234567.89) #> [1] "1,234,567.89"  big_mark(c(1234.56, 7890123.45), decimals = 1, big_mark = " ") #> [1] "1 234.6"     "7 890 123.5"  # NA-safe wrappers sum2(c(1, 2, NA, 4)) #> [1] 7  mean2(c(1, 2, NA, 4)) #> [1] 2.333333  median2(c(1, 2, NA, 4, 5)) #> [1] 3

title: Project utilities
text: Routine surveillance data full partial rows, -NA months, columns arrived character Excel snuck comma somewhere. Plain sum() - either propagate NA everywhere silently coerce. three helpers solve problem three different scales correct_outliers(), impute_outlier_ma() cascade reconciliation paths call internally. ’re equally useful scripts.

what: safe_sum() - sum a vector without surprises
title: Project utilities
text: Returns NA every value missing; otherwise sums non-missing values never silently coerces character input zero.
code: safe_sum(c(1, 2, NA, 4)) #> [1] 7  safe_sum(c(NA, NA, NA)) #> [1] NA            # NOT 0 - you wanted "everything missing", you got it

what: fallback_row_sum() - sum a set of columns row-wise
title: Project utilities
text: Sums number columns row-wise explicit min_present floor: row needs least many non-missing columns total stays NA rather masquerading zero. Use .keep_zero_as_zero = FALSE want row explicit zeros also count informative; default treats c(0, 0, 0) valid zero total.
code: dplyr::tibble(   conf_u5  = c(10, NA, 0,  NA),   conf_5_14 = c( 5, 12, NA, NA),   conf_ov15 = c(20, 18, NA, NA) ) |>   dplyr::mutate(     conf_total = fallback_row_sum(conf_u5, conf_5_14, conf_ov15,                                   min_present = 2)   ) #> # A tibble: 4 × 4 #>   conf_u5 conf_5_14 conf_ov15 conf_total #>     <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl>      <dbl> #> 1      10         5        20         35 #> 2      NA        12        18         30   # 2 of 3 present - OK #> 3       0        NA        NA         NA   # only 1 present - flagged #> 4      NA        NA        NA         NA

what: fallback_diff() - subtraction that won’t go negative
title: Project utilities
text: conf - maltreat non-negative clean cascade. ’s , want either NA (mark review) clamped value. fallback_diff() latter configurable floor. function powers cascade-reconciliation step correct_outliers(): outlier replaced, downstream cascade variable recomputed via fallback_diff() resulting row stays internally consistent.
code: fallback_diff(col1 = c(100, 80, 50), col2 = c(40, 100, NA)) #> [1] 60  0 NA          # clamped at the `minimum = 0` floor  # stricter: NA out any negative or invalid subtraction fallback_diff(c(100, 80, 50), c(40, 100, NA), minimum = NA) #> [1] 60 NA NA

title: Project utilities
text: handful internal building blocks exported country teams reuse custom one-charts: get_palette() / list_palettes() - AHADI-branded discrete gradient palettes (ahadi_main, ahadi_warm, ahadi_cool, …). auto_bin() - choose bin edges indicator using Fisher-Jenks quantile breaks, returning labelled factor. utilities power auto-parsers exposed ad-hoc use. get_model(), generate_ir_plot(), run_resistance_trend() - fit render incidence-rate / resistance-trend plots used insecticide-resistance reports. prepare_plot_data(), get_pathway_vars() - internal data-shaping helpers show custom reporting pipelines.

title: Project utilities
text: typical script header AHADI ends looking like : ’s six lines script country-month reporting plot audit trail, stable IDs, French labels compressed PNG ready drop PDF. plumbing article makes analysis lines header short.
code: library(sntutils)  # 1. resolve where everything lives paths <- setup_project_paths()  # 2. read with audit trail sl_dhis2 <- read_snt_data(paths$dhis2, "sl_dhis2_clean")  # 3. mint stable IDs sl_dhis2 <- sl_dhis2 |>   dplyr::mutate(     hf_uid    = vdigest(paste0(adm1, adm2, hf), algo = "xxhash32"),     record_id = vdigest(paste(hf_uid, year_mon),  algo = "xxhash32")   )  # 4. produce country-team-language outputs reporting_rate_plot(   data             = sl_dhis2,   vars_of_interest = "conf",   x_var            = "year_mon",   y_var            = "adm2",   hf_col           = "hf_uid",   key_indicators   = c("allout", "test", "treat", "conf", "pres"),   target_language  = "fr",   compress_image   = TRUE,   plot_path        = paths$final_fig )

title: Rasters
text: default CHIRPS / ERA5-monthly / MODIS-monthly. Dates detected automatically filenames; function aligns CRS raster shapefile, crops raster shapefile extent extraction, returns tibble admin x time x stat. Pass aggregations = c("mean", "sum", "median", "max") get several stats one pass.
code: library(sntutils)  adm3_shp <- sf::st_read(system.file(   "extdata", "sle_adm3_example.geojson", package = "sntutils" ))  raster_dir <- system.file("extdata", "chirps_test_rasters",                           package = "sntutils")  rainfall_df <- process_raster_collection(   directory    = raster_dir,   shapefile    = adm3_shp,   id_cols      = c("adm0", "adm1", "adm2", "adm3"),   aggregations = c("mean"),   pattern      = "\\.tif$" )  rainfall_df |> utils::head() #>                                file_name    adm1     adm2          adm3 year month      mean #> 1   africa_monthly_chirps-v2.0.2020.01.tif EASTERN KAILAHUN          DEA 2020     1 12.871692 #> 2   africa_monthly_chirps-v2.0.2020.01.tif EASTERN KAILAHUN         JAHN 2020     1  9.820749 #> 3   africa_monthly_chirps-v2.0.2020.01.tif EASTERN KAILAHUN        JAWIE 2020     1 12.042542 #> 4   africa_monthly_chirps-v2.0.2020.01.tif EASTERN KAILAHUN   KISSI KAMA 2020     1  8.951293

title: Rasters
text: Use raster needs extracted several shapefiles (adm1, adm2, adm3 simultaneously), different ID column sets.

what: An extraction pipeline, end to end
title: Rasters
text: Two archives (climate + population), one tibble keyed admin date - canonical input stratification modelling. Want map extracted tibble small-area surface? See Climate article’s mapping section worked example using facetted_map_gradient() get_palette() colour helpers. recipe works raster-extracted tibble.
code: # 1. download climate (see the Climate article) download_chirps(   dataset = "africa_monthly",   start   = "2020-01", end = "2024-12",   out_dir = "01_data/1.5_environment/1.5a_climate/raw/chirps" )  # 2. download population (see the WorldPop article) download_worldpop(   country_codes = "SLE",   years         = 2020:2024,   resolution    = "1km",   dest_dir      = "01_data/1.1_foundational/1.1c_population/1.1cii_worldpop_rasters" )  # 3. unweighted population sum by adm2-year - sum the WorldPop pixels pop_adm2 <- process_raster_collection(   directory    = "01_data/1.1_foundational/1.1c_population/1.1cii_worldpop_rasters",   shapefile    = sle_adm2_clean,   id_cols      = c("adm0_name", "adm1_name", "adm2_name"),   aggregations = "sum",   pattern      = "_ppp_\\d{4}_1km.*\\.tif$" )  # 4. population-weighted mean rainfall by adm2-month rain_adm2 <- process_weighted_raster_collection(   directory     = "01_data/1.5_environment/1.5a_climate/raw/chirps",   shapefile     = sle_adm2_clean,   weight_raster = "01_data/1.1_foundational/1.1c_population/1.1cii_worldpop_rasters/sle_ppp_2020_1km.tif",   id_cols       = c("adm0_name", "adm1_name", "adm2_name"),   aggregations  = "mean" )  # 5. join - one tibble keyed by admin and year analysis_ready <- rain_adm2 |>   dplyr::left_join(pop_adm2, by = c("adm0_name", "adm1_name", "adm2_name", "year"))

title: Reporting rates
text: calculate_reporting_metrics() computes completeness routine reporting checking whether health facilities submitted valid data specified set indicators time. calculates rates one target variables (vars_of_interest) expectation set defined key indicators (key_indicators). facility counts reporting given year-month selected vars_of_interest non-missing. facility included denominator given year-month already reported key_indicators month. prevents newly-opened facilities dragging historic non-reporting rate . Let: aa - administrative unit (e.g. district) tt - time period (year-month) ff - facility aa key_indicators - variables used determine whether facility active, e.g. "test", "treat", "conf", "pres", "allout" vars_of_interest - variables want reporting rate , e.g. "conf", "pres" reporting rate unit aa period tt Reporting Ratea,t=oa,tea,t \text{Reporting Rate}_{,t} \;=\; \frac{o_{,t}}{e_{,t}} oa,to_{,t} - facilities aa reported value vars_of_interest tt ea,te_{,t} - facilities aa whose first-ever report key_indicators occurred tt.

title: Reporting rates
text: Suppose want reporting rate district dd March: 6 facilities dd 6 first-reported least one key indicator March 4 reported "conf" March Reporting Rated,Mar=46=0.667 \text{Reporting Rate}_{d,\text{Mar}} \;=\; \frac{4}{6} \;=\; 0.667

title: Reporting rates
text: rate almost always want reporting country team. uses evolving denominator (facilities ever reported) early months new system aren’t punished. plot version reporting_rate_plot() arguments:
code: calculate_reporting_metrics(   data             = sl_dhis2,   vars_of_interest = c("conf", "pres"),   x_var            = "year_mon",   y_var            = "adm2",   hf_col           = "hf_uid",   key_indicators   = c("allout", "test", "treat", "conf", "pres") ) |>   utils::tail() #> # A tibble: 6 × 6 #>   year_mon adm2                                  rep   exp reprate missrate #>   <chr>    <chr>                               <int> <int>   <dbl>    <dbl> #> 1 2023-12  Moyamba District Council              106   108   0.981   0.0185 #> 2 2023-12  Port Loko City Council                  2     2   1       0 #> 3 2023-12  Port Loko District Council             99   103   0.961   0.0388 #> 4 2023-12  Pujehun District Council               96   104   0.923   0.0769 #> 5 2023-12  Tonkolili District Council            109   115   0.948   0.0522 #> 6 2023-12  Western Area Rural District Council    62    64   0.969   0.0312 reporting_rate_plot(   data             = sl_dhis2,   vars_of_interest = "conf",   x_var            = "year_mon",   y_var            = "adm2",   hf_col           = "hf_uid",   key_indicators   = c("allout", "test", "treat", "conf", "pres") )

text: deeper diagnostics, classify_facility_activity() labels facility-month active, inactive, never-reported discontinued, using configurable rolling window. facility_reporting_plot() renders tiled timeline can spot facilities stopped reporting mid-2022 never came online: get_active_facilities() returns just active subset, ready feed back filtered analysis. compare_methods_plot() lets us compare two reporting-rule choices (e.g. indicator vs indicators) data. validate_routine_hf_data() upstream sanity check - runs battery structural checks routine HF dataset (required columns, parseable dates, plausible value ranges) reporting-rate functions get . Run ingest.

title: Spatial
text: SNT work geohub boundaries canonical input. download_shapefile() pulls ISO3 code admin level returns sf object ready use. dest_path NULL file downloaded session-scoped cache persisted across runs.
code: library(sntutils)  # pull adm2 boundaries for Sierra Leone and Togo boundaries <- download_shapefile(   country_codes = c("SLE", "TGO"),   admin_level   = "adm2",   latest        = TRUE,   dest_path     = "01_data/1.1_foundational/1.1a_admin_boundaries" )  class(boundaries) #> [1] "sf"         "tbl_df"     "tbl"        "data.frame"

title: Spatial
text: shapefile hand, validate_process_spatial() runs battery checks - invalid geometry, mixed Z/M dimensions, wrong CRS, missing admin codes, duplicated admin names - (fix_issues = TRUE) attempts safe automatic repairs. always returns shape: list cleaned sf object plus tibble issues found. issues tibble audit trail - keep next shapefile disk reviewers can see changed.
code: boundaries_clean <- validate_process_spatial(   shp          = boundaries,   name         = "WHO geohub SLE adm2",   adm0_col     = "adm0_name",   adm1_col     = "adm1_name",   adm2_col     = "adm2_name",   fix_issues   = TRUE,   geometry_crs = 4326,   drop_z       = TRUE )  names(boundaries_clean) #> [1] "shp"     "issues"  "summary"

title: Spatial
text: facility data, validate_process_coordinates() checks lat/lon columns parse, sit within plausible bounds, enough decimal precision real readings (default min_decimals = 3), fall inside country polygon pass adm0_sf. Returns sf valid POINT geometry plus issues tibble dropped flagged rows.
code: hf_clean <- validate_process_coordinates(   data         = hf_raw,   name         = "SLE master facility list",   lon_col      = "longitude",   lat_col      = "latitude",   adm0_sf      = boundaries_clean$shp |>     dplyr::filter(adm0_name == "Sierra Leone"),   geometry_crs = 4326,   min_decimals = 3,   id_col       = "facility_uid",   fix_issues   = TRUE )  hf_clean$summary #> ● 4,213 input rows #> ● 4,189 valid coordinates retained #> ●     8 rows dropped (low precision) #> ●    16 rows dropped (outside adm0)

title: Spatial
text: country redistricts (Sierra Leone 2017, Togo 2019, several recent DRC changes), historical surveillance data keyed old boundaries new analyses new. crosswalk_shapefiles_sf() computes area-weighted overlap two sf layers can reproject indicators forward backward. Multiply weight dplyr::group_by(adm2_name) |> summarise(sum()) push aggregate counts old units new.
code: xwalk <- crosswalk_shapefiles_sf(   old_sf = sle_adm2_2014,   new_sf = sle_adm2_2022,   level  = "adm2",   old_suffix = "_old",   min_weight = 0.01,        # drop slivers <1%   area_crs   = 32629,       # Sierra Leone equal-area   verbose    = TRUE )  xwalk |> dplyr::select(adm2_old, adm2_name, weight, primary) |> head() #> # A tibble: 6 × 4 #>   adm2_old        adm2_name       weight primary #>   <chr>           <chr>            <dbl> <lgl> #> 1 Bo District     Bo District      0.998 TRUE #> 2 Bo District     Bo City          0.002 FALSE #> 3 Bonthe          Bonthe District  1.000 TRUE #> 4 …

title: Spatial
text: DHIS2 facility names rarely line perfectly master facility list. fuzzy_match_facilities() runs staged matching pipeline: exact match, match standardize_names() normalisation, string-distance match using one methods (jw, lv, osa, …), optional interactive picker unresolved rows, returns tibble best matches plus diagnostics stage. calculate_match_stats() summarises results method can compare matching strategies side side.
code: matches <- fuzzy_match_facilities(   target_df       = dhis2_facilities,        # what we're cleaning   lookup_df       = mfl_facilities,          # the reference list   admin_cols      = c("adm1", "adm2", "adm3"),   hf_col_name     = "hf",   uid_col         = "hf_uid",   fuzzy_methods   = c("jw", "osa"),   fuzzy_threshold = 95,   match_interactivity = TRUE,   save_path = "01_data/1.1_foundational/1.1b_health_facilities/processed" )  matches$results |>   dplyr::count(match_status) #> # A tibble: 3 × 2 #>   match_status     n #>   <chr>        <int> #> 1 high          4012 #> 2 medium         147 #> 3 low             54

Pair check_snt_var() rename confirm resulting column names match canonical SNT schema (see Read & clean).

what: A spatial pipeline, end to end
title: Spatial
text: end pipeline single, validated sf boundary file, validated sf facility-points file, high-confidence link DHIS2 facility names MFL, baseline map. Everything follows - reporting rates, climate extraction, population weighting - assumes foundation.
code: # 1. pull boundaries sle_adm2 <- download_shapefile(   country_codes = "SLE",   admin_level   = "adm2",   latest        = TRUE )  # 2. validate them sle_adm2_clean <- validate_process_spatial(   shp = sle_adm2, name = "SLE adm2",   adm0_col = "adm0_name", adm1_col = "adm1_name", adm2_col = "adm2_name",   fix_issues = TRUE )$shp  # 3. validate facility coordinates against the polygon hf_geo <- validate_process_coordinates(   data    = hf_raw,   lon_col = "longitude", lat_col = "latitude",   adm0_sf = sle_adm2_clean,   id_col  = "facility_uid",   fix_issues = TRUE )$shp  # 4. fuzzy-match DHIS2 facility names to the master list match_results <- fuzzy_match_facilities(   target_df  = sl_dhis2 |> dplyr::distinct(adm1, adm2, adm3, hf, hf_uid),   lookup_df  = hf_geo,   admin_cols = c("adm1", "adm2", "adm3"),   hf_col_name = "hf",   uid_col    = "hf_uid" )$results  # 5. plot plot_admin_map_distinct(   sf_data  = sle_adm2_clean,   fill_col = "adm1_name",   title    = "Sierra Leone - adm2 by region" )

title: WorldPop
text: default download. Automatically picks right WorldPop release based years requested: Legacy (2000–2020) - UN-adjusted, 1 km, count density. R2025A (2015–2030) - constrained built-areas, 1 km 100 m, count . year range spans releases (e.g. 2010:2020) function pulls stitches .
code: library(sntutils)  # Guinea, 2015–2020, 1 km count (default) download_worldpop(   country_codes = "GIN",   years         = 2015:2020,   type          = "count",   resolution    = "1km",   dest_dir      = "01_data/1.1_foundational/1.1c_population/1.1cii_worldpop_rasters" )  # legacy persons-per-km² for the same country download_worldpop(   country_codes = "GIN",   years         = 2010:2014,   type          = "density",   resolution    = "1km",   dest_dir      = "01_data/1.1_foundational/1.1c_population/1.1cii_worldpop_rasters" )  # high-resolution: 100 m, R2025A only download_worldpop(   country_codes = "SLE",   years         = 2020:2024,   resolution    = "100m",   dest_dir      = "01_data/1.1_foundational/1.1c_population/1.1cii_worldpop_rasters" )

title: WorldPop
text: Pass "GLOBAL" (case-insensitive) instead ISO3 code download worldwide mosaic WorldPop’s 0_Mosaicked directory. right input multi-country / continental analyses don’t want stitch per-country rasters . GLOBAL requires year ≥ 2015, 1 km, type = "count".
code: download_worldpop(   country_codes = "GLOBAL",   years         = 2020:2024,   dest_dir      = "01_data/1.1_foundational/1.1c_population/1.1cii_worldpop_rasters" )

title: WorldPop
text: download_worldpop() skips files already exist right size, re-running call -op. returned (invisible) list resolved file paths per-country success counter logging.

title: WorldPop
text: interventions targeted specific demographic groups (women reproductive age ITN allocation, children 5 SMC, etc.) WorldPop publishes 5-year age-band rasters. sntutils download two modes: years ≥ 2015 sex = "", function fetches single pre-summed "t" total raster (one download per band). years < 2015 (explicit sex requested), male female rasters downloaded summed locally. Switch release = "R2024B" specifically need previous release; default R2025A. global mosaic (country_codes = "GLOBAL") also works years ≥ 2015 1 km.
code: # women 15–49 (reproductive age) for ITN allocation download_worldpop_age_band(   country_codes = "SLE",   years         = 2020:2024,   age_range     = c(15, 49),   sex           = "female",   resolution    = "1km",   release       = "R2025A",   out_dir       = "01_data/1.1_foundational/1.1c_population/1.1cii_worldpop_rasters" )  # children 1–9 (both sexes, total) - uses the "t" variant under the hood download_worldpop_age_band(   country_codes = "SLE",   years         = 2020:2024,   age_range     = c(1, 9),   sex           = "both",   resolution    = "1km",   release       = "R2025A",   out_dir       = "01_data/1.1_foundational/1.1c_population/1.1cii_worldpop_rasters" )

title: WorldPop
text: WorldPop’s urbanicity layer classifies pixel urban / peri-urban / rural. Used SNT urban microstratification weight interventions differently across urban gradient.
code: download_worldpop_urbanicity(   country_codes = c("SLE", "TGO"),   years         = 2020,   out_dir       = "01_data/1.5_environment/1.5c_land_use" )

title: WorldPop
text: series downloads spread different days, usually need know “2020 1 km count raster end ?”. get_worldpop_paths() returns resolved paths country × year matrix: Feed resulting path column process_weighted_raster_collection() population-weighted climate extraction, process_raster_collection() plain zonal sums population admin.
code: get_worldpop_paths(   country_codes = c("SLE", "TGO"),   years         = 2020:2024,   dest_dir      = "01_data/1.1_foundational/1.1c_population/1.1cii_worldpop_rasters" ) #> # A tibble: 10 × 4 #>    country year  resolution path #>    <chr>   <int> <chr>      <chr> #>  1 SLE     2020  1km        .../sle_ppp_2020_1km_Aggregated_UNadj.tif #>  2 SLE     2021  1km        .../sle_ppp_2021_1km_Aggregated_UNadj.tif #>  ...

title: WorldPop
text: Methodology note. code library’s Population rasters chapter authoritative reference growth-rate-based population projection SNT. Use national statistics bureau’s growth rate whenever available - function makes easy apply right number, invent one. Avoid ad hoc rates outside early exploration. WorldPop ends 2030 (R2025A). years WorldPop doesn’t publish, extrapolate_pop() fills gap. supports three strategies: Automatic growth rate - fit growth rate observed years per location. Single multiplier - apply growth every column / year. Per-column, per-year multipliers - full control via named list. Extrapolation can also backward (years earlier earliest observed) using syntax - pass older years years_to_extrap.
code: dummy_pop <- expand.grid(   adm0 = "COUNTRYX",   adm1 = c("RegionA", "RegionB"),   adm2 = c("District1", "District2"),   year = 2018:2020 ) |>   dplyr::mutate(     adm3      = paste0(adm2, "_Sub"),     pop_total = sample(1000:5000, dplyr::n(), replace = TRUE),     pop_0_11m = pop_total * 0.08,     pop_0_4y  = pop_total * 0.15,     pop_u15   = pop_total * 0.45   )  # 1. let the function figure out the growth rate extrapolate_pop(   data            = dummy_pop,   year_col        = "year",   pop_cols        = c("pop_total", "pop_0_11m", "pop_0_4y", "pop_u15"),   group_cols      = c("adm0", "adm1", "adm2", "adm3"),   years_to_extrap = c(2021, 2022) )  # 2. flat 3% growth across everything extrapolate_pop(   data            = dummy_pop,   year_col        = "year",   pop_cols        = c("pop_total", "pop_0_11m", "pop_0_4y", "pop_u15"),   group_cols      = c("adm0", "adm1", "adm2", "adm3"),   years_to_extrap = c(2021, 2022),   multiplier      = 1.03 )  # 3. per-column, per-year - fine control extrapolate_pop(   data            = dummy_pop,   year_col        = "year",   pop_cols        = c("pop_total", "pop_0_11m"),   group_cols      = c("adm0", "adm1", "adm2", "adm3"),   years_to_extrap = c(2021, 2022),   multiplier = list(     pop_total = c(`2021` = 1.03, `2022` = 1.025),     pop_0_11m = c(`2021` = 1.035, `2022` = 1.030)   ) )

what: Reshaping to the SNT long format
title: WorldPop
text: final step stratification: reshape wide population tibble canonical SNT long format, optionally translated labels country-team reports. snt_process_population() builds per-admin-level summaries (adm0..adm3, whichever present), produces bilingual EN + optional FR data dictionary, records admin levels available - exactly inputs downstream plotting stratification functions expect.
code: snt_process_population(   pop_data    = pop_long,   pop_cols    = c("pop_total", "pop_u5", "pop_5_14", "pop_ov15"),   translate   = TRUE,   language    = "fr",   infer_types = TRUE )

what: A WorldPop pipeline, end to end
title: WorldPop
text: result one tibble keyed admin year, total age-band populations, extrapolated whatever year project ends - canonical denominator incidence, intervention coverage need-based allocation downstream.
code: # 1. download total + age-band rasters for Sierra Leone, 2020–2024 download_worldpop(   country_codes = "SLE",   years         = 2020:2024,   type          = "count",   resolution    = "1km",   dest_dir      = "01_data/1.1_foundational/1.1c_population/1.1cii_worldpop_rasters" )  download_worldpop_age_band(   country_codes = "SLE",   years         = 2020:2024,   age_range     = c(0, 4),   sex           = "both",   resolution    = "1km",   out_dir       = "01_data/1.1_foundational/1.1c_population/1.1cii_worldpop_rasters" )  # 2. resolve their paths and aggregate to adm2 (sum across pixels) paths <- get_worldpop_paths(   country_codes = "SLE",   years         = 2020:2024,   dest_dir      = "01_data/1.1_foundational/1.1c_population/1.1cii_worldpop_rasters" )  pop_adm2 <- process_raster_collection(   directory    = unique(dirname(paths$path)),   shapefile    = sle_adm2_clean,   id_cols      = c("adm0_name", "adm1_name", "adm2_name"),   aggregations = "sum",   pattern      = "_ppp_\\d{4}_1km.*\\.tif$" )  # 3. extend to 2025–2026 (out of WorldPop's window) pop_adm2 <- extrapolate_pop(   data            = pop_adm2,   year_col        = "year",   pop_cols        = "sum",   group_cols      = c("adm0_name", "adm1_name", "adm2_name"),   years_to_extrap = c(2025, 2026),   multiplier      = 1.029 )  # 4. reshape into the canonical SNT long format with French labels pop_snt <- snt_process_population(   pop_data = pop_adm2,   pop_cols = "sum",   translate = TRUE,   language  = "fr" )

what: sntutils - utilities for Subnational Tailoring of malaria interventions
title: sntutils - utilities for Subnational Tailoring of malaria interventions
text: sntutils R package developed AHADI support Subnational Tailoring (SNT) malaria interventions. bundles small, repeated operations every SNT support analysis - reading DHIS2 exports, harmonising admin names across shapefile vintages, validating facility coordinates, calculating reporting rates, extracting climate population rasters admin units, rendering country-team-language plots maps. Full documentation, worked examples every workflow, lives https://ahadi-analytics.github.io/sntutils/ pkgdown site canonical reference. README short orientation index package’s surface area.

title: sntutils - utilities for Subnational Tailoring of malaria interventions
text: System dependencies: sntutils uses sf terra, require GDAL, GEOS PROJ. macOS install brew install gdal proj geos; Ubuntu use GDAL PPA.
code: # install pak if needed install.packages("pak")  # install sntutils from GitHub pak::pkg_install("ahadi-analytics/sntutils")

title: sntutils - utilities for Subnational Tailoring of malaria interventions

code: library(sntutils)  # 1. read a DHIS2-style export (any common format works) sl_dhis2 <- read(   system.file("extdata", "sl_exmaple_dhis2.rds", package = "sntutils") )  # 2. clean it sl_dhis2 <- sl_dhis2 |>   standardize_names() |>   autoparse_dates(date_cols = "date") |>   dplyr::rename(year_mon = date) |>   dplyr::mutate(     hf_uid = vdigest(paste0(adm1, adm2, hf), algo = "xxhash32")   )  # 3. calculate district-month reporting rates calculate_reporting_metrics(   data             = sl_dhis2,   vars_of_interest = c("conf", "pres"),   x_var            = "year_mon",   y_var            = "adm2",   hf_col           = "hf_uid",   key_indicators   = c("allout", "test", "treat", "conf", "pres") )

what: Resolve path inside AHADI OneDrive shared library — ahadi_path
title: Resolve path inside AHADI OneDrive shared library — ahadi_path
text: ahadi_path() builds robust, OS-aware path file folder inside AHADI OneDrive "Shared Library". : detects OS (Windows macOS) scans common OneDrive roots (including CloudStorage macOS) finds "Shared Libraries" folder given organisation fuzzy-matches requested library name navigates given base folder inside library optionally joins relative path function caches resolved library root R option subsequent calls fast. Use refresh = TRUE force re-detection.

title: Resolve path inside AHADI OneDrive shared library — ahadi_path
code: ahadi_path(   relative = NULL,   org = "Applied Health Analytics for Delivery and Innovation Inc",   library = "AHADI Information - technical",   base = "Documentation per topic/data/dhs_data",   refresh = FALSE,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Resolve path inside AHADI OneDrive shared library — ahadi_path
text: relative character. Relative path inside base. NULL, function returns resolved base folder path. org character. Organisation name appears OneDrive shared library naming. cleaned internally matching. Default "Applied Health Analytics Delivery Innovation Inc". library character. Display name OneDrive shared library. Example: "AHADI Information - technical". base character. Base folder inside library. Example: "Documentation per topic/data/dhs_data". refresh logical. TRUE, ignores cached location forces fresh search. verbose logical. TRUE, prints diagnostic messages path resolved.

title: Resolve path inside AHADI OneDrive shared library — ahadi_path
text: character scalar giving absolute path. relative NULL, resolved base path. Otherwise file.path(base, relative).

title: Resolve path inside AHADI OneDrive shared library — ahadi_path
text: detection logic follows several steps: detect OS using Sys.info()[["sysname"]] construct list candidate OneDrive root folders within root, look folders whose names suggest OneDrive "Shared Libraries" mount inside , fuzzy match requested organisation library names construct validate requested base folder Matching done "cleaned" names (lowercase, spaces punctuation). makes function resilient cosmetic differences naming "AHADI Information - technical" vs "AHADI information (technical)". function find matching organisation, library, base folder, fails cli::cli_abort() prints closest matches see.

title: Resolve path inside AHADI OneDrive shared library — ahadi_path
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # get the base DHS data directory base_path <- ahadi_path()  # get a specific file inside the base folder file_path <- ahadi_path("surveys/2020/survey_data.csv")  # use a different library and base folder other_path <- ahadi_path(   relative = "my_file.xlsx",   library = "AHADI Operations",   base = "SOPs" )  # force re-detection if the OneDrive setup changed ahadi_path(refresh = TRUE, verbose = TRUE) } # }

what: Automatically bin numeric data for choropleth maps — auto_bin
title: Automatically bin numeric data for choropleth maps — auto_bin
text: Selects appropriate binning method based data distribution returns bins matching colors. Three methods available: head-tail breaks highly skewed data, hybrid (quantile + tail) moderately skewed data, pure quantile roughly symmetric data.

title: Automatically bin numeric data for choropleth maps — auto_bin
code: auto_bin(   x,   palette = "default",   bin = 6,   decimals = 2,   round_to = 50,   reverse = FALSE,   labels = NULL,   outlier_threshold = NULL,   outlier_color = "#636363",   outlier_label = NULL )

title: Automatically bin numeric data for choropleth maps — auto_bin
text: x Numeric vector bin. palette Character. Either preset name custom character vector hex colors. Use list_palettes() see available presets. bin Integer. Number bins. Default 6. decimals Integer. Number decimal places labels. Default 2. round_to Numeric. Round break points increment. Default 50. Set NULL raw values (useful decimal data like rates). reverse Logical. Reverse color order? Default FALSE. labels Character vector. Optional custom bin labels. provided, skips automatic binning uses labels breaks parsed label strings. Supports formats: "0–50", "50-100", ">1000". Example: c("0–50", "50–100", "100–250", "250–450", "450–700", "700–1000", ">1000") outlier_threshold Numeric. Optional threshold create separate outlier bin values threshold. Useful metrics like TPR values >1 unusual. Default NULL (outlier handling). outlier_color Character. Hex color outlier bin. used outlier_threshold specified. Default "#636363" (dark grey). outlier_label Character. Optional custom label outlier bin. NULL (default), uses auto-generated format.

title: Automatically bin numeric data for choropleth maps — auto_bin
text: list : bins Ordered factor bin labels value x colors Named character vector mapping labels colors counts Data frame bin labels counts (n) method Character. binning method used: "headtail", "hybrid", "quantile", "custom" diagnostics List prop_zero, skew_ratio, tail_share

title: Automatically bin numeric data for choropleth maps — auto_bin
text: Method selection logic: headtail: prop_zero > 0.1, skew_ratio > 4, tail_share > 0.4 hybrid: skew_ratio > 2 quantile: otherwise custom: labels parameter provided Use list_palettes() see available palette names.

title: Automatically bin numeric data for choropleth maps — auto_bin

what: Infer column types using readr, then layer factor detection — auto_parse_types
title: Infer column types using readr, then layer factor detection — auto_parse_types
text: use readr::type_convert() infer non-factor types (numeric, integer, date, datetime, logical). propose factors via low-cardinality rules. protect id-like names leading-zero codes. return dataset default, include metadata plan requested.

title: Infer column types using readr, then layer factor detection — auto_parse_types
code: auto_parse_types(   data,   max_levels = 50,   max_unique_ratio = 0.2,   protect_patterns = c("id$", "uid$", "code$", "ref$", "key$"),   keep_leading_zero_chars = TRUE,   prefer_logical_for_binary = TRUE,   apply = TRUE,   return = c("data", "both", "plan") )

title: Infer column types using readr, then layer factor detection — auto_parse_types
text: data data.frame tibble. max_levels integer. max distinct values factor. default 50. max_unique_ratio numeric (0, 1]. max unique/n factor. default 0.2. protect_patterns character regexes protected names. default c("id$", "uid$", "code$", "ref$", "key$"). keep_leading_zero_chars logical. keep character value leading zeros digit-strings. default TRUE. prefer_logical_for_binary logical. kept api compatibility, used delegating readr. default TRUE. apply logical. TRUE, apply factor conversions top parsed types. default TRUE. return one "data", "", "plan". default "data".

title: Infer column types using readr, then layer factor detection — auto_parse_types
text: invisible object depending return: "data": tibble parsed data (factors apply = TRUE) "": list(plan = tibble, data = tibble ) "plan": tibble

title: Infer column types using readr, then layer factor detection — auto_parse_types
code: df <- tibble::tibble(   id  = c("001", "002", "003"),   sex = c("M", "F", "F"),   age = c("1", "2", "3"),   dt  = c(     "2024-01-01 12:00:00",     "2024-01-02 00:00:00",     "2024-01-03 01:02:03"   ) )  # parsed types + inferred factors dat <- auto_parse_types(df, apply = TRUE, return = "data")  # dataset and plan both <- auto_parse_types(df, apply = TRUE, return = "both") both$plan |> dplyr::select(name, current_type, proposed_type, rule) #> # A tibble: 4 × 4 #>   name  current_type proposed_type rule              #>   <chr> <chr>        <chr>         <chr>             #> 1 id    character    character     protected_by_name #> 2 sex   character    character     readr:character   #> 3 age   character    integer       readr:integer     #> 4 dt    character    POSIXct       readr:POSIXct     dplyr::glimpse(both$data) #> Rows: 3 #> Columns: 4 #> $ id  <chr> "001", "002", "003" #> $ sex <chr> "M", "F", "F" #> $ age <int> 1, 2, 3 #> $ dt  <dttm> 2024-01-01 12:00:00, 2024-01-02 00:00:00, 2024-01-03 01:02:03

what: Batch Extraction of Weighted Mean from Raster Using Population Weights — batch_extract_weighted_stats
title: Batch Extraction of Weighted Mean from Raster Using Population Weights — batch_extract_weighted_stats
text: Computes population-weighted means value raster (e.g., PfPR2-10) using corresponding population raster weights, across administrative boundaries.

title: Batch Extraction of Weighted Mean from Raster Using Population Weights — batch_extract_weighted_stats
code: batch_extract_weighted_stats(   value_raster_file,   pop_raster_file,   shapefile,   id_cols = c("adm0", "adm1", "adm2"),   value_layer_to_process = 1,   weight_na_as_zero = TRUE,   stat_type = "mean",   pattern_info = NULL,   time_components = NULL )

title: Batch Extraction of Weighted Mean from Raster Using Population Weights — batch_extract_weighted_stats
text: value_raster_file Path raster file containing values weight (e.g., PfPR2-10) pop_raster_file Path population raster file used weighting shapefile sf SpatVector object administrative boundaries id_cols Character vector administrative columns keep value_layer_to_process Integer character. Specifies layer multi-layer raster extract. raster contains multiple layers (e.g., different years indicators), argument selects layer processed. Default 1. weight_na_as_zero Logical; TRUE, treats NA weights zero (default: TRUE) stat_type Character string specifying statistic type: "mean": Population-weighted mean using exactextractr's weighted_mean function "median": Population-weighted median using matrixStats::weightedMedian() approach finds value 50% population weight lies 50% , interpolating pixel values needed "": Returns weighted mean weighted median statistics (default: "mean") pattern_info Optional. Pre-parsed time pattern detect_time_pattern(). NULL, detected filename. time_components Optional. Pre-parsed time components extract_time_components(). NULL, extracted filename.

title: Batch Extraction of Weighted Mean from Raster Using Population Weights — batch_extract_weighted_stats
text: Data frame id_cols population-weighted mean values

what: build a compact data dictionary — build_dictionary
title: build a compact data dictionary — build_dictionary
