title: build a compact data dictionary — build_dictionary
code: build_dictionary(   data,   labels_path = base::getOption("snt.labels_en_path", NULL),   language = NULL,   max_levels = 50L,   n_examples = 3L,   trans_cache_path = NULL,   override_yaml = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: build a compact data dictionary — build_dictionary
text: data data.frame tibble; sf columns allowed. labels_path optional csv columns like name,label override english labels. language optional iso code (e.g., "fr") add label_<language>. max_levels max factor levels summarize notes. default 50. n_examples number example values show. default 3. trans_cache_path optional cache dir translate_text_vec(). override_yaml logical; TRUE, CSV labels override YAML labels. default FALSE (YAML takes precedence). verbose logical; TRUE (default), prints info messages fuzzy match notifications.

title: build a compact data dictionary — build_dictionary
text: tibble : variable, type, label_en, n, n_missing, pct_missing, n_unique, example_values, min, max, notes, optionally label_<language>.

title: build a compact data dictionary — build_dictionary
text: english labels merged : internal defaults, csv overrides. unknown variables fall back column name. performance: snt variable tree cached package environment first use. subsequent calls reuse flattened tree. cache automatically refreshes tree version changes (tracked via _meta$last_updated).

title: build a compact data dictionary — build_dictionary
code: dd <- build_dictionary(dplyr::as_tibble(iris)) dd |> dplyr::select(variable, type, label_en) |> utils::head() #> # A tibble: 5 × 3 #>   variable     type   label_en     #>   <chr>        <chr>  <chr>        #> 1 Sepal.Length double Sepal.Length #> 2 Sepal.Width  double Sepal.Width  #> 3 Petal.Length double Petal.Length #> 4 Petal.Width  double Petal.Width  #> 5 Species      factor Species

what: Calculate and report geo-naming match statistics — calculate_match_stats
title: Calculate and report geo-naming match statistics — calculate_match_stats
text: Compares entries dataset lookup across specified admin levels (e.g., country, province/state/region, district, subdistrict, settlement) reports match statistics console.

title: Calculate and report geo-naming match statistics — calculate_match_stats
code: calculate_match_stats(   data,   lookup_data,   level0 = NULL,   level1 = NULL,   level2 = NULL,   level3 = NULL,   level4 = NULL )

title: Calculate and report geo-naming match statistics — calculate_match_stats
text: data data frame containing target data matched. lookup_data data frame serving reference matching. level0 Column name (country) present data lookup_data. level1 Column name (province/state/region) present datasets. level2 Column name (district) present datasets. level3 Column name (subdistrict) present datasets. level4 Column name (settlement) present datasets.

title: Calculate and report geo-naming match statistics — calculate_match_stats
text: Invisibly returns NULL. Output produced via cli messages.

title: Calculate and report geo-naming match statistics — calculate_match_stats
text: Input columns supplied via level* normalized lower case matching. Base N side count unique hierarchical names formed supplied levels (e.g., level0_level1_...). Per-level rows show matches side's Base N. Console output includes: two-column summary (Target vs Lookup base N), side-level completeness messages (success/info/warning), per-level report missing names (NA empty strings) either side. missing names included N implementation drops counting (see Note ).

title: Calculate and report geo-naming match statistics — calculate_match_stats
text: want missing names (NA/empty) excluded Base N, ensure key-building step drops counting (see example patch ).

title: Calculate and report geo-naming match statistics — calculate_match_stats
code: # minimal runnable example (toy data) data <- data.frame(   country = c("Kenya", "Kenya", "Uganda"),   district = c("Nairobi", "Kisumu", "Kampala") ) lookup <- data.frame(   country = c("Kenya", "Uganda"),   district = c("Nairobi", "Kampala") ) calculate_match_stats(   data, lookup, level0 = "country", level2 = "district" ) #>  #> ── ℹ Match Summary ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #>  #> ℹ Data has names not in lookup. #>  #> Target data as base N                                                        #> • country (level0): 2 out of 2 matched                                       #> • district (level2): 2 out of 3 matched                                      #> Lookup data as base N                                                        #> • country (level0): 2 out of 2 matched                                       #> • district (level2): 2 out of 2 matched

what: Calculate reporting/missing rate and proportion of reporting facilities — calculate_reporting_metrics
title: Calculate reporting/missing rate and proportion of reporting facilities — calculate_reporting_metrics
text: function calculates reporting metrics health facility data across three common scenarios. IMPORTANT: facility identification (hf_col) key_indicators provided, function excludes inactive facilities denominators, ensuring missing rates reflect proportion ACTIVE facilities failed report, facilities dataset.

title: Calculate reporting/missing rate and proportion of reporting facilities — calculate_reporting_metrics
code: calculate_reporting_metrics(   data,   vars_of_interest,   x_var,   y_var = NULL,   hf_col = NULL,   key_indicators = c("allout", "conf", "test", "treat", "pres"),   method = 3,   nonreport_window = 6,   reporting_rule = "any_non_na",   require_all = FALSE,   weighting = FALSE,   weight_var = NULL,   weight_window = 12,   exclude_current_x = TRUE,   cold_start = "median_within_y" )

title: Calculate reporting/missing rate and proportion of reporting facilities — calculate_reporting_metrics
text: data data frame containing health facility data. vars_of_interest Character vector variable names assess reporting (used numerator). x_var Character. Name primary grouping variable (e.g., time period). y_var Character. Optional. Name second grouping variable (e.g., district). hf_col Character. Optional (defaults NULL). Name column containing unique health facility IDs. provided, enables facility-level analysis filtering inactive facilities (key_indicators specified). Can used without y_var. Required weighting = TRUE. key_indicators Optional. Character vector indicators used define facility activity scenario 1. Defaults c("allout", "conf", "test", "treat", "pres"). method Character numeric. Classification method facility activity status. Can numeric (1, 2, 3) character ("method1", "method2", "method3"). Defaults 3. See classify_facility_activity details. nonreport_window Integer. Minimum number consecutive non-reporting months classify facility inactive method 3. Defaults 6. reporting_rule Character. Defines counts reporting: "any_non_na" (default, counts NA non-reporting, 0 counts reported) "positive_only" (requires >0 value count reported). require_all Logical. TRUE multiple vars_of_interest provided, calculates proportion facilities reporting variables (complete data). FALSE (default), calculates per-variable reporting rates. applies facility-level analysis (hf_col provided). weighting Logical. Whether use weighted reporting rates. TRUE, facilities weighted typical size, giving importance larger facilities overall reporting rate calculation. provides volume-adjusted measure data completeness. Default FALSE. weight_var Character. Name variable use proxy facility size (e.g., "allout" total outpatients, "test" tests done). count variable reflects facility activity/size. NULL weighting TRUE, auto-select allout, test, conf (order). weight_window Integer. Number periods rolling window calculate typical facility size. facility's weight based average size past weight_window periods. Larger windows provide stable weights may miss recent changes. Default 12. exclude_current_x Logical. Whether exclude current period calculating weights. TRUE, prevents current reporting influencing weight (avoids circularity). Default TRUE. cold_start Character. Method handling facilities insufficient history (< weight_window periods). Options: "median_within_y" (default): Uses median size facilities within y_var group (e.g., district) "median_global": Uses median size across facilities

title: Calculate reporting/missing rate and proportion of reporting facilities — calculate_reporting_metrics
text: tibble number reporting (rep) expected (exp) facilities records, computed reprate missrate. Understanding metrics: exp (expected): hf_col provided key_indicators, count ACTIVE facilities . Without hf_col, count data rows (including inactive facilities). rep (reported): Count facilities/rows reported (non-NA values). reprate: Reporting rate (rep/exp). proper facility identification, percentage ACTIVE facilities reported. missrate: Missing rate (1 - reprate). proper facility identification, percentage ACTIVE facilities failed report. Without hf_col, may artificially high due inactive facilities. weighting TRUE, additional columns included: reprate_w: Weighted reporting rate (0-1) missrate_w: Weighted missing rate (0-1) avg_<weight_var>: Average raw value weight variable (e.g., avg_allout) min_<weight_var>: Minimum raw value weight variable max_<weight_var>: Maximum raw value weight variable

title: Calculate reporting/missing rate and proportion of reporting facilities — calculate_reporting_metrics
text: Proportion facilities reporting single variable vars_of_interest contains single variable: Calculates proportion active facilities (defined reporting key_indicators) reported variable given period. Requires hf_col. Can used without y_var. Per-variable facility reporting rates vars_of_interest contains multiple variables hf_col provided: Calculates reporting/missing rates variable separately, counting distinct facilities reported specific variable. Uses facility-level analysis, filtering active facilities (key_indicators specified). Can used without y_var. Row-level reporting rates (without facility filtering) hf_col provided: Computes reporting/missing rates based data rows, facilities. WARNING: counts data rows including inactive facilities, potentially inflating missing rates. Use scenarios 1 2 (hf_col) accurate facility-based metrics.

title: Calculate reporting/missing rate and proportion of reporting facilities — calculate_reporting_metrics
text: weighting = TRUE, function calculates volume-adjusted reporting rates give importance larger facilities. useful want overall reporting rate reflect proportion patient visits services covered rather just proportion facilities. weighting algorithm works follows: Calculate typical facility size: facility, compute rolling mean weight_var past weight_window periods. represents facility's typical size/volume. Handle cold starts: facilities insufficient history: cold_start = "median_within_y": Use median typical size facilities group (y_var) cold_start = "median_global": Use overall median typical size Normalize weights: Within time period group, weights normalized sum 1. ensures larger facilities get proportionally weight. Calculate weighted rates: reprate_w = sum(weight * reported) / sum(weight) missrate_w = 1 - reprate_w

title: Calculate reporting/missing rate and proportion of reporting facilities — calculate_reporting_metrics
text: district 10 facilities : 3 large facilities (80% patient volume) report 7 small facilities (20% patient volume) 4 reporting : Unweighted reporting rate = 7/10 = 70% Weighted reporting rate \u2248 84% (reflecting patient volume covered)

title: Calculate reporting/missing rate and proportion of reporting facilities — calculate_reporting_metrics
code: # Example with dates instead of month names for compatibility hf_data <- data.frame(   month = rep(as.Date(c("2024-01-01", "2024-02-01", "2024-03-01")), each = 10),   district = rep(c("North", "South"), each = 5, times = 3),   facility_id = rep(1:5, times = 6),   conf = c(     10, 0, 15, NA, 8, 12, 0, NA, 7, 9,     11, 0, 14, 6, NA, 13, 8, 10, 0, 12,     9, 7, 0, 11, 14, 8, NA, 12, 10, 15   ),   pres = c(     5, 0, NA, 7, 3, 6, 0, 4, NA, 2,     8, 0, 6, NA, 4, 7, 3, 0, 5, 6,     4, 0, 7, 5, NA, 6, 0, 8, 4, 3   ),   allout = c(     5, 0, NA, 7, 3, 6, 0, 4, NA, 2,     8, 0, 6, NA, 4, 7, 3, 0, 5, 6,     4, 0, 7, 5, NA, 6, 0, 8, 4, 3   ) )  # Scenario 1: Proportion of active facilities reporting # any data (using numeric method) calculate_reporting_metrics(   data = hf_data,   vars_of_interest = c("conf"),   x_var = "month",   y_var = "district",   hf_col = "facility_id",   key_indicators = c("allout", "conf", "pres"),   method = 3  # Can also use "method3" ) #> ℹ Facility activity classification: #>   5 of 5 facilities are active (100%) #>   0 facilities are inactive (0%) #> ✔ Returning data filtered to 5 active facilities (of 5 total) #> # A tibble: 6 × 6 #>   month      district   rep   exp reprate missrate #>   <date>     <chr>    <int> <int>   <dbl>    <dbl> #> 1 2024-01-01 North        4     5     0.8      0.2 #> 2 2024-01-01 South        4     5     0.8      0.2 #> 3 2024-02-01 North        4     5     0.8      0.2 #> 4 2024-02-01 South        5     5     1        0   #> 5 2024-03-01 North        5     5     1        0   #> 6 2024-03-01 South        4     5     0.8      0.2  # Scenario 2: Reporting rate by month and district calculate_reporting_metrics(   data = hf_data,   vars_of_interest = c("conf"),   x_var = "month",   y_var = "district",   hf_col = NULL ) #> Warning: ! No facility identification column (hf_col) provided. #> ℹ Unable to exclude inactive facilities from denominators. #> ℹ This may result in inaccurate rates if inactive facilities are present. #> ℹ Consider providing 'hf_col' parameter to enable facility activity #>   classification. #> # A tibble: 6 × 7 #>   month      district variable   exp   rep reprate missrate #>   <date>     <chr>    <fct>    <int> <int>   <dbl>    <dbl> #> 1 2024-01-01 North    conf         5     4     0.8      0.2 #> 2 2024-01-01 South    conf         5     4     0.8      0.2 #> 3 2024-02-01 North    conf         5     4     0.8      0.2 #> 4 2024-02-01 South    conf         5     5     1        0   #> 5 2024-03-01 North    conf         5     5     1        0   #> 6 2024-03-01 South    conf         5     4     0.8      0.2  # Scenario 3: Reporting trends over time calculate_reporting_metrics(   data = hf_data,   vars_of_interest = c("conf"),   x_var = "month",   hf_col = NULL ) #> Warning: ! No facility identification column (hf_col) provided. #> ℹ Unable to exclude inactive facilities from denominators. #> ℹ This may result in inaccurate rates if inactive facilities are present. #> ℹ Consider providing 'hf_col' and 'y_var' parameters to enable facility #>   activity classification. #> # A tibble: 3 × 6 #>   month      variable   exp   rep reprate missrate #>   <date>     <fct>    <int> <int>   <dbl>    <dbl> #> 1 2024-01-01 conf        10     8     0.8      0.2 #> 2 2024-02-01 conf        10     9     0.9      0.1 #> 3 2024-03-01 conf        10     9     0.9      0.1   # Example with weighted reporting rate # Create data with facilities of different sizes weighted_data <- data.frame(   month = rep(1:6, each = 5),   district = rep("A", 30),   hf_id = rep(c("Large1", "Large2", "Small1", "Small2", "Small3"), 6),   # Large facilities see ~1000 patients, small ones ~100   allout = c(     # Month 1-3: Historical data for weight calculation     1050, 980, 95, 110, 105,  # Month 1     1100, 1020, 100, 98, 112,  # Month 2     990, 1080, 105, 102, 108,  # Month 3     # Month 4-6: Current periods     1070, 1050, 98, 105, 110,  # Month 4     1020, 990, 102, 108, 95,   # Month 5     1100, 1030, 110, 100, 105  # Month 6   ),   # Reporting pattern: large facilities always report, small ones sporadic   malaria = c(     # Month 1-3     50, 48, 5, NA, 6,    # Month 1     55, 51, NA, 4, NA,   # Month 2     49, 54, 6, NA, 5,    # Month 3     # Month 4-6     52, 53, NA, NA, 6,   # Month 4     51, 49, 5, 6, NA,    # Month 5     54, 52, NA, 5, NA    # Month 6   ) )  # Compare unweighted vs weighted reporting rates unweighted_result <- calculate_reporting_metrics(   data = weighted_data,   vars_of_interest = "malaria",   x_var = "month",   y_var = "district",   hf_col = "hf_id",   weighting = FALSE ) #> Warning: ! Some key indicators not found in data #> ℹ Using only: allout #> ℹ Facility activity classification: #>   5 of 10 facilities are active (50%) #>   5 facilities are inactive (50%) #> ✔ Returning data filtered to 5 active facilities (of 10 total)  weighted_result <- calculate_reporting_metrics(   data = weighted_data,   vars_of_interest = "malaria",   x_var = "month",   y_var = "district",   hf_col = "hf_id",   weighting = TRUE,   weight_var = "allout",   weight_window = 3,   exclude_current_x = TRUE ) #> Warning: ! >25% cold starts in 1 periods #> ℹ Consider adjusting weight_window or data range #> Warning: ! Some key indicators not found in data #> ℹ Using only: allout #> ℹ Facility activity classification: #>   5 of 10 facilities are active (50%) #>   5 facilities are inactive (50%) #> ✔ Returning data filtered to 5 active facilities (of 10 total)  # Unweighted: counts facilities equally (e.g., 3/5 = 60%) # Weighted: reflects patient volume covered (e.g., ~88% if large facilities report)

title: Calculate reporting metrics based on facility date ranges — calculate_reporting_metrics_dates
code: # Sample facility data with operating dates facilities <- data.frame(   hf_uid = rep(1:10, each = 12),   date = rep(seq.Date(     as.Date("2024-01-01"),     by = "month",     length.out = 12   ), times = 10),   district = rep(c("North", "South"), each = 60),   opening_date = as.Date("2023-01-01"),   closing_date = as.Date("2024-12-31") )  # Calculate with default grouping (adm2) facilities$adm2 <- facilities$district result <- calculate_reporting_metrics_dates(   data = facilities,   start_col = "opening_date",   end_col = "closing_date" )  # Calculate with multiple grouping levels facilities$country <- "Example" result2 <- calculate_reporting_metrics_dates(   data = facilities,   start_col = "opening_date",   end_col = "closing_date",   group_cols = c("country", "district") )  # Use custom record column facilities$report_period <- facilities$date result3 <- calculate_reporting_metrics_dates(   data = facilities,   start_col = "opening_date",   end_col = "closing_date",   record_col = "report_period",   group_cols = "district" )

title: Classify health facility activity status by reporting behaviour — classify_facility_activity
code: classify_facility_activity(   data,   hf_col,   date_col = "date",   key_indicators = c("test", "pres", "conf"),   method = 1,   nonreport_window = 6,   reporting_rule = "any_non_na",   binary_classification = FALSE )

title: Classify health facility activity status by reporting behaviour — classify_facility_activity
text: data Data frame containing routine health facility records. hf_col Character. Column storing health facility identifiers. date_col Character. Column storing observation dates. Defaults "date". key_indicators Character vector columns defining reporting activity. Defaults c("test", "pres", "conf"). method Character numeric. Classification method - can numeric (1, 2, 3) character ("method1", "method2", "method3", ""). nonreport_window Integer. Minimum number consecutive non-reporting months classify facility inactive method 3. Defaults 6. reporting_rule Character. Defines counts reporting: "any_non_na" (default, counts NA non-reporting, 0 counts reported) "positive_only" (requires >0 value count reported). binary_classification Logical. TRUE, collapses categories "Active" vs "Inactive". Defaults FALSE.

title: Classify health facility activity status by reporting behaviour — classify_facility_activity
text: Data frame original columns plus reporting activity status. method = "", includes three activity classification columns.

title: Classify health facility activity status by reporting behaviour — classify_facility_activity
text: Three activity classification methods supported: Method 1 (Permanent activation): Facility active first report onwards, remains active afterwards. Method 2 (First-Last activation): Facility active first last report dates. Method 3 (Dynamic): Facility active, becomes inactive misses nonreport_window consecutive months. Reactivates reporting resumes. reporting_rule parameter controls reporting flagged: "any_non_na": non-missing value counts reported (including 0). "positive_only": values strictly >0 count reported.

title: Consistency Check Function — consistency_check
text: malaria surveillance data, function validates logical coherence care cascade ensuring upstream events (inputs) greater equal downstream events (outputs). Common malaria data validation checks include: Common malaria variable definitions: allout = outpatients susp = suspected malaria cases test = malaria tests (tested malaria) conf = confirmed malaria cases maltreat = malaria cases treated alladm = admissions (hospital admissions) maladm = malaria admissions (hospital admissions malaria) alldth = deaths (total deaths) maldth = malaria deaths Typical logical validation checks: susp vs test: suspected malaria >= malaria tests allout vs susp: outpatients >= suspected malaria allout vs test: outpatients >= malaria tests test vs conf: malaria tests >= confirmed malaria cases conf vs maltreat: confirmed malaria cases >= malaria treated alladm vs maladm: admissions >= malaria admissions alldth vs maldth: deaths >= malaria deaths maladm vs maldth: malaria admissions >= malaria deaths

title: Consistency Check Function — consistency_check
code: # Basic consistency checks for malaria surveillance data if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # get path path <- system.file(   "extdata",   "fake_epi_df_togo.rds",   package = "epiCleanr" )  # get example data fake_epi_df_togo <- sntutils::read(path)  # Example 1: Check test-to-confirmation cascade # Validates: malaria tests >= confirmed malaria cases consistency_check(   fake_epi_df_togo,   inputs = c("malaria_tests"),   outputs = c("malaria_cases") )  # Example 2: Multiple validation checks at once # Checks common malaria care cascade validations consistency_check(   fake_epi_df_togo,   inputs = c("test", "allout", "conf", "alladm", "alldth", "maladm"),   outputs = c("conf", "susp", "maltreat", "maladm", "maldth", "maldth") )  # Example 3: Save the plot to disk consistency_check(   fake_epi_df_togo,   inputs = c("malaria_tests", "all_outpatients"),   outputs = c("malaria_cases", "suspected_malaria"),   plot_path = tempdir() )  # Example 4: Validate with multiple diseases consistency_check(   fake_epi_df_togo,   inputs = c("malaria_tests", "cholera_tests"),   outputs = c("malaria_cases", "cholera_cases") )  # Example 5: Facet by year for time-series analysis (single pair only) consistency_check(   fake_epi_df_togo,   inputs = c("malaria_tests"),   outputs = c("malaria_cases"),   facet_by = "year" )  # Example 6: Facet by administrative unit (single pair only) consistency_check(   fake_epi_df_togo,   inputs = c("test"),   outputs = c("conf"),   facet_by = "adm1" )  # Old syntax (deprecated but still supported for backward # compatibility) consistency_check(   fake_epi_df_togo,   tests = c("malaria_tests"),   cases = c("malaria_cases") ) } # }

title: Create an area-weighted crosswalk between old and new admin polygons — crosswalk_shapefiles_sf
text: old_sf sf object. Old administrative boundaries. Must contain columns adm0, adm1, adm2 EPSG:4326. new_sf sf object. New administrative boundaries. Must contain columns adm0, adm1, adm2 EPSG:4326. level character. One "adm0", "adm1", "adm2". Determines administrative level crosswalked. old_suffix character. Suffix appended old admin names. min_weight numeric. Minimum old--new area proportion retain. area_crs integer. EPSG code planar equal-area CRS. keep_overlap_geometry logical. TRUE, include overlap geometries. min_primary_weight numeric. Minimum weight label primary. min_secondary_weight numeric. Minimum weight secondary overlaps. coverage_tol numeric. Tolerance area coverage diagnostics. verbose logical. TRUE, print CLI progress messages. include_areas logical. TRUE, include area columns km2. include_reverse_prop logical. TRUE, include new--old proportion. Outputs Returns list : data: data.frame crosswalk mappings diagnostics dictionary: data.frame describing column metadata: data.frame run parameters timestamps overlap_geometry: (keep_overlap_geometry = TRUE) sf object Key columns data output overlap_prop: proportion old unit area overlapping new unit is_primary: TRUE dominant mapping old unit (overlap_prop >= 0.5) n_new_adm: number new units overlapping old unit split_flag: TRUE old unit overlaps multiple new units n_old_per_new: number old units overlapping new unit merge_flag: TRUE multiple old units merged new unit Assumptions Areas computed planar CRS. Attributes (admin names) spatially constant within polygons. Weights area-based, population-weighted.

what: Detect outliers with guardrails and consensus — detect_outliers
title: Detect outliers with guardrails and consensus — detect_outliers
text: detect_outliers() evaluates numeric indicator administrative unit time using three methods (mean + SD, median + MAD, Tukey upper fence) identify unusually HIGH values (potential outbreaks data anomalies). Low values flagged outliers. enforces reporting sample-size guardrails, supports strictness presets. Optional outbreak classification distinguishes isolated outliers sustained outbreak patterns using gap-aware clustering can bridge short interruptions outbreak signal. returns per-method flags (optional), consensus classification, scale statistics, metadata.

title: Detect outliers with guardrails and consensus — detect_outliers
code: detect_outliers(   data,   column,   record_id = "record_id",   admin_level = c("adm1", "adm2"),   spatial_level = "hf_uid",   date = "date",   time_mode = c("across_time", "within_year", "seasonal"),   value_type = c("count", "rate"),   strictness = c("balanced", "lenient", "strict", "advanced"),   methods = c("iqr", "median", "mean", "consensus"),   sd_multiplier = 3,   mad_constant = 1.4826,   mad_multiplier = 9,   iqr_multiplier = 2,   consensus_rule = 3,   output_profile = c("standard", "lean", "audit"),   min_n = 8,   reporting_rate_col = NULL,   reporting_rate_min = 0.5,   key_indicators_hf = NULL,   classify_outbreaks = FALSE,   outbreak_min_run = 2,   outbreak_prop_tolerance = 0.9,   outbreak_max_gap = 12,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Detect outliers with guardrails and consensus — detect_outliers
text: data Data frame containing indicator analyse. column Name numeric column evaluate. record_id Unique record identifier column. admin_level Character vector administrative level columns parallel grouping, ordered higher lower resolution. Defaults c("adm1", "adm2"). spatial_level Character string specifying finest spatial unit analysis (e.g., "hf_uid" facility-level). specified, admin_level defines grouping boundaries spatial_level defines unit analysis. prevents excessive grouping maintaining spatial granularity. Default hf_uid. date Date column (Date, POSIXt, parseable character string). Year, month, yearmon automatically derived column. time_mode Pooling strategy: "across_time", "within_year", "seasonal". Seasonal mode groups month across years (e.g., Januaries together), useful detecting values unusual specific month regardless year. value_type Indicator type: "count" "rate". Counts floor lower bounds 0. strictness Strictness preset: "lenient", "balanced", "strict", "advanced". Presets map method multipliers. "advanced", manual multipliers ignored. methods Character vector specifying outlier detection methods use: "iqr" (Interquartile Range), "median" (Median Absolute Deviation), "mean" (Mean +/- SD), /"consensus". Default c("iqr", "median", "mean", "consensus"). consensus, least two methods must selected. sd_multiplier Width (SD units) mean method (used strictness = "advanced"). mad_constant Constant passed stats::mad() advanced mode (default 1.4826). mad_multiplier Width multiplier MAD method (advanced mode). iqr_multiplier Tukey fence multiplier IQR method (advanced mode). consensus_rule Number methods must agree (1, 2, 3) consensus flag call outlier. Default 2. output_profile Controls amount detail returned: "lean" (minimal columns: id, admin, date, value, consensus flag, reason), "standard" (lean + per-method flags + bounds + seasonality mode), "audit" (columns full reproducibility). Default "standard". min_n Minimum observations required active comparison bucket flagging attempted (applies seasonal bucket fallback). reporting_rate_col Optional column reporting completeness [0, 1]. reporting_rate_min Minimum acceptable reporting rate. Rows threshold receive reason = "low_reporting" flagged. key_indicators_hf Optional character vector indicator names used determine facility activeness. supplied, function uses fast path filter inactive facility-months. facility-month considered active specified key indicators non-NA values. Inactive facility-months excluded outlier detection. NULL (default), activeness filtering skipped. Typical indicators include "allout", "test", "conf". adjustment prevents false positives caused facilities start stop reporting mid-period. classify_outbreaks Logical. TRUE (default), applies outbreak classification distinguish isolated outliers sustained outbreak patterns. Consecutive outliers meeting outbreak criteria reclassified "outlier" "outbreak". particularly useful epidemiological surveillance identify disease outbreak patterns. Set FALSE disable outbreak classification. outbreak_min_run Integer. Minimum number consecutive outliers required classify outbreak (default 2). Must >= 2. outbreak_prop_tolerance Numeric. Proportional tolerance outbreak consistency (default 0.9). Values within tolerance run median considered consistent. Range: (0, 1). outbreak_max_gap Integer. Maximum allowed gap (non-outlier months) outliers can still considered part outbreak (default 1). example, outbreak_max_gap = 12, pattern "outlier-normal-outlier-outlier" classified one outbreak length 3, rather separate incidents. Set 0 strict consecutive-outbreaks. Useful real-world data reporting gaps. verbose Logical. TRUE, prints informative summary showing methods applied, pooling strategy, strictness settings, guardrails, consensus rule. Default FALSE.

title: Detect outliers with guardrails and consensus — detect_outliers
text: Tibble outlier classifications metadata. Columns include: identifiers (record_id, admin levels, yearmon, year, derived month), column_name, value, value_type, scale stats (mean, sd, median, mad, q1, q3, iqr), method bounds, n_in_group, guardrail reason, method flags (optional), outlier_flag_consensus, strictness multipliers, (activeness filtering applied) activeness_applied key_indicators_used. Outlier flag categories simplified three intuitive groups: "normal": Values within expected statistical bounds "outlier": Values exceeding thresholds (potential anomalies/outbreaks) "insufficient_data": Various data quality issues preventing determination (consolidates insufficient_n, insufficient_evidence, unstable_scale, etc.)

title: Detect outliers with guardrails and consensus — detect_outliers
text: Workflow Validation & prep: confirm required columns, coerce target numeric safely, derive month yearmon. Activeness filtering (key_indicators_hf supplied): call classify_facility_activity() tag inactive facilities. Inactive facility-months excluded detection assigned reason = "inactive_facility". Strictness: presets map (SD, MAD, IQR) multipliers; advanced mode honours manual multipliers. across-time fallback strictness shifts one step toward lenient. Guardrails: rows failing reporting_rate_min min_n bypass flagging record reason (low_reporting, insufficient_n, insufficient_data). Flagging: method checks values exceed UPPER threshold (e.g., mean + multiplier x SD). Low values never flagged. Methods suppressed scales unstable (sd, mad, iqr equals zero). Consensus: final outlier_flag_consensus requires least consensus_rule methods agree available (non-suppressed) methods. Facility activeness adjustment inactive newly activated health facilities included aggregated totals, apparent spikes dips can occur represent real epidemiological changes. example, ten new facilities start reporting Matoto mid-2022, total number confirmed cases rises even incidence per facility remains constant. prevent artefacts, detect_outliers() uses fast, optimized approach activeness filtering. key_indicators_hf specified, function checks facility-month non-NA values specified key indicators. facility-months least one reported key indicator contribute comparison pool outlier detection. lightweight approach avoids computational overhead full activity classification still preventing false positives inactive facilities. Presets (high outliers ) lenient: values > mean + 4.0 x SD, median + 12 x MAD, Q3 + 3.0 x IQR balanced: values > mean + 3.0 x SD, median + 9 x MAD, Q3 + 2.0 x IQR strict: values > mean + 2.5 x SD, median + 6 x MAD, Q3 + 1.5 x IQR advanced: use user-supplied multipliers Note: values upper thresholds flagged. Low values always classified "normal" regardless far mean/median. returned tibble always contains identifiers, scale statistics, bounds, strictness metadata, guardrail reason. output_profile = "standard" "audit", method-specific flags included alongside consensus.

title: Detect outliers with guardrails and consensus — detect_outliers
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # NOTE: Only HIGH values are flagged as outliers (e.g., disease outbreaks). # Low values are always considered "normal".  # 1) Minimal consensus output at adm1-only level detect_outliers(   data = malaria_data,   column = "confirmed_cases",   date = "date",   record_id = "facility_id",   admin_level = c("adm1"),        # ignore adm2 if not present   time_mode = "across_time" )  # 2) Within-year comparison detect_outliers(   data = malaria_data,   column = "confirmed_cases",   date = "date",   admin_level = c("adm1"),   time_mode = "within_year",   consensus_rule = 2,   output_profile = "audit" )  # 3) Advanced overrides for rates detect_outliers(   data = malaria_data,   column = "positivity_rate",   date = "date",   value_type = "rate",   strictness = "advanced",   sd_multiplier = 2.5,   mad_multiplier = 7,   iqr_multiplier = 1.8,   min_n = 10,   output_profile = "audit" )  # 4) With facility activeness filtering detect_outliers(   data = malaria_data,   column = "conf",   date = "date",   admin_level = c("adm1", "adm2"),   time_mode = "across_time",   key_indicators_hf = c("allout", "test", "conf") )  # 5) With binary activeness classification detect_outliers(   data = malaria_data,   column = "conf",   date = "date",   admin_level = c("adm1", "adm2"),   time_mode = "across_time",   key_indicators_hf = c("allout", "test", "conf") )  # 6) Seasonal comparison (same month across years) # Compares all Januaries together, all Februaries together, etc. # Useful for detecting values unusual for a specific month detect_outliers(   data = malaria_data,   column = "conf",   date = "date",   admin_level = c("adm1", "adm2"),   time_mode = "seasonal" ) } # }

what: Download ERA5 Data from Copernicus CDS — download_era5
title: Download ERA5 Data from Copernicus CDS — download_era5
text: Downloads ERA5 data Copernicus Climate Data Store (CDS) using ecmwfr package. Supports daily monthly pre-aggregated products. Requires CDS API credentials.

title: Download ERA5 Data from Copernicus CDS — download_era5
code: download_era5(   dataset,   cds_key = Sys.getenv("ERA5_API_KEY"),   years,   months,   variables,   daily_stat = c("mean", "maximum", "minimum", "sum"),   daily_hfreq = c("1_hourly", "3_hourly", "6_hourly"),   daily_tz = "UTC+00:00",   hours = 0:23,   pressure_levels = NULL,   bbox = NULL,   out_dir = "era5_data",   overwrite = FALSE,   aggregated_dir = NULL,   aggregated_format = "qs2" )

title: Download ERA5 Data from Copernicus CDS — download_era5
text: dataset Character. One dataset codes listed era5_options(). cds_key Character. CDS API credentials. Defaults Sys.getenv("ERA5_API_KEY"). new CDS (ecmwfr >= 2.0.0), use just Personal Access Token (PAT). old CDS, use "uid:apikey" format. Get https://cds.climate.copernicus.eu/profile years Integer vector. Years download (e.g., 2020:2022). months Integer vector. Months download (1-12). variables Character vector. CDS variable names (e.g., c("2m_temperature", "total_precipitation")). daily_stat Character. daily dataset: "mean", "maximum", "minimum", "sum". Note: daily statistics dataset can unreliable. Consider using hourly data instead. daily_hfreq Character. daily dataset: "1_hourly", "3_hourly", "6_hourly". daily_tz Character. Time zone daily data (e.g., "UTC+00:00"). hours Integer vector. Hours download hourly datasets (0-23). Default hours. pressure_levels Integer vector. Pressure levels hPa pressure level data (e.g., c(1000, 850, 500)). bbox Numeric vector. Bounding box c(xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax) lon/lat. out_dir Character. Directory path files saved. overwrite Logical. TRUE, overwrite existing files. aggregated_dir Character. Optional directory save aggregated data files. NULL (default), aggregation performed. specified, downloads read aggregated variable, filenames like "era5_precip_Jan2020-Dec2024_v20250919.qs2" aggregated_format Character. Format aggregated files: "qs2" (default), "parquet", "feather", "rds", "csv"

title: Download ERA5 Data from Copernicus CDS — download_era5
text: tibble download information including file paths status.

title: Download ERA5 Data from Copernicus CDS — download_era5
text: Use era5_options() view available datasets metadata.

title: Download ERA5 Data from Copernicus CDS — download_era5
code: # View available datasets era5_options() #> # A tibble: 4 × 6 #>   dataset                label       cds_id product_type common_vars description #>   <chr>                  <chr>       <chr>  <chr>        <chr>       <chr>       #> 1 daily_single_levels    Daily Stat… deriv… NA           2m_tempera… Pre-aggreg… #> 2 monthly_single_levels  Monthly Me… reana… monthly_ave… 2m_tempera… Pre-aggreg… #> 3 hourly_single_levels   Hourly Dat… reana… reanalysis   2m_tempera… Hourly atm… #> 4 hourly_pressure_levels Hourly Dat… reana… reanalysis   temperatur… Hourly upp…  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Download monthly temperature data (uses ERA5_API_KEY env var) download_era5(   dataset = "monthly_single_levels",   years = 2020,   months = 1:3,   variables = c("2m_temperature"),   bbox = c(29.0, -4.5, 30.9, -2.3),   out_dir = "era5_data" )  # Download daily precipitation totals download_era5(   dataset = "daily_single_levels",   years = 2020,   months = 1,   variables = c("total_precipitation"),   daily_stat = "sum",   daily_hfreq = "1_hourly",   daily_tz = "UTC+00:00",   bbox = c(29.0, -4.5, 30.9, -2.3),   out_dir = "era5_data" )  # Download hourly temperature and wind data download_era5(   dataset = "hourly_single_levels",   years = 2020,   months = 1,   variables = c(     "2m_temperature",     "10m_u_component_of_wind",     "10m_v_component_of_wind"   ),   hours = c(0, 6, 12, 18),  # Every 6 hours   bbox = c(29.0, -4.5, 30.9, -2.3),   out_dir = "era5_data" )  # Download pressure level data download_era5(   dataset = "hourly_pressure_levels",   years = 2020,   months = 1,   variables = c("temperature", "relative_humidity"),   pressure_levels = c(1000, 850, 500),  # Surface, 850hPa, 500hPa   hours = c(0, 12),  # Twice daily   bbox = c(29.0, -4.5, 30.9, -2.3),   out_dir = "era5_data" ) } # }

what: Download MODIS Data from NASA — download_modis
title: Download MODIS Data from NASA — download_modis
text: Searches NASA's CMR catalog, downloads MODIS HDF files via httr2, extracts requested band, mosaics tiles, crops area interest, scales values, saves one GeoTIFF per time step. Output files named modis_{band}_{YYYY}_{MM}.tif work directly process_raster_collection().

title: Download MODIS Data from NASA — download_modis
code: download_modis(   shapefile,   start,   end,   product = "MOD13A3",   band = "1 km monthly EVI",   out_dir = ".",   username = NULL,   password = NULL,   version = "061",   scale_factor = "auto",   keep_hdf = FALSE,   overwrite = FALSE )

title: Download MODIS Data from NASA — download_modis
text: shapefile sf object defining area interest. Reprojected WGS84 (EPSG:4326) NASA query. start Character. Start date common format (e.g. "2023-01-01"). end Character. End date common format. product Character. MODIS product short name (default "MOD13A3" monthly vegetation indices 1 km). band Character. HDF subdataset name extract (default "1 km monthly EVI"). Use terra::describe(hdf_file) downloaded HDF see available subdatasets. out_dir Character. Output directory GeoTIFFs (default "."). Created exist. username Character NULL. NASA Earthdata username. Falls back Sys.getenv("EARTHDATA_USERNAME"). password Character NULL. NASA Earthdata password. Falls back Sys.getenv("EARTHDATA_PASSWORD"). version Character. MODIS collection version (default "061"). scale_factor "auto", numeric, NULL. "auto" (default), scale factor looked built-table known MODIS bands (e.g. 0.0001 EVI/NDVI, 0.02 LST). Pass number override, NULL skip scaling. keep_hdf Logical. Keep raw HDF files processing? Default FALSE (deletes ). TRUE, saved {out_dir}/hdf/. overwrite Logical. Re-download reprocess existing files? Default FALSE.

title: Download MODIS Data from NASA — download_modis
text: Invisible character vector output GeoTIFF paths.

title: Download MODIS Data from NASA — download_modis
text: Use modis_options() browse available products.

title: Download MODIS Data from NASA — download_modis
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ boundary <- sf::st_read("guinea_adm0.geojson")  # download monthly EVI for 2023 paths <- download_modis(   shapefile = boundary,   start     = "2023-01-01",   end       = "2023-12-31",   out_dir   = "data/evi",   username  = "my_user",   password  = "my_pass" )  # then use the existing raster processing pipeline result <- process_raster_collection(   directory    = "data/evi",   shapefile    = sf::st_read("guinea_adm3.geojson"),   id_cols      = c("adm0", "adm1", "adm2", "adm3"),   aggregations = "mean" )  # download NDVI instead download_modis(   shapefile = boundary,   start     = "2023-01-01",   end       = "2023-12-31",   band      = "1 km monthly NDVI",   out_dir   = "data/ndvi" )  # keep raw HDF files for inspection download_modis(   shapefile = boundary,   start     = "2023-01-01",   end       = "2023-12-31",   out_dir   = "data/evi",   keep_hdf  = TRUE ) } # }

what: Download and process NASA POWER daily climate data — download_process_nasapower
title: Download and process NASA POWER daily climate data — download_process_nasapower
text: Downloads daily climate variables NASA POWER API representative administrative units, processes daily monthly datasets, generates data dictionaries output.

title: Download and process NASA POWER daily climate data — download_process_nasapower
code: download_process_nasapower(   adm_sf,   admin_cols = c("adm0", "adm1", "adm2"),   start_date = "2025-01-01",   end_date = "2025-12-31",   power_vars = c("PRECTOTCORR", "T2M_MAX", "T2M_MIN", "T2M", "TS", "TS_MAX", "TS_MIN",     "RH2M", "T2MDEW"),   power_community = "ag",   max_retries = 3,   dict_language = "fr" )

title: Download and process NASA POWER daily climate data — download_process_nasapower
text: adm_sf sf object containing administrative polygons. Must include columns specified admin_cols valid CRS. admin_cols Character vector administrative identifier columns, ordered highest lowest level. last column treated unit identifier. Default c("adm0", "adm1", "adm2"). start_date Start date "YYYY-MM-DD" format. end_date End date "YYYY-MM-DD" format. power_vars Character vector NASA POWER variable codes download. Drop code exclude variable outputs; add code opt . Codes recognised rename map (clean column name parentheses): PRECTOTCORR (rainfall_mm), T2M (air_temperature_c), T2M_MAX/T2M_MIN (max/min_air_temperature_c), TS (land_temperature_c), TS_MAX/TS_MIN (max/min_land_temperature_c), RH2M (humidity_pct), T2MDEW (dewpoint_c), T2MWET (wet_bulb_temperature_c), QV2M (specific_humidity_gkg), PS (surface_pressure_kpa), WS2M/WS2M_MAX/WS2M_MIN (wind_speed_ms), ALLSKY_SFC_SW_DWN (solar_radiation_kwhm2), CLOUD_AMT (cloud_amount_pct), GWETTOP (surface_soil_wetness), GWETROOT (root_soil_wetness), GWETPROF (profile_soil_wetness). Unrecognised codes downloaded dropped cleaned daily/monthly outputs. power_community NASA POWER community parameter. Default "ag" (agroclimatology). max_retries Maximum retry attempts per unit. Default 3. dict_language Language data dictionaries. Default "fr".

title: Download and process NASA POWER daily climate data — download_process_nasapower
text: list four elements: daily Tibble daily climate data admin columns plus elevation_m column (MERRA-2 grid-cell elevation, static per polygon, parsed POWER response metadata). monthly Tibble monthly aggregated climate data. requested variable: mean_, median_, min_, max_ versions; rainfall additionally gets total_. elevation_m carried static column. dict_daily Data dictionary daily data dict_monthly Data dictionary monthly data

title: Download and process NASA POWER daily climate data — download_process_nasapower
text: polygon, single representative point (centroid) used download data. Returned values correspond NASA POWER grid-cell averages (approximately 0.5 degree resolution) interpreted regional conditions rather point measurements. function robust partial download failures. Administrative units fail download specified number retries skipped warning. Execution stops units return data.

title: Download and process NASA POWER daily climate data — download_process_nasapower
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # load administrative boundaries adm_sf <- sf::st_read("path/to/admin_boundaries.shp")  # download NASA POWER data for 2024 result <- download_process_nasapower(   adm_sf = adm_sf,   admin_cols = c("adm0", "adm1", "adm2"),   start_date = "2024-01-01",   end_date = "2024-12-31" )  # access daily and monthly data daily_data <- result$daily monthly_data <- result$monthly } # }

what: Download WHO Administrative Boundaries with Partial Update — download_shapefile
title: Download WHO Administrative Boundaries with Partial Update — download_shapefile
text: Downloads administrative boundaries ArcGIS services specified countries administrative levels. function supports incremental updates, downloading missing data appending existing files. automatically converts date fields milliseconds proper Date format can filter current boundaries include historical boundaries.

title: Download WHO Administrative Boundaries with Partial Update — download_shapefile
code: download_shapefile(   country_codes,   admin_level = "ADM2",   latest = TRUE,   dest_path = NULL )

title: Download WHO Administrative Boundaries with Partial Update — download_shapefile
text: country_codes Character vector ISO3 country codes (e.g. c("KEN","UGA")). admin_level Character string specifying administrative level ("ADM0", "ADM1", "ADM2"). Default "ADM2". latest Logical. TRUE (default), returns latest/active boundaries. FALSE, returns historical boundaries. dest_path File path data saved. NULL (default), data returned without saving disk. latest = TRUE, output filename include suffix _latest extension.

title: Download WHO Administrative Boundaries with Partial Update — download_shapefile
text: sf object containing administrative boundaries following columns: adm0_code ISO3 country code adm0 Country name adm1 Admin level 1 name (regions/states) - ADM1 ADM2 adm2 Admin level 2 name (districts) - ADM2 start_date Date boundary became effective end_date Date boundary ceased (9999-12-31 current) geometry Spatial geometry column dest_path provided, data also saved location.

title: Download WHO Administrative Boundaries with Partial Update — download_shapefile
text: function retrieves boundary data Global Administrative Boundaries service hosted ArcGIS. uses httr2 package API requests sf spatial data handling. Date fields automatically converted milliseconds since epoch R Date objects. latest = TRUE (default), active boundaries returned (ENDDATE 9999-12-31). latest = FALSE, historical boundaries included, useful tracking boundary changes time (e.g., country splits administrative reorganizations). incremental update feature checks existing files already downloaded countries fetches missing ones, making efficient building large boundary datasets.

title: Download WHO Administrative Boundaries with Partial Update — download_shapefile
text: Requires httr2 package API requests. Install : install.packages("httr2")

title: Download WHO Administrative Boundaries with Partial Update — download_shapefile
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ tf <- file.path(tempdir(), "test_env")  # Download latest ADM2 boundaries without saving to disk kenya_districts <- download_shapefile(   country_codes = "KEN",   admin_level = "ADM2",   latest = TRUE  # Only current boundaries )  # Download all historical boundaries (e.g., to track boundary changes) sudan_historical <- download_shapefile(   country_codes = "SDN",   admin_level = "ADM0",   latest = FALSE  # Include historical boundaries ) # This would show Sudan before and after South Sudan independence # (2011-07-11)  # Download multiple countries and save to directory # File will be saved as "who_shapefile_ken_uga_tza_adm0_latest.gpkg" (latest=TRUE) africa_countries <- download_shapefile(   country_codes = c("KEN", "UGA", "TZA"),   admin_level = "ADM0",   dest_path = tf )  # Incremental update - add more countries to existing file # Will automatically use the same filename for the same countries+admin combo download_shapefile(   country_codes = c("KEN", "UGA", "TZA", "RWA", "BDI"),   admin_level = "ADM0",   dest_path = tf ) # Creates "who_shapefile_ken_uga_tza_rwa_bdi_adm0_latest.gpkg" (latest=TRUE)  # Different admin levels saved to different files automatically # Saves as "who_shapefile_ken_adm1_latest.gpkg" (latest=TRUE) download_shapefile("KEN", "ADM1", dest_path = tf)  # Saves as "who_shapefile_ken_adm2_latest.gpkg" (latest=TRUE) download_shapefile("KEN", "ADM2", dest_path = tf)  # Multiple countries at ADM2 level # Saves as "who_shapefile_com_syc_mus_adm2_latest.gpkg" (latest=TRUE) download_shapefile(c("COM", "SYC", "MUS"), "ADM2", dest_path = tf) } # }

text: country_codes Character vector ISO country codes (e.g., "GBR", "GIN"). Pass "GLOBAL" (case-insensitive) download worldwide mosaic 0_Mosaicked directory instead per-country raster. "GLOBAL" works years >= 2015 1km resolution type = "count". years Numeric vector years download data (2000-2030). type Character; either "density" persons per sq km "count" total population count (default: "count"). Note: density available years 2000-2020. resolution Character; either "1km" (default) "100m". 100m resolution available years 2015-2030 available GLOBAL mosaic. dest_dir Destination directory downloaded files (default: current dir) quiet Logical; TRUE, suppresses progress messages (default: FALSE)

Progress shown downloads summary provided upon completion.

what: Download WorldPop Population Raster Data for Specific Age Bands — download_worldpop_age_band
title: Download WorldPop Population Raster Data for Specific Age Bands — download_worldpop_age_band
text: Downloads WorldPop population rasters specified age bands years. sex = "" year >= 2015, downloads pre-summed "t" (total) raster instead fetching male female separately summing locally. Automatically selects legacy (2000-2014) R2025A / R2024B (2015-2030) dataset based year.

title: Download WorldPop Population Raster Data for Specific Age Bands — download_worldpop_age_band
code: download_worldpop_age_band(   country_codes,   years,   age_range = c(1, 9),   sex = "both",   resolution = "1km",   release = "R2025A",   out_dir = ".",   quiet = FALSE,   keep_band_files = FALSE )

title: Download WorldPop Population Raster Data for Specific Age Bands — download_worldpop_age_band
text: country_codes Character vector. ISO3 country codes (e.g., "TUN", "BDI"). Pass "GLOBAL" (case-insensitive) download worldwide mosaic 0_Mosaicked directory instead per-country raster. "GLOBAL" works years >= 2015 1km resolution. years Numeric vector. Years download data (2000-2030). age_range Numeric vector length 2 specifying lower upper age range bounds (e.g., c(1, 9) ages 1-9). Default: c(1, 9). sex Character. sex download: "" (default, total population), "m" (male ), "f" (female ). years >= 2015 "" satisfied single t (total) variant; years < 2015 male female downloaded separately summed locally. resolution Character. Either "1km" (default) "100m". 100m resolution available years >= 2015 available GLOBAL mosaic. release Character. WorldPop release years >= 2015: "R2025A" (default, constrained / built-areas ) "R2024B" (unconstrained, covers populated areas via interpolation). Ignored years < 2015 (legacy dataset always used). two releases answer different methodological questions directly comparable. out_dir Character string. Directory downloaded files saved. Default: ".". quiet Logical; TRUE, suppresses progress messages (default: FALSE). keep_band_files Logical. FALSE (default), individual per-band rasters downloaded build combined output deleted combined raster written. Set TRUE keep , e.g. reuse across calls overlapping age ranges.

title: Download WorldPop Population Raster Data for Specific Age Bands — download_worldpop_age_band
text: return value. Files saved output directory pattern: {iso3}_{sex}_{lower}_{upper}_{year}.tif sex "total", "m", "f", upper "80plus" 80+ band included. GLOBAL requests, iso3 global.

title: Download WorldPop Population Raster Data for Specific Age Bands — download_worldpop_age_band
text: function automatically selects appropriate dataset:

title: Download WorldPop Population Raster Data for Specific Age Bands — download_worldpop_age_band
text: URL: https://data.worldpop.org/GIS/AgeSex_structures/Global_2000_2020_1km/ Resolution: 1km Sex variants: m, f (t total available) Files: {iso3}_{sex}_{code}_{year}_1km.tif

title: Download WorldPop Population Raster Data for Specific Age Bands — download_worldpop_age_band
text: URL: https://data.worldpop.org/GIS/AgeSex_structures/Global_2015_2030/R2025A/ Constrained built-areas Resolution: 1km 100m Sex variants: m, f, t (total) Files: {iso3}_{sex}_{code}_{year}_CN_{res}_R2025A{_UA}_v1.tif

title: Download WorldPop Population Raster Data for Specific Age Bands — download_worldpop_age_band
text: URL: https://data.worldpop.org/GIS/AgeSex_structures/Global_2015_2030/R2024B/ Unconstrained Resolution: 1km 100m Sex variants: m, f, t (total) Files: {iso3}_{sex}_{code}_{year}_UC_{res}_R2024B{_UA}_v1.tif

title: Download WorldPop Population Raster Data for Specific Age Bands — download_worldpop_age_band
text: Passing country_codes = "GLOBAL" swaps {ISO3} URL segment 0_Mosaicked uses global_ instead ISO3 prefix filename. Available years >= 2015 1km . worldwide files large (R2025A: ~280 MB, R2024B: ~650 MB per band).

title: Download WorldPop Population Raster Data for Specific Age Bands — download_worldpop_age_band
text: exact age range covered single WorldPop band, function combines adjacent bands. example: Requesting ages 2-9 combine bands 1-4 5-9 Requesting ages 0-10 combine bands 0-1, 1-4, 5-9, 10-14

title: Download WorldPop Population Raster Data for Specific Age Bands — download_worldpop_age_band
Download WorldPop Population Raster Data for Specific Age Bands — download_worldpop_age_band
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Download age 1-9 data for Burundi (both sexes; uses R2025A 't' variant) download_worldpop_age_band(   country_codes = "BDI",   years = 2020:2024,   age_range = c(1, 9),   out_dir = "data/worldpop" )  # Download female-only age 1-9 data download_worldpop_age_band(   country_codes = "BDI",   years = 2020:2024,   age_range = c(1, 9),   sex = "f",   out_dir = "data/worldpop" )  # Use R2024B unconstrained release instead of the R2025A default download_worldpop_age_band(   country_codes = "BDI",   years = 2020,   release = "R2024B",   out_dir = "data/worldpop" )  # Download the worldwide mosaic (large file, ~280 MB per band) download_worldpop_age_band(   country_codes = "GLOBAL",   years = 2020,   age_range = c(0, 4),   out_dir = "data/worldpop" ) } # }

what: Download WorldPop DUG Urbanicity Rasters — download_worldpop_urbanicity
title: Download WorldPop DUG Urbanicity Rasters — download_worldpop_urbanicity
text: Downloads Degree Urbanisation Grid (DUG) rasters WorldPop specified countries, years, classification layers. L1 (DEGURBA 3-class) L2 (sub-classified) layers downloaded default.

title: Download WorldPop DUG Urbanicity Rasters — download_worldpop_urbanicity
code: download_worldpop_urbanicity(   country_codes,   years = 2015:2024,   layers = c("L1", "L2"),   release = "R2025A",   version = "v1",   dest_dir = here::here(),   quiet = FALSE )

title: Download WorldPop DUG Urbanicity Rasters — download_worldpop_urbanicity
text: country_codes Character vector ISO3 country codes (e.g., "DZA", "GIN"). Case-insensitive; uppercased URL building. Pass "GLOBAL" download every country available server. Unlike WorldPop products worldwide DUG mosaic, "GLOBAL" expands per-country rasters available countries (~240 per year; files small, roughly 50-500 KB ). years Numeric vector years download (2015-2030). Default: 2015:2024 (full available historical range). layers Character vector. Subset c("L1", "L2"). Default: . L1 DEGURBA 3-class scheme endorsed UN Statistical Commission (3 = urban centre, 2 = urban cluster, 1 = rural). L2 finer-grained sub-classification. release Character. WorldPop release tag. Default: "R2025A". version Character. WorldPop version tag. Default: "v1". dest_dir Destination directory downloaded files (default: ::()). quiet Logical; TRUE, suppresses per-file progress messages (default: FALSE).

title: Download WorldPop DUG Urbanicity Rasters — download_worldpop_urbanicity
text: Invisible list containing: files: Character vector paths downloaded/existing files counts: Named integer vector successful files per country

title: Download WorldPop DUG Urbanicity Rasters — download_worldpop_urbanicity
text: Files pulled https://data.worldpop.org/GIS/DUG/Global_2015_2030/{release}/{version}/{year}/{ISO3}/ filenames form {ISO3}_DUG_{year}_GRID_{layer}_{release}_{version}.tif. Coverage WorldPop mirror limited 2015-2030. Pre-2015 historical years post-2030 projections underlying JRC / Copernicus GHS-DUG product hosted elsewhere scope function. Existing files skipped (idempotent). Per-file failures (e.g., transient 404 one country/year combination) soft-failed warning rest batch completes.

title: Download WorldPop DUG Urbanicity Rasters — download_worldpop_urbanicity
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Download both L1 and L2 for Algeria, full historical range download_worldpop_urbanicity("DZA")  # Multiple countries, narrower year range, L1 only download_worldpop_urbanicity(   country_codes = c("DZA", "GIN"),   years = 2020:2024,   layers = "L1",   dest_dir = here::here("data/worldpop/dug") )  # Every available country for one year (no mosaic exists, so GLOBAL # expands to ~240 per-country rasters) download_worldpop_urbanicity(   country_codes = "GLOBAL",   years = 2020,   layers = "L1",   dest_dir = here::here("data/worldpop/dug") ) } # }

what: Extract time components from a filename — extract_time_components
title: Extract time components from a filename — extract_time_components
text: Uses pattern parser returned detect_time_pattern() parse date embedded filename computes derived units (month, semester, quarter).

title: Extract time components from a filename — extract_time_components
code: extract_time_components(filename, info, year_extractor = NULL)

title: Extract time components from a filename — extract_time_components
text: filename Character string file path name info List returned detect_time_pattern(), containing: pattern One "monthly" "yearly". parser Function parse character dates Date/POSIXct. year_extractor Optional function extract year filename. provided, function used instead automatic year detection yearly data. take character string (filename) return character string (year). Useful filenames multiple 4-digit numbers heuristics may fail.

title: Extract time components from a filename — extract_time_components
text: list : year Integer year month Integer month (1–12) NA yearly data semester Integer semester (1–2) NA quarter Integer quarter (1–4) NA date Date object first day period

title: Extract time components from a filename — extract_time_components
text: yearly data, multiple plausible years (1980-2099) present filename, last one returned. heuristic assumes dataset version stamps appear early filename data years appear later. deterministic control, pass custom year_extractor function.

title: Extract time components from a filename — extract_time_components
code: info <- detect_time_pattern("rainfall_2022-01.tif") extract_time_components("rainfall_2022-01.tif", info) #> $year #> [1] 2022 #>  #> $month #> [1] 1 #>  #> $semester #> [1] 1 #>  #> $quarter #> [1] 1 #>  #> $date #> [1] "2022-01-01" #>  info <- detect_time_pattern("temp_2020.tif") extract_time_components("temp_2020.tif", info) #> $year #> [1] 2020 #>  #> $month #> [1] NA #>  #> $semester #> [1] NA #>  #> $quarter #> [1] NA #>  #> $date #> [1] "2020-01-01" #>  # Custom year extractor for complex filenames my_extractor <- function(f) {   stringr::str_extract(f, "Rate\\.(\\d{4})\\.tiff$", group = 1) } extract_time_components("Malaria_Rate.2020.tiff", info, my_extractor) #> $year #> [1] 2020 #>  #> $month #> [1] NA #>  #> $semester #> [1] NA #>  #> $quarter #> [1] NA #>  #> $date #> [1] "2020-01-01" #>

what: Extrapolate Population Estimates for Target Years — extrapolate_pop
title: Extrapolate Population Estimates for Target Years — extrapolate_pop
text: Fills missing population values specified years applying multipliers nearest available year within location group. Can handle multiple population columns simultaneously. Supports extending forward beyond latest data backward earliest data. Automatically calculates growth rates existing data multipliers provided.

title: Extrapolate Population Estimates for Target Years — extrapolate_pop
code: extrapolate_pop(   data,   year_col,   pop_cols,   group_cols,   years_to_extrap,   multiplier = NULL )

title: Extrapolate Population Estimates for Target Years — extrapolate_pop
text: data data frame containing population data year, multiple population columns, location columns. year_col name year column (unquoted character). pop_cols character vector population column names extrapolate. group_cols character vector grouping column names defining location. years_to_extrap vector target years extrapolate. Can unnamed (e.g., c(2021, 2022)) named specific multipliers (e.g., c(2021 = 1.5, 2022 = 1.3)). multiplier single numeric multiplier apply years years_to_extrap unnamed (e.g., 1.5). Can also named list/vector multipliers population column. year-specific multipliers, use nested list structure like list(pop_total = c('2021' = 1.03, '2022' = 1.025)). NULL sufficient data exists, growth rates calculated automatically.

title: Extrapolate Population Estimates for Target Years — extrapolate_pop
text: data frame updated population estimates specified population columns years.

title: Extrapolate Population Estimates for Target Years — extrapolate_pop
code: # Dummy data for 3 districts over 3 years with multiple population columns dummy_data <- expand.grid(  adm0 = "COUNTRYX",  adm1 = c("RegionA", "RegionB"),  adm2 = c("District1", "District2"),  year = 2018:2020 ) |>  dplyr::mutate(    adm3 = paste0(adm2, "_Subarea"),    pop_total = sample(1000:5000, size = dplyr::n(), replace = TRUE),    pop_0_11m = pop_total * 0.08,    pop_0_4y = pop_total * 0.15,    pop_u15 = pop_total * 0.45  ) |>  dplyr::arrange(adm0, adm1, adm2, year)  # what: Plot Faceted Choropleth Maps from sf Data with Discrete Bins — facetted_map_bins
title: Plot Faceted Choropleth Maps from sf Data with Discrete Bins — facetted_map_bins
text: Creates faceted choropleth map sf object using binned categorical variable fill colors. function designed programmatic mapping indicators space time, optional administrative boundary overlays optional file export. function assumes fill variable already discretised (e.g. incidence bins, prevalence bins, rate categories) mapped explicitly colors via fill_colors. Typical use cases include: Mapping crude adjusted incidence district multiple years Visualising binned prevalence coverage indicators Producing consistent, publication-ready spatial outputs

title: Plot Faceted Choropleth Maps from sf Data with Discrete Bins — facetted_map_bins
code: facetted_map_bins(   data,   fill_col,   facet_col = NULL,   facet_row = NULL,   adm1_shp = NULL,   fill_colors,   title = NULL,   subtitle = NULL,   caption = NULL,   fill_label = NULL,   ncol = 3,   output_file = NULL,   width = 7,   height = 10,   dpi = 300,   scale = 1,   title_size = 14,   subtitle_size = 11,   legend_title_size = 10,   compress_image = TRUE )

title: Plot Faceted Choropleth Maps from sf Data with Discrete Bins — facetted_map_bins
text: data sf object containing geometries attributes plot. Must include columns specified fill_col facet_col. fill_col Character scalar. Name column data used polygon fill. column factor character vector levels matching fill_colors. facet_col Optional character scalar. Name column used faceting columns, typically time variable year. NULL, faceting applied single map produced. Default NULL. facet_row Optional character scalar. Name column used faceting rows. provided, function uses facet_grid(row ~ col) instead facet_wrap(). Default NULL. adm1_shp Optional sf object containing higher-level administrative boundaries (e.g. ADM1). provided, boundaries overlaid thin black outlines. Default NULL. fill_colors Named character vector colors. Names must correspond exactly levels fill_col. Colors hex codes. title Optional character scalar. Plot title. Default NULL (title). subtitle Optional character scalar. Plot subtitle. Default NULL (subtitle). caption Optional character scalar. Plot caption (annotation bottom right). Default NULL (caption). fill_label Optional character scalar. Legend title fill scale. Default NULL (legend title). ncol Integer. Number columns facet layout. used facet_row NULL (.e., using facet_wrap()). Default 3. output_file Optional character scalar. File path plot saved. NULL, plot saved disk. width Numeric. Width saved plot inches. Default 7. height Numeric. Height saved plot inches. Default 10. dpi Numeric. Resolution (dots per inch) saved output. Default 300. scale Numeric. Scaling factor plot. Values greater 1 make text elements relatively smaller; values less 1 make larger. Default 1. title_size Numeric. Font size plot title. Default 14. subtitle_size Numeric. Font size plot subtitle. Default 11. legend_title_size Numeric. Font size legend title. Default 10. compress_image Logical. Compress PNG using compress_png() saving. Defaults TRUE.

title: Plot Faceted Choropleth Maps from sf Data with Discrete Bins — facetted_map_bins
text: ggplot object. output_file provided, plot also written disk using ggsave().

title: Plot Faceted Choropleth Maps from sf Data with Discrete Bins — facetted_map_bins
text: Uses geom_sf() spatial rendering. Uses scale_fill_manual() explicit legend control ensure consistent color interpretation across plots. Faceting implemented via facet_wrap() using dynamically constructed formula. function perform binning internally. binning logic applied upstream.

title: Plot Faceted Choropleth Maps from sf Data with Discrete Bins — facetted_map_bins
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # example incidence color scale (cases per 1,000 population) incidence_colors <- c(   "0-5"       = "#c6dbef",   "5-50"      = "#9ecae1",   "50-100"    = "#4292c6",   "100-250"   = "#fb6a4a",   "250-500"   = "#cb181d",   "500-5,000" = "#7f0000" )  facetted_map_bins(   data = incidence_shp,   fill_col = "n1_incidence_bin",   facet_col = "year",   adm1_shp = shp_file$adm1,   fill_colors = incidence_colors,   title = "Crude Malaria Incidence by District (ADM2), Burundi",   subtitle = "All-age crude incidence aggregated annually",   fill_label = "Cases per 1,000 population",   ncol = 3,   output_file = here::here(     "outputs",     "burundi_crude_incidence.png"   ) ) } # }

what: Plot Faceted Choropleth Maps from sf Data with Continuous Gradient — facetted_map_gradient
title: Plot Faceted Choropleth Maps from sf Data with Continuous Gradient — facetted_map_gradient
text: Creates faceted choropleth map sf object using continuous numeric variable fill colors displayed gradient. function designed programmatic mapping continuous indicators space time, optional administrative boundary overlays optional file export. function expects fill variable continuous numeric data (e.g. incidence rates, prevalence percentages, continuous coverage metrics) maps color gradient. Typical use cases include: Mapping continuous incidence rates district multiple years Visualising continuous prevalence coverage indicators Producing consistent, publication-ready spatial outputs gradients

title: Plot Faceted Choropleth Maps from sf Data with Continuous Gradient — facetted_map_gradient

title: Plot Faceted Choropleth Maps from sf Data with Continuous Gradient — facetted_map_gradient
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # continuous incidence rates facetted_map_gradient(   data = incidence_shp,   fill_col = "incidence_rate",   facet_col = "year",   adm1_shp = shp_file$adm1,   title = "Continuous Malaria Incidence by District (ADM2), Burundi",   subtitle = "All-age crude incidence aggregated annually",   fill_label = "Cases per 1,000 population",   ncol = 3,   output_file = here::here(     "outputs",     "burundi_continuous_incidence.png"   ) )  # percentage coverage with fixed 0-100 scale facetted_map_gradient(   data = coverage_shp,   fill_col = "coverage_pct",   limits = c(0, 100),   facet_col = "year",   title = "ITN Coverage (%)",   fill_label = "Coverage (%)" ) } # }

what: Plot monthly reporting activity by health facility — facility_reporting_plot
title: Plot monthly reporting activity by health facility — facility_reporting_plot
text: Builds balanced monthly panel, flags reporting key indicators, derives first reporting date activity status, returns ggplot.

title: Plot monthly reporting activity by health facility — facility_reporting_plot
code: facility_reporting_plot(   data,   hf_col,   date_col = "date",   key_indicators = c("test", "pres", "conf"),   method = 1,   nonreport_window = 6,   reporting_rule = "any_non_na",   binary_classification = FALSE,   facet_col = NULL,   facet_ncol = 2,   year_breaks = NULL,   palette = "classic",   include_never_reported = TRUE,   target_language = "en",   source_language = "en",   lang_cache_path = base::tempdir(),   plot_path = NULL,   compress_image = FALSE,   image_overwrite = TRUE,   compression_speed = 1,   compression_verbose = TRUE,   plot_scale = 0.75,   plot_width = 22,   plot_height = 15,   plot_dpi = 300,   show_plot = TRUE,   ... )

title: Plot monthly reporting activity by health facility — facility_reporting_plot
text: data Data frame containing routine health facility records. hf_col Character. Column storing health facility identifiers. date_col Character. Column storing observation dates. Defaults "date". key_indicators Character vector columns defining reporting activity. Defaults c("test", "pres", "conf"). method Character numeric. Classification method - can numeric (1, 2, 3) character ("method1", "method2", "method3", ""). Defaults 1. nonreport_window Integer. Minimum number consecutive non-reporting months classify facility inactive method 3. Defaults 6. reporting_rule Character. Defines counts reporting: "any_non_na" (default, counts NA non-reporting, 0 counts reported) "positive_only" (requires >0 value count reported). binary_classification Logical. TRUE, uses binary classification ("Active", "Inactive") instead three-level classification. Defaults FALSE. facet_col Character. Optional column name use faceting plot. provided, creates separate panels unique value column (e.g., one panel per province administrative region). Can column type. Default NULL (faceting). facet_ncol Integer. Number columns facet layout facet_col provided. Defaults 2. year_breaks Numeric value specifying interval (months) x-axis date breaks. NULL (default), uses "3 months". palette Character. Colour palette activity statuses. One c("classic", "sunset", "forest", "coral", "violet", "slate", "citrus", "orchid"). Defaults "classic". include_never_reported Logical. TRUE, includes facilities never reported plot. TRUE (default), shows facilities reported least . target_language Target language labels (ISO 639-1). Defaults "en". source_language Source language labels. Defaults "en". lang_cache_path Path used cache translations. Defaults base::tempdir(). plot_path Path directory saving plot output. NULL (default), plot saved. compress_image Logical. Compress PNG using compress_png() saving. Defaults FALSE. image_overwrite Logical. Overwrite existing file TRUE. Defaults TRUE. compression_speed Integer (1-10) controlling compression effort. Defaults 1. compression_verbose Logical. Emit compression progress TRUE. Defaults TRUE. plot_scale Numeric. Scaling factor saved plots. Values > 1 increase size, < 1 decrease size. Default 0.75. plot_width Numeric. Width saved plot inches. Default 20. plot_height Numeric. Height saved plot inches. Default 15. plot_dpi Numeric. Resolution saved plot dots per inch. Default 300. show_plot Logical. FALSE, plot returned invisibly (displayed). Useful saving plots. Default TRUE. ... Additional arguments passed internal functions.

what: Fallback Absolute Difference Between Two Vectors (type-preserving) — fallback_diff
title: Fallback Absolute Difference Between Two Vectors (type-preserving) — fallback_diff
text: Computes difference two numeric integer vectors element-wise. values present, returns pmax(abs(col1 - col2), minimum). one value present, returns non-missing value minimum, whichever greater. missing, returns NA appropriate type.

title: Fallback Absolute Difference Between Two Vectors (type-preserving) — fallback_diff
code: fallback_diff(col1, col2, minimum = 0)

title: Fallback Absolute Difference Between Two Vectors (type-preserving) — fallback_diff
text: col1 Numeric integer vector. First input column. col2 Numeric integer vector. Second input column. minimum Numeric integer scalar. Minimum allowable value result (default 0).

title: Fallback Absolute Difference Between Two Vectors (type-preserving) — fallback_diff
text: integer numeric vector length inputs. element : absolute difference col1 col2, non-missing, non-missing value one present, NA_integer_ NA_real_ missing. cases, result constrained less minimum.

title: Fallback Absolute Difference Between Two Vectors (type-preserving) — fallback_diff
text: return type matches inputs: inputs integer vectors (minimum integer scalar), result integer NA_integer_ missing. Otherwise, result numeric (double) NA_real_ missing.

title: Fallback Absolute Difference Between Two Vectors (type-preserving) — fallback_diff
code: fallback_diff(5, 3)        # 2 #> [1] 2 fallback_diff(NA, 4)       # 4 #> [1] 4 fallback_diff(7, NA)       # 7 #> [1] 7 fallback_diff(4, 9)        # 0 #> [1] 5 fallback_diff(NA, NA)      # NA_real_ #> [1] NA  xi <- c(5L, NA, 7L) yi <- c(3L, 4L, NA_integer_) fallback_diff(xi, yi)      # integer output #> [1] 2 4 7

what: Smart row-wise sum with missing data handling and type preservation — fallback_row_sum
title: Smart row-wise sum with missing data handling and type preservation — fallback_row_sum
text: Computes row-wise sum across multiple input vectors, allowing control many non-missing values must present valid result. number non-NA values row min_present, result set NA appropriate type.

title: Smart row-wise sum with missing data handling and type preservation — fallback_row_sum
code: fallback_row_sum(..., min_present = 1, .keep_zero_as_zero = TRUE)

title: Smart row-wise sum with missing data handling and type preservation — fallback_row_sum
text: ... Numeric integer vectors equal length summed row-wise. min_present Integer. Minimum number non-NA values required per row return sum. Rows fewer min_present non-NA values return NA_integer_ inputs integer, otherwise NA_real_. .keep_zero_as_zero Logical. Currently unused, reserved future development. Defaults TRUE.

title: Smart row-wise sum with missing data handling and type preservation — fallback_row_sum
text: integer numeric vector row-wise sums, depending input types, appropriate NA values insufficient data present.

title: Smart row-wise sum with missing data handling and type preservation — fallback_row_sum
text: return type matches inputs: inputs integer vectors, result integer vector NA_integer_ insufficient data present. Otherwise, result numeric (double) vector NA_real_ insufficient data present.

title: Smart row-wise sum with missing data handling and type preservation — fallback_row_sum
code: # all integer inputs -> integer output x <- c(1L, 2L, NA_integer_) y <- c(3L, NA_integer_, 4L) fallback_row_sum(x, y) #> [1] 4 2 4 typeof(fallback_row_sum(x, y))  # "integer" #> [1] "integer"  # mixed integer and numeric inputs -> numeric output z <- c(1, 2, NA) fallback_row_sum(x, z) #> [1]  2  4 NA typeof(fallback_row_sum(x, z))  # "double" #> [1] "double"  # using min_present to control NA behaviour fallback_row_sum(c(1, NA), c(2, NA), min_present = 1) #> [1]  3 NA
