what: Facility name matching across datasets (DHIS2 vs MFL) — fuzzy_match_facilities
title: Facility name matching across datasets (DHIS2 vs MFL) — fuzzy_match_facilities
text: Orchestrates multi-step matching pipeline target dataset (e.g., DHIS2 facilities) lookup dataset (e.g., MFL). Steps include exact match, standardized-name match, fuzzy match, interactive stratified match (using sntutils::prep_geonames()). Returns consolidated results QA summaries.

title: Facility name matching across datasets (DHIS2 vs MFL) — fuzzy_match_facilities
code: fuzzy_match_facilities(   target_df,   lookup_df,   admin_cols = c("adm0", "adm1", "adm2"),   hf_col_name = "hf",   uid_col = "hf_uid",   steps = c(exact = TRUE, standardization = TRUE, fuzzy = TRUE, interactive = TRUE),   lookup_cols = character(),   match_interactivity = TRUE,   fuzzy_methods = c("jw", "lv"),   fuzzy_threshold = 95L,   score_exact = 100L,   score_standardization = 100L,   score_interactive = 95L,   status_cuts = c(high = 95, medium = 80, low = 70),   include_missing_name_rows = TRUE,   save_path = NULL,   matching_cache_path = NULL,   save_stem = "facility_matching",   summary_language = "en",   verbose = TRUE )

title: Facility name matching across datasets (DHIS2 vs MFL) — fuzzy_match_facilities
text: target_df Tibble/data.frame facility names admin columns. lookup_df Tibble/data.frame facility names admin columns. admin_cols Character vector admin columns ordered high low. Default c("adm0", "adm1", "adm2"). hf_col_name Character. Facility name column used data frames. Default "hf". uid_col Character. Column target_df uniquely identifying facility. Default "hf_uid". steps Named logical vector toggling steps: c(exact = TRUE, standardization = TRUE, fuzzy = TRUE, interactive = FALSE). lookup_cols Character vector lookup_df columns append match results returned target table. Default character(). match_interactivity Logical flag forwarded interactive matching helper. Default TRUE. fuzzy_methods Character vector string distance methods combine. Supported: c("jw", "lv"). Default c("jw", "lv"). fuzzy_threshold Integer 0-100 acceptance. Default 95. score_exact Integer score exact matches. Default 100. score_standardization Integer score standardized matches. Default 100. score_interactive Integer score interactive matches. Default 95. status_cuts Named numeric thresholds status classification: c(high = 95, medium = 80, low = 70). include_missing_name_rows Logical. TRUE, adds rows missing name match_method = "missing_name". Default TRUE. save_path Character. Directory path root outputs. Default NULL; NULL, results written disk. matching_cache_path Character. Directory interactive cache unmatched files. Default ::(paths$cache). save_stem Character. Base filename stem saved outputs. Default "facility_matching". summary_language Character vector choosing summary language columns. Supported codes: "en", "fr", "pt". Default "en". verbose Logical. TRUE, prints CLI summary box preview matching results. Default TRUE.

title: Facility name matching across datasets (DHIS2 vs MFL) — fuzzy_match_facilities
text: list results, by_method, dhis2_only, mfl_only, target_augmented, summary_table, coverage_summary, params. One--one matching always enforced, ensuring MFL facility matched one DHIS2 facility.

what: Generate insecticide resistance trend plot — generate_ir_plot
title: Generate insecticide resistance trend plot — generate_ir_plot
text: Creates faceted time-series plot showing insecticide resistance mortality trends multiple projection scenarios. Inspired facetted_map_gradient() sntutils. Supports flexible value columns, faceting, automatic PNG compression.

title: Generate insecticide resistance trend plot — generate_ir_plot
code: generate_ir_plot(   data,   value_col = "mean_ir",   facet_col = "adm1",   ncol = 4,   title = NULL,   subtitle = NULL,   caption = NULL,   y_label = "Mortalité (mean)\n",   legend_title = NULL,   continue_trend_start_year = 2010,   year_cut = 2025,   width = 16,   height = 9,   output_file = NULL,   dpi = 300,   compress_image = TRUE,   image_overwrite = TRUE,   compression_speed = 1,   compression_verbose = TRUE,   show_plot = TRUE )

title: Generate insecticide resistance trend plot — generate_ir_plot
text: data Data frame containing pre-filtered IR data year, value column, facet column, trendmethod column. value_col Character scalar. Name y-axis value column. Default "mean_ir". facet_col Character scalar. Name facetting column. Default "adm1". ncol Numeric. Number facet columns. Default 4. title Character scalar. Plot title. NULL, title shown. subtitle Character scalar. Plot subtitle. NULL, subtitle shown. caption Character scalar. Plot caption. NULL, caption shown. y_label Character scalar. Y-axis label. Default "Mortalit\u00e9 (mean)\n". legend_title Character scalar. Legend title trend method. NULL, default title generated. continue_trend_start_year Numeric. First year show plot. Default 2010. year_cut Numeric. Year show vertical reference line indicating projection start. Default 2025. width Numeric. Plot width inches. Default 16. height Numeric. Plot height inches. Default 9. output_file Optional character scalar. File path plot saved. NULL, plot saved disk. dpi Numeric. Resolution (dots per inch) saved output. Default 300. compress_image Logical. Compress PNG using compress_png() saving. Default TRUE. image_overwrite Logical. Overwrite existing PNG compression. Default TRUE. compression_speed Numeric. Compression speed 1 (best quality, slowest) 10 (fastest, lower quality). Default 1. compression_verbose Logical. Print compression statistics. Default TRUE. show_plot Logical. Display plot. FALSE, returns plot object invisibly. Default TRUE.

title: Generate insecticide resistance trend plot — generate_ir_plot
text: ggplot object. output_file provided, plot also written disk using ggsave() optionally compressed.

title: Generate insecticide resistance trend plot — generate_ir_plot
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ir_trends <- data.frame(   adm0 = "BDI",   adm1 = rep(c("Province A", "Province B"), each = 48),   year = rep(2010:2030, 4),   trendmethod = rep(c("constantTrend", "hybridTrend"), each = 42),   mean_ir = runif(96, 0.6, 0.95) )  plot_obj <- generate_ir_plot(   data = ir_trends,   value_col = "mean_ir",   facet_col = "adm1",   title = "Deltamethrin Mortality Trends",   output_file = here::here("outputs", "ir_trends.png") ) } # }

what: Get active facilities from a dataset — get_active_facilities
title: Get active facilities from a dataset — get_active_facilities

title: Get active facilities from a dataset — get_active_facilities
code: get_active_facilities(   data,   hf_col,   date_col = "date",   key_indicators = c("allout", "conf", "test", "treat", "pres"),   method = 3,   nonreport_window = 6,   reporting_rule = "any_non_na",   return_summary = FALSE )

title: Get active facilities from a dataset — get_active_facilities
text: data data frame containing health facility data hf_col Character. Name column containing unique facility IDs date_col Character. Name date column. Default "date" key_indicators Character vector indicator columns used determine reporting activity. Default c("allout", "conf", "test", "treat", "pres") method Numeric character. Classification method (1, 2, 3, "method1", "method2", "method3"). Default 3 (dynamic activation/inactivation) nonreport_window Integer. Number consecutive non-reporting periods facility considered inactive (method 3). Default 6 reporting_rule Character. counts reporting: "any_non_na" (default, 0 counts reported) "positive_only" (requires >0) return_summary Logical. TRUE, returns summary tibble instead filtered data. Default FALSE

title: Get active facilities from a dataset — get_active_facilities
text: return_summary = FALSE (default): Returns input data frame filtered include rows active facilities, added activity_status column. return_summary = TRUE: Returns summary tibble columns: n_total: Total number unique facilities n_active: Number active facilities n_inactive: Number inactive facilities pct_active: Percentage facilities active pct_inactive: Percentage facilities inactive

title: Get active facilities from a dataset — get_active_facilities
text: function particularly useful : Pre-filtering data calculating reporting rates Understanding proportion operational facilities Ensuring denominators include facilities reporting function uses classify_facility_activity internally determine facility status based reporting patterns key indicators.

title: Get active facilities from a dataset — get_active_facilities
code: # Create sample data sample_data <- data.frame(   date = rep(as.Date(c("2024-01-01", "2024-02-01", "2024-03-01")), each = 4),   facility_id = rep(c("HF001", "HF002", "HF003", "HF004"), 3),   district = rep(c("North", "North", "South", "South"), 3),   allout = c(     100, 150, 0, NA,    # Month 1: HF001 and HF002 active,                         #  HF003 reports 0, HF004 never reports     110, 160, 0, NA,    # Month 2     105, 155, NA, NA    # Month 3: HF003 stops reporting   ),   conf = c(     10, 15, 0, NA,     11, 16, 0, NA,     10, 15, NA, NA   ) )  # Get only active facilities active_only <- get_active_facilities(   data = sample_data,   hf_col = "facility_id",   date_col = "date",   key_indicators = c("allout", "conf") ) #> ℹ Facility activity classification: #>   3 of 4 facilities are active (75%) #>   1 facilities are inactive (25%) #> ✔ Returning data filtered to 3 active facilities (of 4 total)  # Get summary of facility activity activity_summary <- get_active_facilities(   data = sample_data,   hf_col = "facility_id",   date_col = "date",   key_indicators = c("allout", "conf"),   return_summary = TRUE ) #> ℹ Facility activity classification: #>   3 of 4 facilities are active (75%) #>   1 facilities are inactive (25%)

what: Get Upstream and Downstream Variables for Malaria Pathway Indicators — get_pathway_vars
title: Get Upstream and Downstream Variables for Malaria Pathway Indicators — get_pathway_vars
text: Determines upstream downstream variables given malaria indicator based clinical care pathway. Structural imputation allowed indicators follow predictable relationships pathway.

title: Get Upstream and Downstream Variables for Malaria Pathway Indicators — get_pathway_vars
code: get_pathway_vars(   var_to_impute,   malaria_pathway = c("susp", "test", "conf", "maltreat", "maladm", "maldth"),   facility_type = NULL,   suffix = NULL )

title: Get Upstream and Downstream Variables for Malaria Pathway Indicators — get_pathway_vars
text: var_to_impute Character. Variable name analyze (e.g., "conf"). include suffix ; suffix appended separately provided. malaria_pathway Character vector. Ordered indicator sequence. facility_type Character NULL. Facility type. Used restrict imputation admissions ('maladm'), apply inpatient- capable facilities. Examples include: "hospital", "referral", "inpatient", "health_facility" "tertiary". facility types (e.g., "health_post", "clinic") treated outpatient-. suffix Character NULL. Optional suffix append variable names output (e.g., "_hf" return "test_hf", "conf_hf").

title: Get Upstream and Downstream Variables for Malaria Pathway Indicators — get_pathway_vars
text: list : var_to_impute Indicator suffix applied. upstream_vars Indicators logically precede . downstream_vars Indicators logically follow . use_structural Logical. TRUE structural imputation allowed.

title: Get Upstream and Downstream Variables for Malaria Pathway Indicators — get_pathway_vars
text: Assumes variable names match standard: 'susp', 'test', 'conf', 'maltreat', 'maladm', 'maldth'. Indicator usage imputation logic depends facility type. Admissions ('maladm') included inpatient-capable facilities. +————+—————–+——————–+———————–+ | Indicator  | Facility Type   | Upstream Used      | Downstream Used       | +————+—————–+——————–+———————–+ | susp       |             | —                  | test, conf, maladm*,  | |            |                 |                    | maldth                | | test       |             | susp               | conf, maladm*, maldth | | conf       |             | susp, test         | maladm*, maldth       | | maltreat   |             | susp, test, conf   | maladm*, maldth       | | maladm     | Inpatient  | susp, test, conf   | maldth                | | maladm     | Outpatient | —                  | —                     | | maldth     |             | susp, test, conf,  |                       | |            |                 | maladm*            | —                     | +————+—————–+——————–+———————–+ used facility inpatient-capable

title: Get Upstream and Downstream Variables for Malaria Pathway Indicators — get_pathway_vars
code: get_pathway_vars("test") #>  #> ── Malaria Pathway Summary ───────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> ℹ Imputing for test #> ✔ Upstream: susp #> ✔ Downstream: conf, maldth #> ℹ Note: "maladm" excluded from dependency logic due to outpatient facility. #> ℹ Note: "maltreat" excluded from dependency logic due to presumptive treatment. #>  #> $var_to_impute #> [1] "test" #>  #> $upstream_vars #> [1] "susp" #>  #> $downstream_vars #> [1] "conf"   "maldth" #>  #> $use_structural #> [1] TRUE #>  get_pathway_vars("conf", suffix = "_hf") #>  #> ── Malaria Pathway Summary ───────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> ℹ Imputing for conf_hf #> ✔ Upstream: susp_hf, test_hf #> ✔ Downstream: maldth_hf #> ℹ Note: "maladm_hf" excluded from dependency logic due to outpatient facility. #> ℹ Note: "maltreat_hf" excluded from dependency logic due to presumptive treatment. #>  #> $var_to_impute #> [1] "conf_hf" #>  #> $upstream_vars #> [1] "susp_hf" "test_hf" #>  #> $downstream_vars #> [1] "maldth_hf" #>  #> $use_structural #> [1] TRUE #>  get_pathway_vars("maltreat") #>  #> ── Malaria Pathway Summary ───────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> ℹ Imputing for maltreat #> ✔ Upstream: susp, test, conf #> ✔ Downstream: maldth #> ℹ Note: "maladm" excluded from dependency logic due to outpatient facility. #>  #> $var_to_impute #> [1] "maltreat" #>  #> $upstream_vars #> [1] "susp" "test" "conf" #>  #> $downstream_vars #> [1] "maldth" #>  #> $use_structural #> [1] TRUE #>  get_pathway_vars("maladm", facility_type = "hospital") #>  #> ── Malaria Pathway Summary ───────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> ℹ Imputing for maladm #> ✔ Upstream: susp, test, conf #> ✔ Downstream: maldth #> ℹ Note: "maltreat" excluded from dependency logic due to presumptive treatment. #>  #> $var_to_impute #> [1] "maladm" #>  #> $upstream_vars #> [1] "susp" "test" "conf" #>  #> $downstream_vars #> [1] "maldth" #>  #> $use_structural #> [1] TRUE #>  get_pathway_vars("maladm", facility_type = "health_post") #>  #> ── Malaria Pathway Summary ───────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> ℹ Imputing for maladm #> ✖ Admissions not applicable at outpatient facilities (e.g., health post). #> ! Structural imputation excluded #> ✔ Upstream: None #> ✔ Downstream: None #> $var_to_impute #> [1] "maladm" #>  #> $upstream_vars #> character(0) #>  #> $downstream_vars #> character(0) #>  #> $use_structural #> [1] FALSE #>

title: Download WorldPop Rasters and Get Paths — get_worldpop_paths
text: country_code Character. Single ISO3 country code (e.g., "TGO"), "GLOBAL" (case-insensitive) download worldwide mosaics instead per-country rasters. "GLOBAL" requires years >= 2015 1km resolution. years Numeric vector years download (2000-2030) groups Named list age group specifications. element list age_range (length-2 numeric vector NULL total population) optionally sex ("", "m", "f"). Default includes total, u5, wra groups. resolution Character. "1km" (default) "100m". release Character. WorldPop release age-band downloads years >= 2015: "R2025A" (default, constrained) "R2024B" (unconstrained). Ignored years < 2015 total-population downloads (always use R2025A years >= 2015). dest_dir Character. Base directory downloaded files. Total population goes dest_dir, age bands go dest_dir/aged_rasters. download Logical. TRUE (default), downloads rasters building paths. Set FALSE just get expected paths. quiet Logical. TRUE (default), suppresses per-file progress messages download. compact summary always shown.

what: Impute outliers using moving average from adjacent time points — impute_outlier_ma
title: Impute outliers using moving average from adjacent time points — impute_outlier_ma
text: Replaces outlier values (exceeding threshold) moving average adjacent non-outlier values. Useful smoothing time series data outliers replaced locally consistent values rather removed.

title: Impute outliers using moving average from adjacent time points — impute_outlier_ma
code: impute_outlier_ma(x, threshold)

title: Impute outliers using moving average from adjacent time points — impute_outlier_ma
text: x Numeric vector values. sorted time within groups calling function. threshold Numeric scalar. Values strictly greater threshold considered outliers imputed.

title: Impute outliers using moving average from adjacent time points — impute_outlier_ma
text: Numeric vector length x, outliers replaced rounded moving average valid adjacent values. valid adjacent values exist, original value retained.

title: Impute outliers using moving average from adjacent time points — impute_outlier_ma
text: function works follows: value, checks previous next values non-outliers (.e., <= threshold) value exceeds threshold, calculates mean valid adjacent values (previous /next) outlier replaced rounded moving average valid adjacent values exist (neighbors also outliers NA), original value kept approach preserves local trends removing spike anomalies. particularly useful time series outliers represent data errors rather true signal.

title: Impute outliers using moving average from adjacent time points — impute_outlier_ma
code: # simple example with one outlier x <- c(10, 12, 100, 14, 11) impute_outlier_ma(x, threshold = 50) #> [1] 10 12 13 14 11 # Returns: c(10, 12, 13, 14, 11) - the 100 is replaced with mean(12, 14) = 13  # consecutive outliers - only outer edges get imputed x <- c(10, 100, 200, 15) impute_outlier_ma(x, threshold = 50) #> [1] 10 10 15 15 # Returns: c(10, 10, 15, 15) - each outlier uses its one valid neighbor  # use with dplyr for grouped time series if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data |>   dplyr::arrange(adm2, date) |>   dplyr::group_by(adm2) |>   dplyr::mutate(     cases_imputed = impute_outlier_ma(cases, threshold = upper_bound)   ) |>   dplyr::ungroup() } # }

title: Create Outlier Detection Plots — outlier_plot
text: function creates plots visualize outliers data using detection methods detect_outliers(). values upper thresholds highlighted outliers. plotting function inherits parameters detection function seamless integration.

title: Create Outlier Detection Plots — outlier_plot
code: outlier_plot(   data,   column,   record_id = "record_id",   admin_level = c("adm1", "adm2"),   spatial_level = "hf_uid",   date = "date",   time_mode = c("across_time", "within_year", "seasonal"),   value_type = c("count", "rate"),   strictness = c("balanced", "lenient", "strict", "advanced"),   methods = c("iqr", "median", "mean", "consensus"),   sd_multiplier = 3,   mad_constant = 1.4826,   mad_multiplier = 9,   iqr_multiplier = 2,   consensus_rule = 3,   min_n = 8,   reporting_rate_col = NULL,   reporting_rate_min = 0.5,   key_indicators_hf = NULL,   classify_outbreaks = FALSE,   outbreak_min_run = 2,   outbreak_prop_tolerance = 0.9,   outbreak_max_gap = 12,   show_outbreaks = FALSE,   consensus_colors = TRUE,   return_plots = "all",   year_breaks = 2,   target_language = "en",   source_language = "en",   lang_cache_path = tempdir(),   plot_path = NULL,   compress_image = FALSE,   image_overwrite = TRUE,   compression_speed = 1,   compression_verbose = TRUE,   plot_scale = 1,   plot_width = NULL,   plot_height = NULL,   plot_dpi = 300,   show_plot = TRUE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Create Outlier Detection Plots — outlier_plot
text: data Data frame containing indicator analyse. column Name numeric column evaluate. record_id Unique record identifier column. admin_level Character vector administrative level columns parallel grouping, ordered higher lower resolution. Defaults c("adm1", "adm2"). spatial_level Character string specifying finest spatial unit analysis (e.g., "hf_uid" facility-level). specified, admin_level defines grouping boundaries spatial_level defines unit analysis. prevents excessive grouping maintaining spatial granularity. Default hf_uid. date Date column (Date, POSIXt, parseable character string). Year, month, yearmon automatically derived column. time_mode Pooling strategy: "across_time", "within_year", "seasonal". Seasonal mode groups month across years (e.g., Januaries together), useful detecting values unusual specific month regardless year. value_type Indicator type: "count" "rate". Counts floor lower bounds 0. strictness Strictness preset: "lenient", "balanced", "strict", "advanced". Presets map method multipliers. "advanced", manual multipliers ignored. methods Character vector specifying outlier detection methods plot: "iqr" (Interquartile Range), "median" (Median Absolute Deviation), "mean" (Mean +/- SD), /"consensus". Default c("iqr", "median", "mean", "consensus"). consensus, least two methods must selected. sd_multiplier Width (SD units) mean method (used strictness = "advanced"). mad_constant Constant passed stats::mad() advanced mode (default 1.4826). mad_multiplier Width multiplier MAD method (advanced mode). iqr_multiplier Tukey fence multiplier IQR method (advanced mode). consensus_rule Number methods must agree (1, 2, 3) consensus flag call outlier. Default 2. min_n Minimum observations required active comparison bucket flagging attempted (applies seasonal bucket fallback). reporting_rate_col Optional column reporting completeness [0, 1]. reporting_rate_min Minimum acceptable reporting rate. Rows threshold receive reason = "low_reporting" flagged. key_indicators_hf Optional character vector indicator names used determine facility activeness. supplied, function uses fast path filter inactive facility-months. facility-month considered active specified key indicators non-NA values. Inactive facility-months excluded outlier detection. NULL (default), activeness filtering skipped. Typical indicators include "allout", "test", "conf". adjustment prevents false positives caused facilities start stop reporting mid-period. classify_outbreaks Logical. TRUE (default), applies outbreak classification distinguish isolated outliers sustained outbreak patterns. Consecutive outliers meeting outbreak criteria reclassified "outlier" "outbreak". particularly useful epidemiological surveillance identify disease outbreak patterns. Set FALSE disable outbreak classification. outbreak_min_run Integer. Minimum number consecutive outliers required classify outbreak (default 2). Must >= 2. outbreak_prop_tolerance Numeric. Proportional tolerance outbreak consistency (default 0.9). Values within tolerance run median considered consistent. Range: (0, 1). outbreak_max_gap Integer. Maximum allowed gap (non-outlier months) outliers can still considered part outbreak (default 1). example, outbreak_max_gap = 12, pattern "outlier-normal-outlier-outlier" classified one outbreak length 3, rather separate incidents. Set 0 strict consecutive-outbreaks. Useful real-world data reporting gaps. show_outbreaks Logical. TRUE, displays outbreaks separately outliers plot using distinct color (#52AAC2 teal). subtitle show separate counts outliers (red) outbreaks (teal). FALSE (default), outbreaks outliers shown together red. relevant classify_outbreaks = TRUE. consensus_colors Logical. TRUE (default), creates single plot showing consensus strength across methods using graduated colors. Point colors indicate many methods flagged observation: grey (normal), light red (1 method, weak signal), medium red (2 methods, moderate signal), dark red (3 methods, strong signal). Requires least 2 detection methods (excluding "consensus"). enabled, show_outbreaks automatically set FALSE two visualization modes mutually exclusive. legend displayed showing color--strength mapping. Set FALSE standard multi-method plot behavior (separate plot per method). return_plots Character vector specifying plots return. Can subset methods computed: "iqr", "median", "mean", "consensus", "" (default). example, methods = c("iqr", "median", "consensus") return_plots = c("iqr", "consensus"), IQR consensus plots returned. computed plots still created saved (plot_path specified), requested ones returned. year_breaks Numeric value specifying interval x-axis breaks. Default 2. example, 2 shows every second tick 3 every third. target_language Character string specifying language plot labels. Defaults "en" (English). Use ISO 639-1 language codes. source_language Source language code. NULL, auto-detection used. Defaults "en". lang_cache_path Path directory storing translation cache. Defaults tempdir(). plot_path Character string specifying path plots saved. NULL (default), plots saved. compress_image Logical. TRUE, compress saved plot. Defaults FALSE image_overwrite Logical. TRUE, overwrite existing files. Defaults TRUE. compression_speed Integer. Speed/quality trade-1 (brute-force) 10 (fastest). Default 1. compression_verbose Logical. Controls output verbosity. FALSE = silent, TRUE = verbose. Defaults TRUE. plot_scale Numeric. Scaling factor saved plots. Values > 1 increase size, < 1 decrease size. Default 1. plot_width Numeric. Width saved plot inches. NULL (default), width calculated based number facets. plot_height Numeric. Height saved plot inches. NULL (default), height calculated facet count. plot_dpi Numeric. Resolution saved plot dots per inch. Default 300. show_plot Logical. FALSE, plot returned invisibly (displayed). Useful saving plots. Default TRUE. verbose Logical. TRUE, prints informative summary describing methods, pooling, strictness, guardrails, consensus rule. Default FALSE.

title: Create Outlier Detection Plots — outlier_plot
text: single method specified, returns ggplot object. multiple methods specified, returns named list ggplot objects (one per method). show_plot FALSE, returns invisibly.

title: Create Outlier Detection Plots — outlier_plot
text: function creates scatter plots showing outliers detected using specified statistical methods. method produces separate plot points colored outlier status. default, outliers outbreaks shown red, normal values grey. show_outbreaks = TRUE, outbreaks displayed teal (#52AAC2) distinguish isolated outliers (red). consensus plots, caption shows consensus rule used. plots faceted administrative levels include summary statistics subtitle. Facet labels longer 15 characters automatically truncated "..." prevent overcrowding.

title: Create Outlier Detection Plots — outlier_plot
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Create plots for all methods (default) - returns named list all_plots <- outlier_plot(   data = malaria_data,   column = "confirmed_cases",   date = "date",   record_id = "facility_id" ) # Access individual plots: all_plots$iqr, all_plots$median, etc.  # Single method - returns single ggplot object iqr_plot <- outlier_plot(   data = malaria_data,   column = "confirmed_cases",   date = "date",   record_id = "facility_id",   methods = "iqr" )  # Multiple specific methods selected_plots <- outlier_plot(   data = malaria_data,   column = "confirmed_cases",   date = "date",   record_id = "facility_id",   methods = c("iqr", "median"),   time_mode = "by_month",   strictness = "strict",   year_breaks = 6 )  # Include consensus (requires 2+ other methods) with_consensus <- outlier_plot(   data = malaria_data,   column = "confirmed_cases",   date = "date",   record_id = "facility_id",   methods = c("iqr", "median", "mean", "consensus"),   consensus_rule = 2,   plot_path = "outliers.png"  # Saves as outliers_iqr.png, etc. )  # Detect at facility level but visualize at district level district_plots <- outlier_plot(   data = malaria_data,  # Contains facility-level outlier detection results   column = "confirmed_cases",   date = "date",   record_id = "facility_id",   spatial_level = "facility_id",  # Detection at facility level   admin_level = c("adm1", "adm2"),  # Plot/facet at district level   methods = c("consensus") )  # Show outbreaks separately in teal color outbreak_plot <- outlier_plot(   data = malaria_data,   column = "confirmed_cases",   date = "date",   record_id = "facility_id",   methods = "consensus",   classify_outbreaks = TRUE,   show_outbreaks = TRUE  # Outbreaks shown in teal, outliers in red )  # Compute multiple methods but return only specific plots selected <- outlier_plot(   data = malaria_data,   column = "confirmed_cases",   date = "date",   record_id = "facility_id",   methods = c("iqr", "median", "mean", "consensus"),   return_plots = c("iqr", "consensus"),  # Only return these two   plot_path = "outliers.png"  # All methods still saved to disk ) # Returns list with only $iqr and $consensus plots  # Consensus color visualization showing agreement strength consensus_plot <- outlier_plot(   data = malaria_data,   column = "confirmed_cases",   date = "date",   record_id = "facility_id",   methods = c("iqr", "median", "mean"),   consensus_colors = TRUE  # Single plot with color-coded consensus strength ) # Returns single ggplot with graduated colors: # grey = normal, light red = 1 method, medium red = 2 methods, dark red = 3 methods } # }

what: Interactive Admin Name Cleaning and Matching — prep_geonames
title: Interactive Admin Name Cleaning and Matching — prep_geonames
text: function streamlines admin name cleaning process, leveraging algorithmic approaches interactive user decisions. combines string distance algorithms initial matching offers user interactivity final decision-making, saved future reference sharing. Although function require limiting name matching exclusively upper-level admins, optimal performance achieved confining stricter within-admin stratifications, ensuring accurate results. function can also work site names even string matching lookup data.

title: Interactive Admin Name Cleaning and Matching — prep_geonames
code: prep_geonames(   target_df,   lookup_df = NULL,   level0 = NULL,   level1 = NULL,   level2 = NULL,   level3 = NULL,   level4 = NULL,   cache_path = NULL,   unmatched_export_path = NULL,   method = "jw",   interactive = TRUE,   max_options = 200,   preserve_case = FALSE,   column_width = 60 )

title: Interactive Admin Name Cleaning and Matching — prep_geonames
text: target_df Data frame containing admin names clean. lookup_df Lookup data frame verifying admin names. provided, internal version geoname data attached sntutils used. level0 level0 col name (country) 'data' 'lookup_data'. level1 level1 col name (province) 'data' 'lookup_data'. level2 level2 col name (district) 'data' 'lookup_data'. level3 level3 col name (subdistrict) 'data' 'lookup_data'. level4 level4 col name (settlement) 'data' 'lookup_data'. cache_path Optional; path cache data frame saved user modifications. Supports file formats supported sntutils read() write() functions including .rds, .csv, .xlsx, .dta, .tsv, . path also used match integrate previously established corrections current session. NULL file exist provided path, users prompted specify new path create new cache data frame. unmatched_export_path Optional; path save unmatched data processing. file include complete rows full hierarchical context, showing column needs matching (typically granular level like health facilities) along administrative levels metadata (timestamp, username, data sources). Supports file formats supported sntutils write() function based file extension. method string distance calculation method(s) used. Users can specify one algorithms stringdist package compute string distances admin names. function default uses "jw" (Jaro-Winkler). options include: "lv" (Levenshtein), "dl" (Damerau-Levenshtein), "lcs" (Longest Common Subsequence), "qgram" (Q-Gram), "jw" (Jaro-Winkler), "soundex". interactive Logical; TRUE, prompts user interactive matching decisions. Defaults FALSE. max_options Maximum number options output string distance matching. Default 200. preserve_case Logical; TRUE, preserves original case admin names lookup data returning matched values. FALSE (default), returns admin names uppercase . column_width Numeric; maximum width (characters) column interactive menu. Controls much text displayed truncation. Default 60 characters. Note actual text display width approximately 8 characters less accommodate number labels truncation markers ("...").

title: Interactive Admin Name Cleaning and Matching — prep_geonames
text: data frame cleaned administrative names saved data frame user decisions.

title: Interactive Admin Name Cleaning and Matching — prep_geonames
text: function performs following steps: Prepares data ensuring administrative names uppercase consistent matching. Attempts load previously saved cache file available, initializes cleaning process. Matches administrative names target_df lookup_df using string distance algorithms, running parallel. Results ranked closeness. Engages user interactive CLI menu make decisions ambiguous matches. Saves user's decisions cache data frame, either specified path prompting user location. Returns cleaned data frame updated administrative names based user choices algorithmic matching.

title: Interactive Admin Name Cleaning and Matching — prep_geonames
code: # \donttest{ # Dummy target data target_df <- data.frame(   country = c("ANGOLA", "UGA", "ZAMBIA"),   province = c("CABONDA", "TESO", "LUSAKA"),   district = c("BALIZE", "BOKEDEA", "RAFUNSA"),   subdistrict = c("AREA1", "AREA2", "AREA3") )  # Dummy lookup data with correct spellings lookup_df <- data.frame(   country = c("ANGOLA", "ANGOLA", "UGANDA", "UGANDA", "ZAMBIA", "ZAMBIA"),   province = c("CABINDA", "CABINDA", "TESO", "TESO", "LUSAKA", "LUSAKA"),   district = c("BELIZE", "BUCO-ZAU", "BUKEDEA", "KUMI", "KAFUE", "LUSAKA"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  # Interactively clean geonames prep_geonames(   target_df,   lookup_df = lookup_df,   level0 = "country", level1 = "province",   level2 = "district",   interactive = FALSE # replace with TRUE for interactivity ) #>  #> ── ℹ Match Summary ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #>  #> ! Both sides have unmatched names; see per-level lines below. #>  #> Target data as base N                                                        #> • country (level0): 2 out of 3 matched                                       #> • province (level1): 1 out of 3 matched                                      #> • district (level2): 0 out of 3 matched                                      #> Lookup data as base N                                                        #> • country (level0): 2 out of 3 matched                                       #> • province (level1): 1 out of 3 matched                                      #> • district (level2): 0 out of 6 matched                                      #> ✔ In non-interactive mode. Exiting after matching with cache... #>   country province district subdistrict #> 1  ANGOLA  CABONDA   BALIZE       AREA1 #> 2     UGA     TESO  BOKEDEA       AREA2 #> 3  ZAMBIA   LUSAKA  RAFUNSA       AREA3 # }

what: Prepare data for reporting rate or missing data visualization — prepare_plot_data
title: Prepare data for reporting rate or missing data visualization — prepare_plot_data
text: function processes health facility data prepare visualizing reporting rates missing data patterns. supports three scenarios: reporting rate time admin unit (x + y, single var) missing rate variable time (x , multiple vars) proportion facilities reporting (x, y) group (needs hf)

title: Prepare data for reporting rate or missing data visualization — prepare_plot_data
code: prepare_plot_data(   data,   x_var,   y_var = NULL,   vars_of_interest,   by_facility = FALSE,   hf_col = "hf_uid",   reprate_col = NULL,   use_reprate = TRUE,   key_indicators = c("allout", "conf", "test", "treat", "pres"),   method = 3,   nonreport_window = 6,   reporting_rule = "any_non_na",   require_all = FALSE,   weighting = FALSE,   weight_var = NULL,   weight_window = 12,   exclude_current_x = TRUE,   cold_start = "median_within_y" )

title: Prepare data for reporting rate or missing data visualization — prepare_plot_data
text: data Original data frame x_var Character. Time variable (e.g. yearmon) y_var Character. Optional grouping variable (e.g. district) vars_of_interest Character vector variables analyze by_facility Logical. TRUE, compute facility hf_col Character. Name health facility ID column (required by_facility) reprate_col Character. Optional name column containing pre-calculated reporting rates. provided, values used instead calculating reporting rates. Default NULL. use_reprate Logical. TRUE, return reporting rate. Else, missing rate key_indicators Optional. Character vector indicators used define facility activity scenario 1. Defaults c("allout", "conf", "test", "treat", "pres"). method Character numeric. Classification method facility activity status. Can numeric (1, 2, 3) character ("method1", "method2", "method3"). Defaults 3. See classify_facility_activity details. nonreport_window Integer. Minimum number consecutive non-reporting months classify facility inactive method 3. Defaults 6. reporting_rule Character. Defines counts reporting: "any_non_na" (default, counts NA non-reporting, 0 counts reported) "positive_only" (requires >0 value count reported). require_all Logical. TRUE multiple vars_of_interest provided, calculates proportion facilities reporting variables (complete data). FALSE (default), calculates per-variable reporting rates. applies facility-level analysis (by_facility = TRUE). weighting Logical. Whether use weighted reporting rates. TRUE, facilities weighted typical size, giving importance larger facilities overall reporting rate calculation. Default FALSE. weight_var Character. Name variable use proxy facility size (e.g., "allout" total outpatients). Required weighting TRUE. Default NULL. weight_window Integer. Number periods rolling window calculate typical facility size. Default 12. exclude_current_x Logical. Whether exclude current period calculating weights. TRUE, prevents current reporting influencing weight. Default TRUE. cold_start Character. Method handling facilities insufficient history. Options: "median_within_y" (default) "median_global".

title: Prepare data for reporting rate or missing data visualization — prepare_plot_data
text: list plot_data plotting metadata

title: Prepare data for reporting rate or missing data visualization — prepare_plot_data
code: # Sample data hf_data <- data.frame(   month = rep(as.Date(c("2024-01-01", "2024-02-01", "2024-03-01")), each = 10),   district = rep(c("North", "South"), each = 5, times = 3),   facility_id = rep(1:5, times = 6),   malaria = c(     10, 0, 15, NA, 8, 12, 0, NA, 7, 9,     11, 0, 14, 6, NA, 13, 8, 10, 0, 12,     9, 7, 0, 11, 14, 8, NA, 12, 10, 15   ),   pneumonia = c(     5, 0, NA, 7, 3, 6, 0, 4, NA, 2,     8, 0, 6, NA, 4, 7, 3, 0, 5, 6,     4, 0, 7, 5, NA, 6, 0, 8, 4, 3   ) )  # Scenario 1: Reporting rate by district and month for each variable district_plot_data <- prepare_plot_data(   data = hf_data,   x_var = "month",   y_var = "district",   vars_of_interest = c("malaria", "pneumonia"),   use_reprate = TRUE ) #> Warning: ! No facility identification column (hf_col) provided. #> ℹ Unable to exclude inactive facilities from denominators. #> ℹ This may result in inaccurate rates if inactive facilities are present. #> ℹ Consider providing 'hf_col' parameter to enable facility activity #>   classification. # Returns list with plot_data and metadata for district-level visualization  # Scenario 2: Missing rate of variables over time (months only) variable_plot_data <- prepare_plot_data(   data = hf_data,   x_var = "month",   vars_of_interest = c("malaria", "pneumonia"),   use_reprate = FALSE ) #> Warning: ! No facility identification column (hf_col) provided. #> ℹ Unable to exclude inactive facilities from denominators. #> ℹ This may result in inaccurate rates if inactive facilities are present. #> ℹ Consider providing 'hf_col' and 'y_var' parameters to enable facility #>   activity classification. # Returns list with plot_data and metadata for variable-level visualization  # Scenario 3: Proportion of facilities reporting in each district by month facility_plot_data <- prepare_plot_data(   data = hf_data,   x_var = "month",   y_var = "district",   vars_of_interest = "malaria",   by_facility = TRUE,   hf_col = "facility_id" ) #> ℹ No key indicators provided - including all facilities in denominators #> ℹ Consider providing 'key_indicators' to exclude inactive facilities # Returns list with plot_data and metadata for facility-level visualization

title: Extract Under-5 Mortality Values from IHME Raster Stack — process_ihme_u5m_raster
text: shape sf object containing geographic areas extraction raster_stack terra SpatRaster raster stack containing IHME mortality data id_col Character vector column names shape use identifiers (default: c("adm1", "adm2")) rates Logical indicating whether values rates (TRUE) counts (TRUE, default) stat_type Character string specifying statistic type: "mean", "median", "". applies rates = TRUE. counts, sum always used.

title: Extract Under-5 Mortality Values from IHME Raster Stack — process_ihme_u5m_raster
text: function processes layer IHME raster stack, represents annual estimates starting 2000. rates, mean median values extracted based stat_type; counts, sums used. IHME data provides high-resolution (5 x 5 km) estimates -5 mortality across LMICs.

title: Extract Under-5 Mortality Values from IHME Raster Stack — process_ihme_u5m_raster
text: IHME raster data represents model-based estimates usedwith consideration underlying methodology uncertainties described source documentation.

title: Extract Under-5 Mortality Values from IHME Raster Stack — process_ihme_u5m_raster
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Load required libraries and data library(sf) library(terra)  # Example with rates using mean (default) mortality_rates_mean <- process_ihme_u5m_raster(   shape = admin_boundaries,   raster_stack = mortality_raster,   id_col = c("adm1", "adm2"),   rates = TRUE,   stat_type = "mean" )  # Example with rates using median mortality_rates_median <- process_ihme_u5m_raster(   shape = admin_boundaries,   raster_stack = mortality_raster,   id_col = c("adm1", "adm2"),   rates = TRUE,   stat_type = "median" )  # Example with both mean and median mortality_rates_both <- process_ihme_u5m_raster(   shape = admin_boundaries,   raster_stack = mortality_raster,   id_col = c("adm1", "adm2"),   rates = TRUE,   stat_type = "both" )  # Example with counts (total deaths - always uses sum) mortality_counts <- process_ihme_u5m_raster(   shape = admin_boundaries,   raster_stack = mortality_raster,   id_col = c("adm1"),   rates = FALSE ) } # }

what: Process multiple raster files from a directory — process_raster_collection
title: Process multiple raster files from a directory — process_raster_collection
text: function processes multiple raster files directory applying process_raster_with_boundaries() file combining results. Includes progress tracking error handling.

title: Process multiple raster files from a directory — process_raster_collection
code: process_raster_collection(   directory,   shapefile,   id_cols = c("adm0", "adm1", "adm2"),   pattern = "\\.tif$",   aggregations = c("mean"),   layer_to_process = 1,   raster_is_density = FALSE,   year_extractor = NULL )

title: Process multiple raster files from a directory — process_raster_collection
text: directory Directory containing raster files process shapefile sf/SpatVector object administrative boundaries. Must coordinate reference system (CRS) raster data id_cols Vector column names shapefile include output. Default c("adm0", "adm1", "adm2") country first admin level pattern Regular expression pattern match raster files. Default "\.tif$" match .tif files aggregations Vector aggregation methods calculate. Supported values "mean", "sum", "median". Default c("mean") layer_to_process Integer character. Specifies layer multi-layer raster extract. raster contains multiple layers (e.g., different years indicators), argument selects layer processed. Default 1. raster_is_density Logical indicating raster values represent density. TRUE, values converted density counts using cell area. Default FALSE. year_extractor Optional function. custom function accepts filename (character) returns year integer character. Passed extract_time_components(). Use default "last plausible 4-digit year 1980-2099" heuristic picks wrong year (example malariaAtlas filenames contain multiple year-like tokens). Defaults NULL, uses built-extractor.

title: Process multiple raster files from a directory — process_raster_collection
text: combined data frame containing results processed files, sorted time unit available. structure output process_raster_with_boundaries() multiple files combined.

title: Process multiple raster files from a directory — process_raster_collection
text: function: Lists files directory matching pattern Creates progress bar tracking Processes file using process_raster_with_boundaries() Combines results single data frame Sorts time unit available

title: Process multiple raster files from a directory — process_raster_collection
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Process all CHIRPS files in a directory districts <- sf::st_read("districts.shp") results <- process_raster_collection(   directory = "chirps_data",   shapefile = districts,   id_cols = c("COUNTRY", "DISTRICT"),   pattern = "chirps.*\\.tif$",   aggregations = c("mean", "sum") ) } # }

what: Process raster data with administrative boundaries — process_raster_with_boundaries
title: Process raster data with administrative boundaries — process_raster_with_boundaries
text: function processes single raster file extracting zonal statistics within administrative boundaries. handles CHIRPS rainfall data format default, extracting dates filenames, works raster data.

title: Process raster data with administrative boundaries — process_raster_with_boundaries
code: process_raster_with_boundaries(   raster_file,   shapefile,   id_cols = c("adm0", "adm1"),   aggregations = c("mean"),   raster_is_density = FALSE,   layer_to_process = 1,   pop_raster_file = NULL,   weight_na_as_zero = TRUE,   pattern_info = NULL,   time_components = NULL )

title: Process raster data with administrative boundaries — process_raster_with_boundaries
text: raster_file Path raster file (.tif format) shapefile sf/SpatVector object administrative boundaries. Must coordinate reference system (CRS) raster data id_cols Vector column names shapefile include output. Default c("adm0", "adm1") country first admin level aggregations Vector aggregation methods calculate. Supported values "mean", "sum", "median", "weighted_mean", "weighted_median". two weighted options require pop_raster_file. Default c("mean") raster_is_density Logical indicating raster values represent density. TRUE, values converted density counts using cell area. Default FALSE. layer_to_process Integer character. Specifies layer multi-layer raster extract. raster contains multiple layers (e.g., different years indicators), argument selects layer processed. Default 1 pop_raster_file Optional path population raster used weights "weighted_mean" "weighted_median" aggregations. Reprojected resampled match raster_file. Default NULL. weight_na_as_zero Logical. TRUE, missing weights treated zero; FALSE, NA. used weighted aggregations requested. Default TRUE. pattern_info Optional. Pre-parsed time pattern detect_time_pattern(). NULL, detected filename. time_components Optional. Pre-parsed time components extract_time_components(). NULL, extracted filename.

title: Process raster data with administrative boundaries — process_raster_with_boundaries
text: data frame containing: file_name: Name processed raster file Selected ID columns shapefile (specified id_cols) Time columns based detected pattern: - monthly data: year, month - yearly data: year Aggregated values column names matching aggregation methods

title: Process raster data with administrative boundaries — process_raster_with_boundaries
text: function performs following steps: Validates requested aggregation methods Detects extracts date information filename Loads raster data handles -data values (-9999) raster_is_density TRUE, converts density counts using cell area Calculates zonal statistics using exactextractr. weighted aggregations requested, loads population raster, reprojects resamples match value raster, uses per-cell weights Combines results shapefile attributes Returns clean data frame without geometry

title: Process raster data with administrative boundaries — process_raster_with_boundaries
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Process single CHIRPS file with district-level boundaries districts <- sf::st_read("districts.shp") results <- process_raster_with_boundaries(   raster_file = "chirps-v2.0.2022.01.tif",   shapefile = districts,   id_cols = c("COUNTRY", "DISTRICT"),   aggregations = c("mean", "sum", "median") )  # Population-weighted statistics alongside plain ones results_weighted <- process_raster_with_boundaries(   raster_file = "pfpr_2022.tif",   shapefile = districts,   id_cols = c("COUNTRY", "DISTRICT"),   aggregations = c("mean", "weighted_mean", "weighted_median"),   pop_raster_file = "worldpop_2022.tif" ) } # }

title: Process Year-Indexed Rasters Against Time-Varying Admin Boundaries — process_rasters_by_year
code: process_rasters_by_year(   raster_files,   shapefile,   id_cols = c("adm0", "adm1", "adm2"),   year_col = "year",   aggregations = c("mean"),   layer_to_process = 1,   raster_is_density = FALSE,   year_extractor = NULL )

title: Process Year-Indexed Rasters Against Time-Varying Admin Boundaries — process_rasters_by_year
text: raster_files Character vector raster file paths. shapefile sf object whose rows admin units valid specific year. (admin x year) combination row; year stored column named year_col. id_cols Character vector shapefile columns carry output (e.g., admin names codes). Default: c("adm0", "adm1", "adm2"). year_col Character. Name year column shapefile. Default: "year". aggregations Vector aggregation methods. options process_raster_with_boundaries(). Default: c("mean"). population count rasters, use "sum". layer_to_process Integer character. Layer index multi-layer rasters. Default: 1. raster_is_density Logical. Convert density counts using cell area extraction. Default: FALSE. year_extractor Optional function (filename) -> year, used default "last plausible 4-digit year filename" heuristic picks wrong year. See extract_time_components().

title: Process Year-Indexed Rasters Against Time-Varying Admin Boundaries — process_rasters_by_year
text: tibble one row per (admin, year) combination, holding id_cols, year, requested aggregation columns. Rasters whose year present shapefile skipped warning.

title: Process Year-Indexed Rasters Against Time-Varying Admin Boundaries — process_rasters_by_year
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # population rasters with year-varying districts shp_by_year <- sf::read_sf("districts_by_year.gpkg") raster_paths <- list.files(   "data/worldpop/raw", pattern = "_pop_.*\\.tif$", full.names = TRUE ) pop_by_admin <- process_rasters_by_year(   raster_files = raster_paths,   shapefile = shp_by_year,   id_cols = c("adm0", "adm1", "adm2"),   aggregations = "sum" ) } # }

what: Process Weighted Raster Data in Batch — process_weighted_raster_collection
title: Process Weighted Raster Data in Batch — process_weighted_raster_collection
text: Processes multiple raster files population weights, matching value rasters corresponding population rasters year.

title: Process Weighted Raster Data in Batch — process_weighted_raster_collection
code: process_weighted_raster_collection(   value_raster_dir,   pop_raster_dir,   shapefile,   id_cols = c("adm0", "adm1", "adm2"),   value_pattern = "\\.tiff$",   pop_pattern = "\\.tif$",   value_layer_to_process = 1,   weight_na_as_zero = TRUE,   stat_type = "mean",   year_extractor = NULL )

title: Process Weighted Raster Data in Batch — process_weighted_raster_collection
text: value_raster_dir Character. Directory containing value raster files pop_raster_dir Character. Directory containing population raster files shapefile sf/SpatVector object. Shapefile spatial aggregation id_cols Character vector. Column names spatial identifiers (default: c("adm0", "adm1", "adm2")) value_pattern Character. Regex pattern match value raster files (default: "\.tiff$") pop_pattern Character. Regex pattern match population raster files (default: "\.tif$") value_layer_to_process Integer character. Specifies layer multi-layer raster extract. raster contains multiple layers (e.g., different years indicators), argument selects layer processed. Default 1. weight_na_as_zero Logical. Treat NA weights 0 (default: TRUE) stat_type Character string specifying statistic type: "mean": Population-weighted mean using exactextractr's weighted_mean function "median": Population-weighted median using matrixStats::weightedMedian() approach finds value 50% population weight lies 50% , interpolating pixel values needed "": Returns weighted mean weighted median statistics (default: "mean") year_extractor Optional function. custom function accepts filename (character) returns year integer character. Passed extract_time_components(). Use default "last plausible 4-digit year 1980-2099" heuristic picks wrong year (example malariaAtlas filenames contain multiple year-like tokens). Defaults NULL, uses built-extractor.

title: Process Weighted Raster Data in Batch — process_weighted_raster_collection
text: data frame containing: Spatial identifiers id_cols Weighted mean values Metadata (filename, year, month, semester, quarter, date)

title: Process Weighted Raster Stacks — process_weighted_raster_stacks
text: value_raster SpatRaster object containing values weighted pop_raster SpatRaster object containing population weights shape sf/shapefile object containing geometries shape_id column value_var Character string naming weighted value column start_year Numeric indicating starting year time series stat_type Character string specifying statistic type: "mean": Population-weighted mean using exactextractr's weighted_mean function "median": Population-weighted median using matrixStats::weightedMedian() approach finds value 50% population weight lies 50% , interpolating pixel values needed "": Returns weighted mean weighted median statistics (default: "mean")

what: Plot reporting rate maps over time — reporting_rate_map
title: Plot reporting rate maps over time — reporting_rate_map
text: Creates faceted maps reporting rates (missing rates) administrative unit time. Designed complement reporting_rate_plot() consistent styling color schemes. function displays variables interest title subtitle clarity.

title: Plot reporting rate maps over time — reporting_rate_map
code: reporting_rate_map(   data,   shapefile,   x_var,   adm_var,   vars_of_interest,   hf_col = "hf_uid",   key_indicators = c("allout", "conf", "test", "treat", "pres"),   use_reprate = TRUE,   full_range = TRUE,   show_title = TRUE,   method = 3,   nonreport_window = 6,   reporting_rule = "any_non_na",   require_all = FALSE,   weighting = FALSE,   weight_var = NULL,   weight_window = 12,   exclude_current_x = TRUE,   cold_start = "median_within_y",   fill_palette = "Zissou1",   facet_label = NULL,   facet_ncol = 4,   target_language = "en",   source_language = "en",   lang_cache_path = tempdir(),   plot_path = NULL,   compress_image = FALSE,   image_overwrite = TRUE,   compression_speed = 1,   compression_verbose = TRUE,   plot_scale = 1,   plot_width = NULL,   plot_height = NULL,   plot_dpi = 300,   show_plot = TRUE,   ... )

title: Plot reporting rate maps over time — reporting_rate_map
text: data Data frame health facility data. shapefile sf object containing administrative boundaries (adm1 adm2). x_var Character. Time variable (e.g., "yearmon", "year", "month"). adm_var Character. Administrative variable data shapefile (e.g., "adm1" "adm2"). vars_of_interest Character vector. Variable(s) compute reporting rates . displayed plot title subtitle. hf_col Character. Health facility ID column, required. Default NULL. key_indicators Character vector. Indicator variables used determine facility activity status. Facilities never report indicators excluded denominators. Default c("allout", "conf", "test", "treat", "pres"). use_reprate Logical. TRUE, plot reporting rate (facilities reported); FALSE, plot missing rate (facilities report). Default TRUE. full_range Logical. TRUE, fill scale use full range 0 100. FALSE, fill scale use range values present data. Default TRUE. show_title Logical. TRUE, display plot title. FALSE, hide title. Default TRUE. method Character numeric. Classification method facility activity status. Can numeric (1, 2, 3) character ("method1", "method2", "method3"). Defaults 3. See classify_facility_activity details. nonreport_window Integer. Minimum number consecutive non-reporting months classify facility inactive method 3. Defaults 6. reporting_rule Character. Defines counts reporting: "any_non_na" (default, counts NA non-reporting, 0 counts reported) "positive_only" (requires >0 value count reported). require_all Logical. TRUE multiple vars_of_interest provided, calculates proportion facilities reporting variables (complete data). FALSE (default), calculates per-variable reporting rates. applies facility-level analysis (hf_col provided). weighting Logical. TRUE, compute weighted reporting rates based facility size. Default FALSE. weight_var Character. Weight variable weighting = TRUE. weight_window Integer. Number periods typical size. Default 12. exclude_current_x Logical. Exclude current period weighting. Default TRUE. cold_start Character. Cold-start strategy ("median_within_y" "median_global"). Default "median_within_y". fill_palette Character. used - kept backward compatibility. Color palette automatically selected based use_reprate parameter match reporting_rate_plot() styling. facet_label Character. Optional custom label administrative level title. NULL, uses adm_var value. Default NULL. facet_ncol Integer. Number facet columns. Default 4. target_language Character. ISO 639-1 code translation. Default "en". source_language Character. Source language translation. Default "en". lang_cache_path Path directory storing translation cache. Default tempdir(). plot_path Character. Path save plot. Can either directory path (filename auto-generated) full file path ending .png. NULL, plot saved. compress_image Logical. TRUE, compress saved plot. Default FALSE. image_overwrite Logical. TRUE, overwrite existing files. Default TRUE. compression_speed Integer. Speed/quality trade-1 (brute-force) 10 (fastest). Default 1. compression_verbose Logical. Controls output verbosity. FALSE = silent, TRUE = verbose. Default TRUE. plot_scale Numeric. Scaling factor saved plots. Values > 1 increase size, < 1 decrease size. Default 1. plot_width Numeric. Width saved plot inches. NULL, width calculated automatically based data. plot_height Numeric. Height saved plot inches. NULL, height calculated automatically based data. plot_dpi Numeric. Resolution saved plot dots per inch. Default 300. show_plot Logical. Display plot (TRUE) return invisibly (FALSE). Default TRUE. ... Additional arguments passed internal functions.

what: Plot Missing data or Reporting Rate over time — reporting_rate_plot
title: Plot Missing data or Reporting Rate over time — reporting_rate_plot
text: function visualizes proportion missing data reporting rate specified variables dataset. creates tile plot x-axis can represent categorical time time (e.g., year, month). function can handle three different scenarios: District-level analysis specified variables Variable-level analysis without district grouping Facility-level analysis

title: Plot Missing data or Reporting Rate over time — reporting_rate_plot
code: reporting_rate_plot(   data,   x_var,   y_var = NULL,   vars_of_interest = NULL,   hf_col = NULL,   reprate_col = NULL,   key_indicators = c("allout", "conf", "test", "treat", "pres"),   method = 3,   nonreport_window = 6,   reporting_rule = "any_non_na",   require_all = FALSE,   use_reprate = TRUE,   full_range = TRUE,   weighting = FALSE,   weight_var = NULL,   weight_window = 12,   exclude_current_x = TRUE,   cold_start = "median_within_y",   target_language = "en",   source_language = "en",   lang_cache_path = tempdir(),   plot_path = NULL,   compress_image = FALSE,   image_overwrite = TRUE,   compression_speed = 1,   compression_verbose = TRUE,   plot_scale = 1,   plot_width = NULL,   plot_height = NULL,   plot_dpi = 300,   show_plot = TRUE,   include_plot_title = FALSE,   y_axis_label = NULL,   x_axis_breaks = 6,   ... )

title: Plot Missing data or Reporting Rate over time — reporting_rate_plot
text: data data frame containing data visualized x_var character string specifying time variable 'data' (e.g., "year", "month"). Must provided. y_var Optional grouping variable name () vars_of_interest optional character vector specifying variables visualized 'data'. NULL, variables except 'x_var' 'y_var' used. hf_col Character. Optional (defaults NULL). Name column containing unique health facility IDs. provided key_indicators, enables facility-level analysis proper exclusion inactive facilities denominators, resulting accurate missing rates. reprate_col Character. Optional (defaults NULL). Name column containing pre-calculated facility-level reporting rates. provided, function aggregates column x_var (y_var specified) instead calculating reporting rates raw indicators. column contain numeric values decimal (0-1) percentage (0-100) format. using parameter, vars_of_interest calculation-related parameters (key_indicators, method, nonreport_window, reporting_rule, require_all, weighting, etc.) ignored. hf_col parameter can still used counting facilities. key_indicators Optional. Character vector indicators used define facility activity scenario 1. Defaults c("allout", "conf", "test", "treat", "pres"). method Character numeric. Classification method facility activity status. Can numeric (1, 2, 3) character ("method1", "method2", "method3"). Defaults 3. See classify_facility_activity details. nonreport_window Integer. Minimum number consecutive non-reporting months classify facility inactive method 3. Defaults 6. reporting_rule Character. Defines counts reporting: "any_non_na" (default, counts NA non-reporting, 0 counts reported) "positive_only" (requires >0 value count reported). require_all Logical. TRUE multiple vars_of_interest provided, calculates proportion facilities reporting variables (complete data). FALSE (default), calculates per-variable reporting rates. applies facility-level analysis (hf_col provided). use_reprate logical value. TRUE, reporting rate visualized; otherwise, proportion missing data visualized. Defaults TRUE full_range logical value. TRUE, fill scale use full range 0 100. FALSE, fill scale use range values present data. Defaults TRUE. weighting Logical. TRUE, calculate weighted reporting rates based facility size. Defaults FALSE. weight_var Character. Column name containing weight variable (e.g., "allout" outpatient volume). Required weighting = TRUE. weight_window Integer. Number periods rolling weight calculation. Defaults 12. exclude_current_x Logical. TRUE, exclude current period weight calculation. Defaults TRUE. cold_start Character. Method handling initial periods: "median_within_y" (default) "median_global". target_language character string specifying language plot labels. Defaults "en" (English). Use ISO 639-1 language codes. source_language Source language code. NULL, auto-detection used. Defaults NULL. lang_cache_path Path directory storing translation cache. Defaults tempdir(). plot_path character string specifying path plot saved. NULL (default), plot saved. compress_image Logical. TRUE, compress saved plot. Defaults FALSE image_overwrite Logical. TRUE, overwrite existing files. Defaults TRUE. compression_speed Integer. Speed/quality trade-1 (brute-force) 10 (fastest). Default 1. compression_verbose Logical. Controls output verbosity. FALSE = silent, TRUE = verbose. Defaults TRUE. plot_scale Numeric. Scaling factor saved plots. Values > 1 increase size, < 1 decrease size. Default 1. plot_width Numeric. Width saved plot inches. NULL (default), width calculated automatically based data. plot_height Numeric. Height saved plot inches. NULL (default), height calculated automatically based data. plot_dpi Numeric. Resolution saved plot dots per inch. Default 300. show_plot Logical. FALSE, plot returned invisibly (displayed). Useful saving plots. Default TRUE. include_plot_title Logical. TRUE, plot titles subtitles included. FALSE, titles hidden. Default FALSE. y_axis_label Optional character string y-axis label. NULL, defaults y_var name "Variable" variable scenario. x_axis_breaks Numeric value specifying interval x-axis breaks. Default 6. example, 2 shows every second tick 6 every sixth. ... Additional arguments passed internal functions.

title: Plot Missing data or Reporting Rate over time — reporting_rate_plot
text: ggplot2 object. show_plot FALSE, returns invisibly.

title: Plot Missing data or Reporting Rate over time — reporting_rate_plot
code: # Sample data hf_data <- data.frame(   month = rep(as.Date(c("2024-01-01", "2024-02-01", "2024-03-01")), each = 10),   district = rep(c("North", "South"), each = 5, times = 3),   facility_id = rep(1:5, times = 6),   malaria = c(     10, 0, 15, NA, 8, 12, 0, NA, 7, 9,     11, 0, 14, 6, NA, 13, 8, 10, 0, 12,     9, 7, 0, 11, 14, 8, NA, 12, 10, 15   ),   pneumonia = c(     5, 0, NA, 7, 3, 6, 0, 4, NA, 2,     8, 0, 6, NA, 4, 7, 3, 0, 5, 6,     4, 0, 7, 5, NA, 6, 0, 8, 4, 3   ) )  # Scenario 1: District-level analysis - reporting rate by district and month reporting_rate_plot(   data = hf_data,   x_var = "month",   y_var = "district",   vars_of_interest = c("malaria", "pneumonia"),   hf_col = NULL ) #>  #> ╔ Reporting Rate Plot Configuration ═══════════════════╗ #> ║                                                      ║ #> ║   Analysis Type: Row-level (no facility filtering)   ║ #> ║   Metric: Reporting Rate                             ║ #> ║   Variables: malaria, pneumonia                      ║ #> ║   Grouping: By month and district                    ║ #> ║                                                      ║ #> ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝ #>  #> Warning: ! No facility identification column (hf_col) provided. #> ℹ Unable to exclude inactive facilities from denominators. #> ℹ This may result in inaccurate rates if inactive facilities are present. #> ℹ Consider providing 'hf_col' parameter to enable facility activity #>   classification.   # Scenario 2: Variable-level analysis - missing rate by variable over time reporting_rate_plot(   data = hf_data,   x_var = "month",   vars_of_interest = c("malaria", "pneumonia"),   use_reprate = FALSE,   hf_col = NULL ) #>  #> ╔ Reporting Rate Plot Configuration ═══════════════════╗ #> ║                                                      ║ #> ║   Analysis Type: Row-level (no facility filtering)   ║ #> ║   Metric: Missing Rate                               ║ #> ║   Variables: malaria, pneumonia                      ║ #> ║   Grouping: By month                                 ║ #> ║                                                      ║ #> ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝ #>  #> Warning: ! No facility identification column (hf_col) provided. #> ℹ Unable to exclude inactive facilities from denominators. #> ℹ This may result in inaccurate rates if inactive facilities are present. #> ℹ Consider providing 'hf_col' and 'y_var' parameters to enable facility #>   activity classification. #> Warning: no non-missing arguments to min; returning Inf #> Warning: no non-missing arguments to max; returning -Inf #> Warning: no non-missing arguments to min; returning Inf #> Warning: no non-missing arguments to max; returning -Inf   # Scenario 3: Facility-level analysis - reporting rate by facility # This properly excludes inactive facilities from denominators reporting_rate_plot(   data = hf_data,   x_var = "month",   y_var = "district",   vars_of_interest = c("malaria", "pneumonia"),  # Multiple variables now allowed!   hf_col = "facility_id",   key_indicators = c("malaria", "pneumonia"),  # Define activity based on these   method = 3,  # Dynamic activation method   nonreport_window = 6  # Facilities inactive after 6 months of non-reporting ) #>  #> ╔ Reporting Rate Plot Configuration ════════════════════════╗ #> ║                                                           ║ #> ║   Analysis Type: Facility-level                           ║ #> ║   Metric: Reporting Rate                                  ║ #> ║   Variables: malaria, pneumonia                           ║ #> ║   Numerator: per-variable                                 ║ #> ║   Grouping: By month and district                         ║ #> ║   Denominator: malaria, pneumonia                         ║ #> ║   Facility activity: Method 3 (inactive after 6 months)   ║ #> ║                                                           ║ #> ╚═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝ #>  #> ℹ Facility activity classification: #>   5 of 5 facilities are active (100%) #>   0 facilities are inactive (0%) #> ✔ Returning data filtered to 5 active facilities (of 5 total)   # Scenario 4: Using pre-calculated reporting rates # When data already has facility-level reporting rates calculated hf_data_with_reprate <- hf_data hf_data_with_reprate$reporting_rate <- runif(nrow(hf_data_with_reprate), 0, 1)  reporting_rate_plot(   data = hf_data_with_reprate,   x_var = "month",   y_var = "district",   reprate_col = "reporting_rate",  # Use pre-calculated rates   hf_col = "facility_id",   use_reprate = TRUE ) #>  #> ╔ Reporting Rate Plot Configuration ════════════════════════╗ #> ║                                                           ║ #> ║   Analysis Type: Facility-level                           ║ #> ║   Metric: Reporting Rate                                  ║ #> ║   Variables:                                              ║ #> ║   Numerator: per-variable                                 ║ #> ║   Grouping: By month and district                         ║ #> ║   Denominator: allout, conf, test, ... (5 total)          ║ #> ║   Facility activity: Method 3 (inactive after 6 months)   ║ #> ║                                                           ║ #> ╚═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝ #>

what: Generate insecticide resistance trend scenarios — run_resistance_trend
title: Generate insecticide resistance trend scenarios — run_resistance_trend
text: Extends mortality time series beyond specified year using one three projection methods: constant trend (flat-line), continued trend (model extrapolation), hybrid trend (average ). Supports flexible grouping can handle IR metric column name.

title: Generate insecticide resistance trend scenarios — run_resistance_trend
code: run_resistance_trend(   df_IR,   year_cut = 2025,   n_years_to_add = 5,   continue_trend_start_year = 2010,   model_type = "unchanged",   trendmethod = c("constantTrend", "continuedTrend", "hybridTrend"),   group_cols = c("adm0", "adm1"),   value_col = "mean_ir" )

title: Generate insecticide resistance trend scenarios — run_resistance_trend
text: df_IR Data frame containing insecticide resistance data grouping columns, year column, value column. year_cut Numeric. Last year observed data. Projections start year_cut + 1. Default 2025. n_years_to_add Numeric. Number years project forward. Default 5. continue_trend_start_year Numeric. First year include model fitting continued trend. Default 2010. model_type Character scalar. Regression family continued trend. One "gaussian", "binomial", "quasibinomial", "lm", "gam", "unchanged". "unchanged", uses existing data values directly without refitting. Default "unchanged". trendmethod Character scalar. Projection method: "constantTrend" (flat-line last observed value), "continuedTrend" (model extrapolation), "hybridTrend" (average constant continued). Default "constantTrend". group_cols Character vector. Names grouping columns (e.g., administrative units, insecticides). Default c("adm0", "adm1"). value_col Character scalar. Name mortality/resistance value column. Default "mean_ir".

title: Generate insecticide resistance trend scenarios — run_resistance_trend
text: data frame grouping columns, year, trendmethod, value column specified value_col.

title: Generate insecticide resistance trend scenarios — run_resistance_trend
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ir_data <- data.frame(   adm0 = "BDI",   adm1 = rep(c("Province A", "Province B"), each = 16),   year = rep(2010:2025, 2),   mean_ir = runif(32, 0.6, 0.95) )  constant_proj <- run_resistance_trend(   df_IR = ir_data,   year_cut = 2025,   n_years_to_add = 5,   trendmethod = "constantTrend" )  hybrid_proj <- run_resistance_trend(   df_IR = ir_data,   year_cut = 2025,   trendmethod = "hybridTrend" ) } # }

text: plot ggplot2 object save plot_data Prepared data frame dimension calculations plot_path Directory path save plot x_var time variable name y_var grouping variable name () y_axis_label Label y-axis vars_of_interest Variables visualized target_language Language code translation (default: "en") source_language Source language code (default: NULL auto-detection) lang_cache_path Path translation cache (default: tempdir()) data Original data extracting year range compression_options List compression settings use_reprate logical value. TRUE, reporting rate visualized; otherwise, proportion missing data visualized. Defaults TRUE save_title_prefix string prefix plot title filename. NULL, default prefix used based visualization type. plot_scale Numeric. Scaling factor saved plots. Values > 1 increase size, < 1 decrease size. Default 1. plot_width Numeric. Width saved plot inches. NULL (default), width calculated based content. plot_height Numeric. Height saved plot inches. NULL (default), height calculated based content. plot_dpi Numeric. Resolution saved plot dots per inch. Default 300. hf_col Character. Health facility ID column (default: NULL). require_all Logical. TRUE, requires variables reported (default: FALSE). weighting Logical. TRUE, uses weighted reporting rates (default: FALSE). weight_var Character. Variable use weighting (default: NULL). method Character. Facility activity classification method (default: "method3"). nonreport_window Integer. Consecutive non-reporting months inactivity (default: 6). reporting_rule Character. counts reporting: "any_non_na" "positive_only" (default: "any_non_na").
