what: Summarise population by available admin levels and build a dictionary — snt_process_population
title: Summarise population by available admin levels and build a dictionary — snt_process_population
text: Produces population summaries admin levels present pop_data (adm0..adm3) returns list containing per-level tables, bilingual (EN + optional FR) data dictionary, record admin levels found. default, column types first inferred via auto_parse_types() apply = TRUE return = "data".

title: Summarise population by available admin levels and build a dictionary — snt_process_population
code: snt_process_population(   pop_data,   pop_cols = "pop",   translate = TRUE,   language = NULL,   trans_cache_path = if (requireNamespace("here", quietly = TRUE)) {          here::here("cache/translations")  } else {      "cache/translations"  },   infer_types = TRUE )

title: Summarise population by available admin levels and build a dictionary — snt_process_population
text: pop_data data.frame/tibble adm0, optional adm1/adm2/adm3, year, population column(s). year may integer, numeric, Date/POSIXt, factor, character; coerced integer years feasible. pop_cols character vector population column names process. Default "pop" backward compatibility. translate logical; TRUE, add translated label column (default FR unless language provided) using translation cache. language Optional ISO code (e.g., "fr"). provided, label_<language> column added dictionary placed immediately label_en. trans_cache_path character path translation cache directory. default: ::("cache/translations") available, else "cache/translations". infer_types logical; TRUE (default) coerce pop_data first using auto_parse_types(apply = TRUE, return = "data").

title: Summarise population by available admin levels and build a dictionary — snt_process_population
text: named list elements (levels present included): pop_data_adm0, pop_data_adm1, pop_data_adm2, pop_data_adm3 (aggregated level year) data_dictionary (build_dictionary()) levels_present (character vector admin levels detected)

title: Summarise population by available admin levels and build a dictionary — snt_process_population
text: Input data must unique rows per admin-year finest level present. data contains sex, age, residence strata, aggregate upstream passing function. function error duplicates detected prevent silent double counting.

title: Summarise population by available admin levels and build a dictionary — snt_process_population
text: function automatically detects population columns contain proportions (non-NA values <= 1) versus counts. Proportion columns aggregated using mean, count columns summed. column contains mixed data (values <= 1 > 1), function abort error message.

title: Summarise population by available admin levels and build a dictionary — snt_process_population
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Basic usage with default "pop" column example_pop <- tibble::tibble(   adm0 = c("A", "A", "B"),   adm1 = c("X", "X", "Y"),   adm2 = c("P", "Q", "R"),   year = c(2020L, 2020L, 2021L),   pop  = c(100, 50, 70) ) out <- snt_process_population(example_pop) names(out) out$levels_present  # Using custom population column names example_multi_pop <- tibble::tibble(   adm0 = c("A", "A", "B"),   adm1 = c("X", "X", "Y"),   year = c(2020L, 2020L, 2021L),   total_pop = c(100, 50, 70),   under5_pop = c(20, 10, 15) ) out_multi <- snt_process_population(   example_multi_pop,   pop_cols = c("total_pop", "under5_pop") ) names(out_multi) } # }

title: SNT Variable Tree — snt_var_tree
text: hierarchical data structure containing standardized variable names, multilingual labels, metadata Subnational Tailoring (SNT) malaria interventions. dataset provides foundation variable validation, labeling, data dictionary generation across SNT workflow.

title: SNT Variable Tree — snt_var_tree
code: snt_var_tree

title: SNT Variable Tree — snt_var_tree
text: named list following top-level elements: _meta Metadata variable tree structure metadata Variables related dataset metadata identifiers population Population-related variables demographics routine_data Health facility routine data variables interventions Malaria intervention coverage variables stock_management Stock supply chain management variables anemia Anemia-related health indicators validation_utility Validation utility terms data dictionaries, validation reports, analysis outputs (rate/trend analysis, outliers, consistency checks) schema Schema definitions variable components including: test_types: Diagnostic test type codes labels age_groups: Age group classifications population_groups: Population subgroup definitions sectors: Health sector classifications service_levels: Service delivery level definitions

title: SNT Variable Tree — snt_var_tree
text: Subnational Tailoring Initiative Malaria Interventions

title: SNT Variable Tree — snt_var_tree
text: variable entry contains: snt_var_name: Standardized variable name label_en: English label label_fr: French label label_pt: Portuguese label (available) Additional metadata disaggregation options schema section provides building blocks constructing parsing SNT variable names, follow pattern: domain_testtype_agegroup_populationgroup_sector_servicelevel

what: Standardize name-like strings with optional steps — standardize_names
title: Standardize name-like strings with optional steps — standardize_names
text: Applies sequence standardization steps vector names. step can enabled disabled. default, steps enabled.

title: Standardize name-like strings with optional steps — standardize_names
code: standardize_names(   name_vec,   to_upper = TRUE,   replace_punct = TRUE,   squish_spaces = TRUE,   normalize_accents = TRUE,   normalize_spaces = TRUE,   roman_to_arabic = TRUE,   sort_tokens = TRUE )

title: Standardize name-like strings with optional steps — standardize_names
text: name_vec Atomic vector. Values standardize. to_upper Logical. Convert uppercase. Default TRUE. replace_punct Logical. Replace punctuation spaces. Default TRUE. squish_spaces Logical. Trim/condense spaces. Default TRUE. normalize_accents Logical. Convert accents ASCII. Default TRUE. normalize_spaces Logical. Normalize space-like chars " ". Default TRUE. roman_to_arabic Logical. Convert roman numerals ..IX 1..9 token boundaries. Default TRUE. sort_tokens Logical. Sort tokens letters first, numbers last, groups alphabetically. Default TRUE.

title: Standardize name-like strings with optional steps — standardize_names
text: Character vector standardized names.

title: Standardize name-like strings with optional steps — standardize_names
code: # basic usage standardize_names(c("Chp Kpalime-III", "Kpalimé CHP 3")) #> [1] "CHP KPALIME 3" "CHP KPALIME 3"  # disable token sorting and roman conversion standardize_names(   c("USP Hanyigba-Todzi ii", "USP Hanyigba Todzi 2"),   roman_to_arabic = FALSE, sort_tokens = FALSE ) #> [1] "USP HANYIGBA TODZI II" "USP HANYIGBA TODZI 2"   # keep accents and case, only squish spaces and replace punctuation standardize_names(   c("Hôpital d’Adéta", "Hopital d'Adeta"),   to_upper = FALSE, normalize_accents = FALSE ) #> [1] "Adéta Hôpital d" "Adeta Hopital d"

Using system default. #> [1] "Jun 2026" translate_yearmon(Sys.Date(), "fr", format = "%B %Y") #> Warning: Locale NA not available. Using system default. #> [1] "June 2026"

title: Coordinate Validation and Cleaning — validate_process_coordinates
text: Validates coordinate data (points) returns cleaned sf object standardized columns. Accepts either sf object POINT geometry data.frame longitude latitude columns. Checks include missing coordinates, DMS detection optional conversion (via suggested 'parzer' package), range, precision, flip detection, country containment.

title: Coordinate Validation and Cleaning — validate_process_coordinates
code: validate_process_coordinates(   data,   name = "points",   lon_col = NULL,   lat_col = NULL,   adm0_sf = NULL,   geometry_crs = 4326,   min_decimals = 4,   id_col = NULL,   fix_issues = TRUE,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Coordinate Validation and Cleaning — validate_process_coordinates
text: data sf object POINT geometry data.frame containing coordinate columns. name Character string identifying dataset (reporting). lon_col Character string, column name longitude (data sf). lat_col Character string, column name latitude (data sf). adm0_sf Optional sf polygon/multipolygon country validate points (EPSG:4326 preferred). provided, function tests points fall within given shape. admin attributes joined points. geometry_crs Target CRS output geometry operations adm0_sf (default EPSG:4326). Lat/Lon attributes remain EPSG:4326. min_decimals Minimum decimal places required lon/lat considered precise (default 4). id_col Optional character name unique identifier column (e.g., facility ID). provided, duplicate reporting includes IDs coordinate-duplicate rows aid de-dup decisions. fix_issues Logical, whether attempt automatic fixes (drop invalids, set CRS, standardize columns, remove duplicates, attempt flip fixes adm0_sf available). quiet Logical, whether suppress progress messages.

title: Coordinate Validation and Cleaning — validate_process_coordinates
text: list validation results. Key elements: issues: Character vector issues detected. final_points_df: Cleaned sf object points. invalid_rows: sf/data.frame rows failing basic coordinate checks. checks$duplicate_rows: Duplicates ID + coordinates (standardized sf). checks$all_shared_locations: records sharing location. checks$all_shared_locations_ungrouped: duplicate rows shared locations. checks$coordinates_adm0: Points outside ADM0 original lon/lat restored geometry. column_dictionary: Data frame mapping old standardized column names.

title: Coordinate Validation and Cleaning — validate_process_coordinates
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Example data with common issues: missing, DMS, flipped, imprecise df <- data.frame(   hf = paste0("HF_", 1:6),   lon = c(-13.26077, "13\u00B015'38\"W", 8.5, -12.09057, NA, 181),   lat = c(8.464283, "8\u00B027'51\"N", -13.26, 8.770261, 8.1, 0.1) )  # Simple rectangle as country polygon (e.g., Sierra Leone-ish bbox) adm0_sf <- sf::st_as_sfc(   sf::st_bbox(c(xmin = -14, ymin = 6.9, xmax = -10, ymax = 10.1),               crs = sf::st_crs(4326)) ) |>   sf::st_sf()  # Validate and auto-fix issues (DMS parsing requires 'parzer') res <- validate_process_coordinates(   data = df,   name = "hf_points",   lon_col = "lon",   lat_col = "lat",   adm0_sf = adm0_sf,   min_decimals = 4,   fix_issues = TRUE,   quiet = TRUE )  # Cleaned points res$final_points_df } # }

what: Spatial Vector Validation and Cleaning — validate_process_spatial
title: Spatial Vector Validation and Cleaning — validate_process_spatial
text: Validates spatial vector data creates admin-level aggregations geometry hashes. Follows validation approach validate_process_coordinates() modular checks standardized outputs.

title: Spatial Vector Validation and Cleaning — validate_process_spatial
code: validate_process_spatial(   shp,   name = "shapefile",   adm0_col = NULL,   adm1_col = NULL,   adm2_col = NULL,   adm3_col = NULL,   adm4_col = NULL,   fix_issues = TRUE,   quiet = FALSE,   geometry_crs = 4326,   drop_z = TRUE )

title: Spatial Vector Validation and Cleaning — validate_process_spatial
text: shp sf object validate name Character string identifying dataset (reporting) adm0_col Character string, column name country level adm1_col Character string, column name region/province level adm2_col Character string, column name district level adm3_col Character string, column name chiefdom/commune level adm4_col Character string, column name sub-chiefdom/village level fix_issues Logical, whether attempt automatic fixes quiet Logical, whether suppress progress messages geometry_crs Target CRS output geometry (default EPSG:4326). CRS validation/fixes, geometries transformed CRS subsequent processing output. drop_z Logical, whether drop Z/M coordinates validation. Defaults TRUE avoid s2 warnings 2D processing.

title: Spatial Vector Validation and Cleaning — validate_process_spatial
text: list validation results. Key elements: issues: Character vector issues detected. invalid_rows: sf/data.frame rows failing basic geometry checks. checks$duplicate_rows: Duplicates admin names + geometry. checks$all_shared_locations: records sharing geometry. checks$all_shared_locations_ungrouped: duplicate rows shared geometry. checks$invalid_geometries: Rows invalid geometries fixing. checks$empty_geometries: Rows empty geometries. checks$self_intersecting: Rows self-intersecting geometries. column_dictionary: Data frame column_name description. final_spat_vec: List admin-level aggregations (adm0, adm1, adm2, adm3, adm4). geometry_types: Tibble summarizing geometry types counts. spatial_extent: Named vector xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax.

title: Spatial Vector Validation and Cleaning — validate_process_spatial
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Basic validation with Sierra Leone data (adm3) result <- validate_process_spatial(   shp = shp_raw,   name = "Sierra Leone boundaries",   adm0_col = "country",   adm1_col = "region",   adm2_col = "district",   adm3_col = "chiefdom" )  # Validation with adm4 result <- validate_process_spatial(   shp = shp_raw,   name = "Sierra Leone boundaries",   adm0_col = "country",   adm1_col = "region",   adm2_col = "district",   adm3_col = "chiefdom",   adm4_col = "village" )  # Access cleaned admin levels shp_adm0 <- result$final_spat_vec$adm0 shp_adm1 <- result$final_spat_vec$adm1 shp_adm2 <- result$final_spat_vec$adm2 shp_adm3 <- result$final_spat_vec$adm3 shp_adm4 <- result$final_spat_vec$adm4  # Check validation status if (length(result$issues) == 0) {   cli::cli_alert_success("Ready for SNT analysis!") }  # Access invalid rows (if any) invalid_rows <- result$invalid_rows  # Access duplicate rows (if any) duplicate_rows <- result$checks$duplicate_rows shared_locations <- result$checks$all_shared_locations  # Check column structure as data frame col_dict <- result$column_dictionary # Shows data frame with shapefile, old_name, new_name, description print(col_dict) } # }

what: Orchestrates a suite of validation checks on routine HF data. It standardizes column resolution, selects indicators, runs missing/duplicate/future/logic/ outlier checks, compiles a summary, and optionally translates and saves. — validate_routine_hf_data
title: Orchestrates a suite of validation checks on routine HF data. It standardizes column resolution, selects indicators, runs missing/duplicate/future/logic/ outlier checks, compiles a summary, and optionally translates and saves. — validate_routine_hf_data
text: Orchestrates suite validation checks routine HF data. standardizes column resolution, selects indicators, runs missing/duplicate/future/logic/ outlier checks, compiles summary, optionally translates saves.

title: Orchestrates a suite of validation checks on routine HF data. It standardizes column resolution, selects indicators, runs missing/duplicate/future/logic/ outlier checks, compiles a summary, and optionally translates and saves. — validate_routine_hf_data
code: validate_routine_hf_data(   data,   id_col = "record_id",   facility_col = "hf_uid",   date_col = "date",   yearmon_col = "yearmon",   year_col = "year",   month_col = "month",   admin_cols = c("adm0", "adm1", "adm2", "adm3"),   admin_guid_cols = c("adm0_guid", "adm1_guid", "adm2_guid", "adm3_guid"),   core_id_cols = NULL,   indicators = NULL,   missing_vars = NULL,   consistency_pairs = NULL,   outlier_pairs = NULL,   check_future_dates = TRUE,   check_duplicates = TRUE,   check_outliers = TRUE,   check_facility_activeness = TRUE,   hf_name_col = "hf",   key_indicators = NULL,   nonreport_window = 6,   reporting_rule = "any_non_na",   min_reporting_rate = 0.5,   outlier_methods = c("iqr", "median", "mean", "consensus"),   time_mode = "across_time",   outlier_strictness = "balanced",   sd_multiplier = 3,   mad_constant = 1.4826,   mad_multiplier = 9,   iqr_multiplier = 2,   min_n = 8,   consensus_rule = 1,   n_neighbour_impute = 5,   output_path = NULL,   output_name = "validation_of_hf_routine_data",   output_formats = c("xlsx", "rds"),   build_dictionary = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE,   language = "en" )

title: Orchestrates a suite of validation checks on routine HF data. It standardizes column resolution, selects indicators, runs missing/duplicate/future/logic/ outlier checks, compiles a summary, and optionally translates and saves. — validate_routine_hf_data
text: data data.frame. Routine facility dataset. id_col character. Unique record id. Default "record_id". facility_col character. Facility id. Default "hf_uid". date_col character. Date column. Default "date". yearmon_col character. Year-month col. Default "yearmon". year_col character. Year col. Default "year". month_col character. Month col. Default "month". admin_cols character. Admin columns (adm0..adm3). Default c("adm0","adm1","adm2","adm3"). admin_guid_cols character. Admin GUID columns. Default c("adm0_guid","adm1_guid","adm2_guid","adm3_guid"). core_id_cols character|NULL. NULL, uses set . indicators character|NULL. Numeric indicators validate. NULL, auto-detect numeric columns excluding core ids. missing_vars character|NULL. Additional variables check missing data beyond core_id_cols. Core ID columns always checked. NULL, checks core_id_cols indicators (default behavior). Use limit missing data checks specific indicators always including core IDs. consistency_pairs list|NULL. element list(input=, output=). NULL, defaults generated common malaria cascade rules. outlier_pairs list|NULL. Pairs outlier detection correction. Structure: list(input = c("test"), output = c("conf")). Uses format consistency_pairs. cascade rule : input >= output. directions checked automatically: (1) output outliers validated output <= input, (2) input outliers validated input >= output. Corrections use neighbor median. NULL, defaults malaria cascade. check_future_dates logical. Default TRUE. check_duplicates logical. Default TRUE. check_outliers logical. Default TRUE. check_facility_activeness logical. Check facility activeness. Default TRUE. hf_name_col character. Facility name column. Default "hf". key_indicators character|NULL. Key indicators activeness check. NULL, uses indicators. Default NULL. nonreport_window integer. Minimum number consecutive non-reporting months classify facility inactive method 3. Defaults 6. reporting_rule character. Defines counts reporting activeness: "any_non_na" (default, counts NA non-reporting, 0 counts reported) "positive_only" (requires >0 value count reported). min_reporting_rate numeric. Minimum reporting rate threshold. Default 0.5. outlier_methods character. c("iqr","median","mean"). time_mode character. Time mode outlier detection: "across_time", "within_year", "seasonal". Seasonal compares month across years. Default "across_time". outlier_strictness character. Outlier detection strictness: "balanced", "lenient", "strict", "advanced". Default "balanced". sd_multiplier numeric. Standard deviation multiplier outlier detection. Default 3. mad_constant numeric. MAD constant outlier detection. Default 1.4826. mad_multiplier numeric. MAD multiplier outlier detection. Default 9. iqr_multiplier numeric. IQR multiplier outlier detection. Default 2. min_n numeric. Minimum sample size outlier detection. Default 8. consensus_rule numeric. Number methods must agree consensus outlier flag. Default 1. n_neighbour_impute integer. Number neighboring time periods () use computing imputation median. Default 5 (uses 5 + 5 = 10 values). output_path character|NULL. provided, results saved path. output_name character. Base output name. Default "validation_routine_data_results". output_formats character. c("xlsx","rds"). Default c("xlsx","rds"). build_dictionary logical. Build data dictionary. Default TRUE. verbose logical. CLI messages. Default TRUE. language character. ISO-639-1 language code output labels ("en", "fr", "pt"). Default "en".

title: Orchestrates a suite of validation checks on routine HF data. It standardizes column resolution, selects indicators, runs missing/duplicate/future/logic/ outlier checks, compiles a summary, and optionally translates and saves. — validate_routine_hf_data
text: invisible named list elements: Summary, Missing values, Missing values detail, Duplicate records, Future dates, Consistency summary, Consistency details, Outlier summary, Outlier detailed, HF activeness summary, HF activeness episodes, Data dictionary

what: Calculate and visualize reporting rates — variables_plot
title: Calculate and visualize reporting rates — variables_plot
text: Calculates visualizes two key metrics: reporting/missing rate variables based x y dimensions proportion health facilities reporting given variable

title: Calculate and visualize reporting rates — variables_plot
code: variables_plot(   plot_data,   x_var,   vars_of_interest,   fill_var,   fill_label,   title_prefix,   subtitle = NULL,   include_plot_title = TRUE,   plot_caption = NULL,   common_elements,   target_language = "en",   source_language = "en",   lang_cache_path = tempdir(),   x_axis_breaks = 6 )

title: Calculate and visualize reporting rates — variables_plot
text: plot_data data frame containing summarized health facility data x_var time variable plotting (e.g., "year", "month") vars_of_interest Variables analyze reporting rates fill_var column use fill values ("reprate" "missrate") fill_label Label fill scale title_prefix Title prefix based whether showing reporting missing rates subtitle Optional subtitle text display title. Default NULL. include_plot_title Logical. TRUE, plot titles included. Default TRUE. plot_caption Optional caption text display bottom plot. Default NULL. common_elements Common ggplot elements apply plots target_language Language code labels (ISO 639-1), defaults "en" source_language Source language code, defaults NULL lang_cache_path Path translation cache, defaults tempdir() x_axis_breaks Numeric value specifying interval x-axis breaks. Default 6. example, 2 shows every second tick 6 every sixth.

title: Calculate and visualize reporting rates — variables_plot

title: Vectorized version of digest::digest — vdigest
text: function applies digest::digest function element vector, returning vector hash values.

title: Vectorized version of digest::digest — vdigest
code: vdigest(   object,   algo = c("md5", "sha1", "crc32", "sha256", "sha512", "xxhash32", "xxhash64",     "murmur32", "spookyhash", "blake3", "crc32c", "xxh3_64", "xxh3_128"),   serialize = TRUE,   file = FALSE,   length = Inf,   skip = "auto",   ascii = FALSE,   raw = FALSE,   seed = 0,   errormode = c("stop", "warn", "silent"),   serializeVersion = .getSerializeVersion() )

title: Vectorized version of digest::digest — vdigest
text: object R object calculate hash value algo hashing algorithm used. See digest::digest options serialize Whether serialize object first. Default TRUE file filename read . Default FALSE length number characters read file. Default NULL skip Number bytes skip reading file. Default 0 ascii TRUE, return hash ASCII format. Default FALSE raw TRUE, return raw vector. Default FALSE seed Random seed hash. Default 0 errormode Error mode file reading. Default "stop" serializeVersion Version used serialization. Default NULL

title: Vectorized version of digest::digest — vdigest

what: Save Data and Shapefiles to Various File Formats — write
title: Save Data and Shapefiles to Various File Formats — write
text: function provides unified interface saving data various file formats supported rio::export function. Additionally, supports fast binary format .qs2 via optional qs2 package. format automatically detected file extension simplify saving process.

title: Save Data and Shapefiles to Various File Formats — write
code: write(data, file_path, ...)

title: Save Data and Shapefiles to Various File Formats — write
text: data dataset saved file_path Character string specifying path output file. ... Additional arguments passed underlying write functions. arguments specific file format saved. Please refer documentation package used information.

title: Save Data and Shapefiles to Various File Formats — write
text: return value, called side effects.

title: Save Data and Shapefiles to Various File Formats — write
code: # Create temporary account tmpdir <- tempfile() dir.create(tmpdir)  # Save a CSV file write(mtcars, file_path = file.path(tmpdir, "file.csv"))  # Save an Excel file write(mtcars, file_path = file.path(tmpdir, "file.xlsx"))  # Save a Stata DTA file write(mtcars, file_path = file.path(tmpdir, "file.dta"))  # Save an RDS file # write(mtcars, file_path = file.path(tmpdir, "file.rds"))  # Save a qs2 file (requires 'qs2')  # Save an RData file write(list(mtcars = mtcars, iris = iris),        file_path = file.path(tmpdir, "file.RData"))  # For saving shapefiles # make example shape data my_shp <-  sf::st_sfc(   sf::st_point(c(43, 23))) |>     cbind(mtcars[1, ]) |>     sf::st_as_sf(crs = sf::st_crs(4326))  # save a shapefile # write(my_shp, file_path = file.path(tmpdir, "file.shp"))  # Remove the temporary directory and its contents unlink(tmpdir, recursive = TRUE)

what: Write EMOD Demographics JSON Files by ADM2 — write_emod_demog_by_adm2
title: Write EMOD Demographics JSON Files by ADM2 — write_emod_demog_by_adm2
text: Writes one demographics JSON per admin-2 unit, mirroring folder layout used write_emod_weather_by_adm2(). folder gets demographics.json matching IdReference, NodeID = 1, NodeCount = 1.

title: Write EMOD Demographics JSON Files by ADM2 — write_emod_demog_by_adm2
code: write_emod_demog_by_adm2(   df,   output_dir,   folder_case = "title",   demog_prefix = "",   demog_suffix = "",   id_reference = "Legacy",   individual_properties = NULL,   age_distribution = NULL )

title: Write EMOD Demographics JSON Files by ADM2 — write_emod_demog_by_adm2
text: df Data frame columns: adm2, lat, lon, pop, crude_death_rate, crude_birth_rate. output_dir Character. Root directory output folders. folder_case Case style subfolder names: "title" (default), "upper", "lower". Must match case used write_emod_weather_by_adm2(). demog_prefix Character. Prefix "demographics" filename. Default "" gives "demographics.json". Set NULL auto-prefix folder name. demog_suffix Character. Optional suffix .json (e.g. "_wSMC_risk"). id_reference Character. IdReference string. Default "Legacy". Must match climate files. individual_properties Optional list IP definitions. age_distribution Optional age distribution list.

title: Write EMOD Demographics JSON Files by ADM2 — write_emod_demog_by_adm2
text: Invisible NULL. Called side effect writing files.

title: Write EMOD Demographics JSON Files by ADM2 — write_emod_demog_by_adm2
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ df <- data.frame(   adm2 = c("Bubanza", "Cibitoke"),   lat = c(-3.08, -2.89),   lon = c(29.37, 29.18),   pop = c(100000L, 120000L),   crude_death_rate = c(8.5, 8.5),   crude_birth_rate = c(38.0, 38.0) ) write_emod_demog_by_adm2(df, tempdir()) } # }

what: Write EMOD binary weather files (.bin + .json) from a data.frame — write_emod_weather
title: Write EMOD binary weather files (.bin + .json) from a data.frame — write_emod_weather
text: R equivalent Python's emodpy_malaria.weather.csv_to_weather(). Writes one .bin / .bin.json pair per weather variable.

title: Write EMOD binary weather files (.bin + .json) from a data.frame — write_emod_weather
code: write_emod_weather(   df,   weather_dir,   climate_profile,   attributes = NULL,   id_reference = "Legacy",   update_resolution = "CLIMATE_UPDATE_DAY" )

title: Write an object to standardized filenames in one or more formats — write_snt_data
text: Writes object (data.frame, list data.frames, sf, etc.) directory using standardized naming. Supports atomic writes, optional pruning older versioned files, returns structured summary. filenames versioned (date tag) file already exists, set overwrite = TRUE replace . Otherwise error thrown.

title: Write an object to standardized filenames in one or more formats — write_snt_data
code: write_snt_data(   obj,   path,   data_name,   file_formats = "rds",   date_format = "%Y-%m-%d",   include_date = TRUE,   version_tag = NULL,   n_saved = 3,   quiet = TRUE,   overwrite = TRUE,   ... )

title: Write an object to standardized filenames in one or more formats — write_snt_data
text: obj Object write path Directory write data_name Base file name without extension file_formats Character vector formats. Default "rds". date_format Date format version tag. Default "%Y-%m-%d". include_date Logical; append _v. Default TRUE. version_tag Optional explicit tag (conflicts include_date) n_saved Optional positive integer; keep newest n_saved versioned files per format. Default 3. quiet Logical; suppress info logs. overwrite Logical; allow overwrite versioned. ... Additional writer-specific arguments

title: renv Setup Guide for sntutils
text: project uses renv manage R package dependencies create reproducible environments. ensures team members work package versions reduces “works machine” issues.

title: renv Setup Guide for sntutils
text: Clone repository: Open R project directory: Install package development mode:
code: git clone https://github.com/ahadi-analytics/sntutils.git cd sntutils # renv will activate automatically due to .Rprofile # If packages are missing, restore them: renv::restore() devtools::load_all()

title: renv Setup Guide for sntutils

title: renv Setup Guide for sntutils
text: auto.snapshot: false - Manual control snapshots snapshot.type: "explicit" - include explicitly used packages use.cache: true - Share packages across projects save space

title: renv Setup Guide for sntutils
text: ✅ renv.lock - Main lockfile ✅ renv/settings.json - Project settings ✅ renv/profiles/*/renv.lock - Profile lockfiles ✅ .Rprofile - renv activation ❌ renv/library/ - Local package installation (ignored) ❌ renv/staging/ - Temporary files (ignored)

title: renv Setup Guide for sntutils
text: Reproducibility: Everyone uses package versions Isolation: Project dependencies don’t conflict projects Efficiency: Shared package cache saves disk space Flexibility: Different profiles different use cases Collaboration: Easy onboarding new team members

title: renv Setup Guide for sntutils
code: # Full development workflow renv::activate(profile = "dev") devtools::load_all() devtools::test() devtools::check() devtools::document()

title: renv Setup Guide for sntutils
code: # Switch to production environment for clean build renv::activate(profile = "prod") devtools::build() devtools::install()

title: renv Setup Guide for sntutils
text: Q: Can use global R packages? : renv isolates project, can access global packages deactivating renv temporarily renv::deactivate(). Q: package isn’t available CRAN? : renv supports GitHub, Bioconductor, sources. Install remotes::install_github() snapshot. Q: update just one package? : renv::install("packagename") renv::snapshot(). Q: Can ignore certain packages? : Add ignored.packages renv/settings.json.

title: renv Setup Guide for sntutils
text: renv-specific issues: - renv documentation - renv GitHub issues sntutils-specific issues: - Create issue sntutils repository - Contact package maintainer

title: sntutils 2.0.0 (2026-06-11)
text: New pkgdown site workflow vignettes covering data cleaning, spatial preparation, climate downloads raster processing. README rewritten point site. New hex sticker logo man/figures/logo.png.

title: sntutils 2.0.0 (2026-06-11)
text: download_nasapower() pulls NASA POWER climate variables (temperature, precipitation, humidity, wind, radiation) plus dewpoint, elevation 11 added release. Samples multiple points per polygon (default 3) better representativeness. download_modis() modis_options() pull NASA MODIS imagery directly, replacing earlier download_appeears() wrapper. download_worldpop_urbanicity() downloads WorldPop urbanicity layers. download_worldpop_age_band() gains sex parameter, handles 80+ band correctly, respects quiet flag. get_worldpop_paths() wraps multi-group WorldPop download path building single call. download_worldpop() now uses t (total) variant default exposes GLOBAL mosaic option.

title: sntutils 2.0.0 (2026-06-11)
text: process_rasters_by_year() wraps process_raster_collection() pipelines admin boundary changes year year. process_raster_with_boundaries() gains optional population weighting optional time-pattern detection switch. Rasters cropped shapefile extent extraction, cutting runtime substantially large global rasters. Compact YYYYMM date pattern now supported process_rasters() helpers alongside YYYY YYYY-MM.

title: sntutils 2.0.0 (2026-06-11)
text: facetted_map_bins() facetted_map_gradient() draw faceted choropleths discrete continuous data. support caption, fixed colour-scale limits configurable text sizes. Insecticide-resistance trend plotting functions added, built-PNG compression. auto_bin() rewritten robust outlier handling adaptive labels; companion colour palette utilities added.

title: sntutils 2.0.0 (2026-06-11)
text: write_emod_demog() writes EMOD demographics JSON correct nested AgeDistribution arrays. read_emod_weather() write_emod_weather_by_adm2() added, folder_case parameter correct filename handling climate_profile empty.

title: sntutils 2.0.0 (2026-06-11)
text: New reporting-rate functions computed directly date columns. Reporting-rate plots outlier detection extended diagnostics standardized plot filenames OneDrive sync. Outlier correction cascade logic refined.

title: sntutils 2.0.0 (2026-06-11)
text: validate_process_spatial() runs multi-stage boundary-artifact cleaning pass (make_valid, s2 toggling, zero-width buffer, hole removal) aggregated geometries always valid render cleanly without double lines polygon overlap. .clean_coord_string() helper hardens coordinate parsing varied input formats. crosswalk_shapefiles_sf() exported now works adm1 without requiring adm2 column.

title: sntutils 2.0.0 (2026-06-11)
text: var_tree.yml extended ACT, IRS, ANC, EPI, SMC, IPTp4+, ITN, N5 cascade, care-seeking adjustment factors, severe anemia, household size, vaccine, health-facility variables, trained-provider care-seeking, rate/incidence/coverage indicators.

title: sntutils 2.0.0 (2026-06-11)
text: clean_filenames() refactored pure string operation explicit path-mapping helper.

title: sntutils 2.0.0 (2026-06-11)
text: crosswalk_shapefiles_sf() longer requires adm2 column matching adm1. auto_bin() rounding bug corrected. clean_filenames() longer drops column names piped .data.frame(). download_worldpop_age_band() recovers corrupt cached files. download_worldpop() friends handle download timeouts gracefully. fuzzy_match_facilities() adds error handling cloud-storage timeouts. NASA POWER download longer emits unnamed-column warnings n_sample = 1. Two pre-existing test flakes fixed.

title: sntutils 2.0.0 (2026-06-11)
text: download_appeears() documentation removed favour download_modis(). Migrate replacing calls download_modis() pulling product/band names modis_options().

title: sntutils 2.0.0 (2026-06-11)
text: Atomic write path hardened Windows: retrying fs::file_move, resilience path lock failures, tests skipped Windows reliable behaviour can’t guaranteed. CI workflows slimmed - live integrations gated, oldrel-1 dropped now ggrepel requires R >= 4.5.0; Windows dependency install step added. System-dependency install script macOS, Ubuntu Fedora. Workflow added block AI mentions commit messages.

title: sntutils 1.12.6 (2026-04-23)
text: tidy_malaria_raster_names() normalizes filenames emitted malariaAtlas::getRaster() (strips bounding-box coordinates download stamps, keeps ...Rate.YYYY.tiff). Idempotent; also removes getRaster/ artifact directory renames files. extract_time_components() gains year_extractor argument. Pass custom function (filename -> year) default “last plausible 4-digit year 1980-2099” heuristic picks wrong token. process_raster_collection() process_weighted_raster_collection() expose year_extractor pass-extract_time_components().

title: sntutils 1.12.6 (2026-04-23)
text: detect_time_pattern() now uses base::() instead base::() deciding whether batch filenames belongs given time pattern. Batches containing files mixed granularity longer get silently misclassified specific pattern present. Callers relying previous lenient behaviour pre-filter file lists. detect_time_pattern() tightens yearly regex plausible years (19[89]\d 20\d{2}) digit word boundaries, tokens like 202005 (YYYYMM stamp) longer match year.

title: sntutils 1.12.6 (2026-04-23)
text: process_weighted_raster_collection() process_raster_collection() now iterate via purrr::map() .progress = ..., replacing hand-rolled progress::progress_bar loop. Population rasters matched using word-boundary regex ((?<!\d)YYYY(?!\d)) year substrings longer false-match longer digit runs. purrr (>= 1.0.0) moved Suggests Imports.

title: sntutils 1.12.5 (2026-03-02)

EMOD pipeline: write_emod_demog(), read_emod_weather() write_emod_weather_by_adm2(). facetted_map_gradient() continuous-data faceted choropleths. facetted_map_bins() gains caption, fixed colour-scale limits text-sizing controls. Insecticide-resistance trend plotting (PNG compression). WorldPop: get_worldpop_paths() multi-group wrapper; download_worldpop_age_band() gains sex parameter, fixes 80+ band, respects quiet flag. correct_outliers() temporal outlier correction. Compact YYYYMM date pattern process_rasters helpers. Togo climate-download example scripts (EN + FR). DHS indicator definitions ACT, IRS, ANC, EPI, SMC IPTp4+ added var_tree.

title: sntutils 1.12.5 (2026-03-02)

NASA POWER longer emits unnamed-column warnings n_sample = 1. EMOD weather filename correct climate_profile empty. R CMD check warnings notes.
